jurisdictions.penalties: Operators: up to $2,000/day; Platforms: up to $500/day for non-compliance

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Arrendador en Divisa / Arrendador Inscrito)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (ONAT - Oficina Nacional de Administración Tributaria)
jurisdictions.fees: Fixed monthly tax per licensed room (regardless of occupancy) paid to ONAT
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No (owners can rent separate properties/rooms, but must be permanent Cuban residents)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Set by the municipal license (typically max 2 adults per room)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Graduated income tax scale (typically effectively around 10% on gross revenue)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed in residential and commercial properties with appropriate municipal license
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb handles payments via third-party agencies due to U.S. banking embargo restrictions
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Mandatory guest registry book (Libro de Registro). All foreign guest passport details must be reported to the local immigration office (Inmigración) within 24 hours of guest arrival.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Officially licensed private rentals (Casas Particulares) are marked by an inverted blue triangle sign on the door. U.S. travelers can legally stay under the OFAC 'Support for the Cuban People' travel category.
jurisdictions.penalties: Severe fines, confiscation of rental income, and potential license revocation or property seizure for renting to unregistered foreigners or operating without a license.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Strict guest registration is heavily enforced by local authorities.
jurisdictions.source: Cuba ONAT and Ministry of Tourism (MINTUR) regulations

jurisdictions.key_notes: Distinct from London's 90-night cap (Deregulation Act 2015) and from the abolished FHL tax regime (ended 6 April 2025)
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown
jurisdictions.additional_context: Unknown

jurisdictions.license_required: Mandatory registration with Welsh Revenue Authority (WRA) for all paid visitor accommodation, including STRs, spare rooms, holiday cottages, campsites and hotels
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — registration with WRA required by law whether let for a single night, occasionally, seasonally or year-round
jurisdictions.fees: Visitor levy (optional per council): £1.30 per adult per night for self-catering; £0.75 for hostels/campsites
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Visitor levy rates 75p (hostels/campsites) and £1.30 (other accommodation) per person per night
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unknown
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Applies to stays of 31 nights or less; stays over 31 consecutive nights exempt from levy. A separate Development of Tourism and Regulation of Visitor Accommodation (Wales) Bill would add a licensing regime. Act current to 11 June 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Unknown
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown
jurisdictions.additional_context: Unknown

jurisdictions.license_required: Scotland STR licensing already mandatory (since 1 January 2025; operating without a licence is a criminal offence)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes – standard rental income tax; possible move to business rates
jurisdictions.fees: Council-set; e.g. new home sharing £120; home letting £120 per occupant
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No, but secondary (non-principal-home) lets face planning hurdles
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No fixed night cap; 140-days-available/70-days-let test for business rates
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 5% visitor levy on the cost of paid overnight stays, applied to the first 5 consecutive nights
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Whole city designated STL Control Area since 5 Sep 2022; secondary lets need change-of-use planning (Class 9 to STL)
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR generally under 31 days per booking
jurisdictions.density_rules: Citywide control area; ~90% of planning applications for STL use refused
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes – minimum £2m public liability insurance (mandatory condition)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Licence number must be displayed on every advert/listing
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Accommodation providers, including STR hosts, responsible for collecting and paying the levy to Edinburgh City Council
jurisdictions.key_notes: 5% visitor levy applies to first 5 paid nights; home-sharing/letting licences valid 3 years, secondary lets initially 1 year
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a licence is a criminal offence; fine up to £2,500; possible one-year ban on reapplying
jurisdictions.additional_context: Tenement properties with shared entrances frequently refused planning; temporary festival exemptions up to 6 weeks/year

jurisdictions.license_required: Hosts offering accommodation for 21 nights or less must register with Fáilte Ireland
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes - income declared to Revenue; VAT if turnover exceeds €42,500
jurisdictions.fees: Nominal annual registration fee (structure to be confirmed)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No, but planning rules favour PPR for entire-home letting in larger towns
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No national cap (90-day PPR cap applies in Rent Pressure Zones)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Income tax at standard rates; accommodation VAT typically 13.5%
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Policy to generally preclude new STR planning permission in towns/cities over ~10,000-20,000 population
jurisdictions.min_stay: STL defined as letting up to 21 nights at a time
jurisdictions.density_rules: Planning compliance must be declared on registration; data shared with local authorities
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must display registration numbers, verify accuracy, run random checks, share data (EU Reg 2024/1028)
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Register tied to new EU-wide data collection/sharing rules; confirm operational specifics with Fáilte Ireland as source is a secondary booking-platform help page
jurisdictions.key_notes: Implements EU STR Regulation 2024/1028; underpinned by Short-Term Letting and Tourism Bill 2025
jurisdictions.penalties: Fixed Payment Notices (~€300) and District Court proceedings; platform sanctions up to 2% of turnover
jurisdictions.additional_context: Ireland uniquely links planning compliance directly to the register; National Planning Statement pending

jurisdictions.license_required: National registration pending; planning permission needed beyond 90 nights
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes - income taxed under standard residential rules
jurisdictions.fees: England register fee expected; not yet confirmed
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No, but 90-night cap applies to whole-property lets without planning consent
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 90 nights per calendar year for whole-property lets in Greater London
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Income tax at standard rates; 100% council tax premium possible on second homes
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Beyond 90 nights requires planning permission; boroughs likely early users of Article 4 directions
jurisdictions.min_stay: No minimum; cap applies to lets under 90 consecutive nights
jurisdictions.density_rules: Leasehold flats often prohibit STR; boroughs targeting non-compliant listings
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes - expected as part of registration/compliance
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Under proposed national register, platforms to verify/display registration numbers
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: 90-night limit hard to enforce without data; register expected to improve enforcement
jurisdictions.effective_date: 90-night cap long-standing; national register expected later 2026
jurisdictions.key_notes: 90-night rule applies across all platforms combined, not per-platform
jurisdictions.penalties: Proposed civil penalties up to £5,000 under national scheme
jurisdictions.additional_context: Greater London Council (General Powers) Act basis for 90-night rule; high concentration of leasehold flats

jurisdictions.license_required: National registration from 2026; change-of-use planning permission for non-PPR or >90 days
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes - income declared to Revenue; VAT if over threshold
jurisdictions.fees: Planning application fees; national register fee (nominal, TBC)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Effectively yes - 90-day exemption applies only to PPR; non-PPR needs planning permission
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 90 days per calendar year for entire PPR while temporarily absent
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Home-sharing exemption covers up to four bedrooms
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Income tax at standard rates; VAT typically 13.5%
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Whole city in Rent Pressure Zone; STR is material change of use requiring planning permission unless exempt
jurisdictions.min_stay: Planning rules define STR as 14 nights or less; register covers up to 21 nights
jurisdictions.density_rules: City centre applications routinely refused to preserve long-term housing stock
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms to verify registration numbers and remove non-compliant listings from 2026
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: PPR home-sharing unrestricted; whole-PPR letting needs Forms 15/16/17 to Dublin City Council

jurisdictions.key_notes: Two regimes overlap: local 14-night/90-day planning rules plus national 21-night register
jurisdictions.penalties: Up to €5,000 fine for advertising without valid planning permission; national register penalties also apply
jurisdictions.additional_context: Dublin, South Dublin, Fingal and Dún Laoghaire-Rathdown designated RPZs

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — registration number via national online service
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No (but secondary residences face change-of-use rules)
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 120 days/year for primary residence (municipalities may reduce to 90)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Micro-BIC allowance 50% classified (cap €77,700) / 30% unclassified (cap €15,000)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Municipalities may set quotas, primary-residence-only zones, change-of-use rules
jurisdictions.density_rules: Local quotas per neighborhood permitted under Le Meur Law
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: DAC7 income reporting to tax authorities; display 13-digit registration number
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Le Meur Law (Loi n°2024-1039); national registration mandatory by May 20, 2026; DPE energy rating min E (A–E) through 2033, A–D from 2034, G banned 2025
jurisdictions.key_notes: Co-ownership can ban STRs by 2/3 vote; primary residence proof via tax notice required
jurisdictions.penalties: Up to €100,000 for unauthorized change of use; €10,000 for non-registration; €20,000 false declaration; €15,000 exceeding day cap; €5,000 DPE breach
jurisdictions.additional_context: France is world's 2nd-largest Airbnb market with over 1 million listings
jurisdictions.source: service-public.gouv.fr; Le Meur Law (Law n°2024-1039 of 19 Nov 2024)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — must register as furnished tourist accommodation
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Per national rules plus tourist tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Only one rental permitted per fiscal household (incl. secondary residence) for up to six years
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration number display required
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: All STR properties must register as furnished tourist accommodations
jurisdictions.key_notes: Time-limited authorizations (six years) for secondary-residence conversions
jurisdictions.penalties: Per national framework
jurisdictions.additional_context: City enforces stricter household-level limits than national baseline

jurisdictions.license_required: No national license; permits/registration set at city/Land level
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (income tax; tourist tax at municipal level)
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No national cap; cities impose 56–90 days for secondary homes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Municipal tourist tax varies; income tax applies
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Zweckentfremdungsverbot (misuse of housing law) adopted by major cities
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: From May 2026, Airbnb/Booking.com must report host data to Bundesnetzagentur
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: No single national permit; rules vary substantially by Land and municipality

jurisdictions.penalties: Vary by municipality, up to €500,000 per violation in some cities
jurisdictions.additional_context: Framework is Zweckentfremdungsverbotsgesetz adopted by Berlin, Munich, Hamburg, Cologne

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — registration number (Registrierungsnummer) and permit required
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — primary residence only (second homes effectively banned without exemption)
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 90 days/year for primary residence (no cap noted in some sources); second homes prohibited
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Tourist tax plus income tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Zweckentfremdungsverbot (ZwVbG) — permit denied in housing-shortage areas
jurisdictions.min_stay: Under 90 days = short-term; 90+ days standard tenancy
jurisdictions.density_rules: Permits may be refused where housing is scarce
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration number must be displayed; Airbnb must disclose registered units & rental periods
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Digital listing verification system since 2023; all listings need a registration number
jurisdictions.key_notes: Second homes banned for STR unless specific exemption permit held
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €500,000
jurisdictions.additional_context: Permit application fee €100–€250; review takes weeks to months

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — housing protection number (Wohnraumschutznummer) for entire apartments
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Varies; stricter scrutiny for secondary homes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Tourist tax plus income tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Zweckentfremdungsverbot enforced
jurisdictions.min_stay: Stay over 3 months classed as residential use; long-term assumed from 6 months
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Display housing protection number
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Stricter enforcement; reporting platform for neighbors to flag misuse
jurisdictions.key_notes: STR permitted up to 8 weeks/year during owner's vacation or business travel; highest German ADR at €139
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €6,000 (some sources cite up to €100,000)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Munich hosts average ~€29,000 annually despite strict limits

jurisdictions.license_required: Registration required where municipality opts in (national registry)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (income tax, VAT, tourist tax)
jurisdictions.fees: Free registration in most cities via national registry
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 21% VAT (host-remitted); municipal tourist tax; income tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Municipalities may set local rules once registration introduced
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration number must appear in listings where required
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Wet toeristische verhuur van woonruimte (Tourist Rental of Living Space Act) under the Housing Act enables municipal registration/permits

jurisdictions.penalties: Set by municipality (fines, license revocation)
jurisdictions.additional_context: National Tourist Rental Registry; some cities use own portals (e.g. The Hague)
jurisdictions.source: Dutch Housing Act / Wet toeristische verhuur; trippz.com; misterbandb.zendesk.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — holiday rental permit + free registration number
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — must be main residence registered in BRP
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 30 nights/year citywide; 15 nights proposed for Centrum and De Pijp (from April 2026)

jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: No rentals of housing-corporation properties; no separate structures (boats/tents)
jurisdictions.density_rules: Permits limited in some areas; B&Bs capped per district

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — permit/exemption via own municipal portal
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes (residential properties must be used for living)
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Set by municipal holiday rental policy
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Tourist tax + income tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Residential properties must remain in residential use
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration number required
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Operates under national Wet toeristische verhuur; uses own portal rather than national registry
jurisdictions.key_notes: Strong housing demand drives rules to keep homes available for residents
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines for non-compliance
jurisdictions.additional_context: Uses dedicated municipal portal rather than national tourist rental registry

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — regional registration (rules differ by Region)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (income tax; municipal tourist tax)
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Defined per Region (Brussels: 1 night to 90 consecutive days)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Municipal tourist tax (varies); income tax; possible VAT
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Regional zoning/urban planning rules; tourist accommodation not classified as residential

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — register with Brussels Economy and Employment (BEE)

jurisdictions.density_rules: Town planning permit required since 30 Jan 2023 (some exceptions, e.g. <6 rooms in private home)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — online notification to Toerisme Vlaanderen (Logiesdecreet)

jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Must comply with local zoning laws

jurisdictions.license_required: No single national permit; building/trade permits may apply by category

jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb reports host data to tax authorities; national register planned
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Guest register required; each guest entered within 24h of arrival

jurisdictions.penalties: Vary by state; building-law breaches up to EUR 50,000

jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Local heritage/old-town restrictions may apply
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Subject to incoming EU data-sharing and national register rules
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Free city registration; must meet local health, safety and tax rules
jurisdictions.effective_date: Local registration in place; national reform pending
jurisdictions.key_notes: Tri-City area among fastest-growing STR markets
jurisdictions.penalties: Per incoming national framework (up to PLN 50,000 proposed)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Climate tax abolished in Gdańsk; day caps debated for Sopot/Gdańsk

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — municipal licence + national NRA; freeze on new licences
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Non-residents ~24% flat; residents per bracket
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: VUTs banned in residential buildings in historic centre; allowed only in entire tourist-dedicated buildings or with separate street entrance
jurisdictions.density_rules: Plan Reside protects residential use in city centre
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Listings must show registration; non-compliant removed
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Guest IDs to police Registro de Viajeros within 24h; tourist tax ~€3/person/night (up to 14 nights) per industry source; ~14,000 units operating illegally
jurisdictions.effective_date: Plan Reside approved 2025-08-27; new-licence freeze through Feb 2026
jurisdictions.key_notes: City fined Airbnb €64M (Dec 2025) for unlicensed listings; intensified inspections
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines starting at €3,000 for unregistered properties
jurisdictions.additional_context: Separate-entrance/zoning rule effectively bans rooms in shared residential buildings

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Alojamento Local (AL) registration via RNAL/ePortugal; RNAL number (XXXXX/AL)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register with Tax Authority; AL income is Category B (business)
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Stays typically under 30 days; former 120-day seasonal cap abolished
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 9 rooms / 27 guests per AL; 3 bedrooms max for room-in-home (per industry source)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Simplified regime (revenue <€200k): coefficient applied; non-residents flat 25% IRS on taxable portion
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Municipalities may designate containment areas and restrict/suspend new AL
jurisdictions.min_stay: No national minimum; up to 30 days defines AL
jurisdictions.density_rules: Set locally via municipal containment zones
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — mandatory liability insurance; expiry published in RNAL
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: RNAL number must appear on all listings; EU Reg. 2024/1028 enforcement from 20 May 2026
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Câmara municipal may oppose registration within 60 days (90 in containment zones); tacit approval if no opposition; CEAL extraordinary tax abolished retroactively to 31 Dec 2023
jurisdictions.key_notes: Licences now permanent and transferable nationally; 5-year validity & condo-approval requirement (except hostels) abolished; ASAE conducted 12,000+ inspections
jurisdictions.penalties: Registration cancellation; AL use can be barred up to 5 years on revocation
jurisdictions.additional_context: DL 76/2024 liberalised national framework while empowering municipalities locally

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — CIN (Codice Identificativo Nazionale) via Ministry of Tourism BDSR portal; regional CIR often prerequisite
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Agenzia delle Entrate; cedolare secca optional flat regime
jurisdictions.fees: No state registration fee for CIN (free); indirect/professional costs €100–300
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Short-term defined as rentals up to 30 days per contract (locazioni brevi)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Cedolare secca 21% (1st property); 26% for properties 2–4; 5+ properties treated as business (IRPEF + VAT); 2026 trend toward universal 26% for platform rentals
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Set locally (e.g., historic-centre limits in major cities)
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Safety requirements mandatory (gas/CO detectors, fire extinguishers); insurance Unknown nationally
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb, Booking, Expedia) must show valid CIN or delist; Airbnb applies 21% withholding on qualifying bookings
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: CIN must be displayed in all ads and outside the property; nationwide ban on remote self-check-in key boxes (late 2024), eased by May 2025 court ruling allowing remote check-in under conditions; in-person guest ID required
jurisdictions.key_notes: Introduced by Decree Law 145/2023 (Art. 13-ter, 'Decreto Anticipi'); CIN replaces patchwork of regional codes but CIR still required during transition
jurisdictions.penalties: No CIN: €800–€8,000; failure to display: €500–€5,000; safety violations: €600–€6,000 per violation; possible delisting
jurisdictions.additional_context: System managed via BDSR (Banca Dati Strutture Ricettive); SPID/CIE login required
jurisdictions.source: investropa.com; taxing.it; italiantaxes.com; yourbusinessinitaly.com; lawzana.com; studiopaci.net

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — CIN + Lombardy CIR; SCIA filing mandatory before listing
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (national rules)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Per national cedolare secca regime
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unknown (no historic-centre STR ban as of 2026)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: CIR/CIN must be displayed in ads; non-compliant listings risk delisting
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: SCIA (Segnalazione Certificata di Inizio Attività) required before listing; CIR (Lombardy ROSS1000) still enforced during transition to CIN; key boxes in public areas banned
jurisdictions.key_notes: Remote check-ins re-permitted from May 2025 under conditions
jurisdictions.penalties: Per national CIN/safety penalties (€500–€8,000); fines for key-box non-removal
jurisdictions.additional_context: Lombardy is among regions explicitly requiring CIR for all tourist rentals

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes – AMA; new AMAs frozen in central districts
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes – same as national
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Same as national
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Moratorium on new STR registrations in 1st Municipal District (Plaka, Monastiraki, Syntagma, Omonia, Kolonaki, Exarcheia, Ilisia, Neapoli, Koukaki)
jurisdictions.density_rules: New AMA suspension in saturated central zones through Dec 31 2026
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Same as national; flagged listings removed
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Existing pre-moratorium AMAs may keep operating; AMA non-transferable on sale/inheritance in restricted zones (registration deleted)
jurisdictions.key_notes: Pre-moratorium AMAs add ~10–20% property premium
jurisdictions.penalties: Standard fine plus moratorium-specific penalty
jurisdictions.additional_context: Central Athens ~14,350 STR units end-2025, +12% YoY; 2026 viewed as test year

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes – AMA; new AMAs frozen in city center
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes – same as national
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Same as national
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Freeze on new AMAs in First Municipal Community (historic/commercial center: Aristotelous Square, Egnatia, Tsimiski)
jurisdictions.density_rules: One-year suspension of new registrations from March 1 2026 (extendable)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Same as national
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: First geographic expansion of policy beyond Athens; targets ~4,800 of city's ~7,500 listings
jurisdictions.key_notes: Owners could secure AMAs until end of February 2026
jurisdictions.penalties: Same as national (€5,000–€20,000)
jurisdictions.additional_context: AMA decoupled from property in saturated areas

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes – permit for hospitality services in household + categorization; unique registration number per unit from June 2026
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes – notify Tax Administration (ePorezna); register in eTourism/eVisitor
jurisdictions.fees: Categorization and registration one-time €100–200; registration number free of charge
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Short-term defined as stays under 30 days
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Flat-rate income tax per bed (e.g. ~€200–400/yr Zagreb 2 beds); annual real estate tax €0.60–€8/m²
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Multi-unit buildings need 2/3 co-owner consent (NN 152/2024) plus adjacent-flat owner consent
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Recommended – standard home policies often exclude tourist activity (not a clear legal mandate)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration number required to advertise; unregistered listings illegal (EU Reg 2024/1028 from May 20 2026)
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Guest registration via eVisitor; display standardized blue plaque with type/star rating/registration number; fire extinguisher + smoke detector required
jurisdictions.effective_date: Registration number mandatory June 2026; new Hospitality Industry Act planned Jan 1 2027
jurisdictions.key_notes: Tourist tax up to €2.00/guest/night in season, €1.30 off-season (2026 board decisions)
jurisdictions.penalties: €1,000–€15,000 for unregistered STR (individuals)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Third-country (non-EU/EEA/Swiss) owners may only operate via a registered company
jurisdictions.source: regent.hr; kljuc.click; Croatia Week; EU Tourism Platform; Hostaway

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes – same national framework; city-specific restrictions emerging
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes – same as national
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Flat-rate per bed; net STR yields ~3.58% in Split
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: City-specific STR restrictions noted as reshaping the market
jurisdictions.density_rules: Unknown – among highest STR density in EU (~24,000 apartments with Zadar)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Same as national
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: High-tourism area with regular inspector checks
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of EU's most concentrated STR markets
jurisdictions.penalties: Same as national (€1,000–€15,000)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Specific local rules detailed in 2026 trackers; verify with city

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes – national framework with city-specific restrictions
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes – same as national
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Flat-rate per bed
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: City-specific restrictions reshaping the market, particularly old town
jurisdictions.density_rules: Local measures to address over-tourism and housing pressure
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Same as national
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Heightened enforcement in high-tourism historic core
jurisdictions.key_notes: Cited alongside Split for distinct city-level STR rules in 2026
jurisdictions.penalties: Same as national (€1,000–€15,000)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Specific restriction details vary; confirm with municipality

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes – MTA Holiday Furnished Premises / Short-Let Rented Accommodation licence (mandatory to operate or advertise)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes – via MTA Licensing Management System; income tax obligations apply
jurisdictions.fees: MTA licence fees via LMS (amount Unknown)
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Individual bookings cannot exceed 90 consecutive days; no annual night cap
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 2 persons per bedroom; total cap 10 persons per unit (unless independent street access)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: MTA may set locality-specific criteria to manage density; basement/underground bedrooms banned
jurisdictions.density_rules: MTA locality-specific licensing criteria (published in Gazette); local councils may add by-laws
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — valid insurance incl. common-parts cover; third-party liability up to €250,000
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Cannot advertise without valid licence; EU platform data sharing from 2026
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Display sign with licence number + 24/7 contact; waste management plan; mandatory A/C; bedroom min 9m² (sale contracts after 15 Jun 2026); no sofa beds for occupancy counts
jurisdictions.key_notes: Consolidates four prior accommodation regulations; MTA can reject low-quality finish properties
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating unlicensed = 3-year disqualification (follows operator and property) plus financial penalties
jurisdictions.additional_context: ~9,300+ STR listings; critics note weak enforcement (zero licences revoked 2022–2023)
jurisdictions.source: Mamo TCV (mamotcv.com); MaltaToday; MTA (mta.com.mt); Casa Rooms

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes – register in Register of Self-Service Accommodation (Deputy Ministry of Tourism); obtain registration/'Estia' number
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes – VAT registration if income exceeds €15,600; income tax declaration (TD1)
jurisdictions.fees: Registration fee payable; valid for 3 years
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 9% VAT on STR (over €15,600); income tax 0–35% progressive (companies 15% from Jan 1 2026); GHS 2.65%; SDC abolished Jan 1 2026
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Valid building/town planning permit confirming residential or tourist use required
jurisdictions.density_rules: No national cap (AG rejected proposed caps March 2025)
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — valid home insurance at least against fire and natural disasters
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Display registration number in all ads; platforms verify and report (EU Reg 2024/1028 from May 20 2026); Deputy Ministry is supervisory authority
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Inspection of premises for safety/comfort standards; renewal periodic (every 3 years)
jurisdictions.effective_date: Law 34(I)/2019 as amended; registration mandatory since July 2021 (advertising from 6 Feb 2023)
jurisdictions.key_notes: ~12,000–13,000 units offered; only ~8,375 registered (mid-2025)
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €5,000; up to 1 year imprisonment for serious breaches; licence revocation; platform delisting
jurisdictions.additional_context: Applies to free areas of the Republic; northern Cyprus (TRNC) has separate regime (~10% rental income tax)
jurisdictions.source: gov.cy Deputy Ministry of Tourism; Cyprus Mail; Clover.Tax; Chambers & Co; Airbnb Cyprus

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes – national registration plus subject to local permit quotas
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes – same as national
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Same as national (9% VAT threshold etc.)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Same national permit requirements
jurisdictions.density_rules: Temporary moratoriums on new rental permits during peak summer months (since 2022) to manage over-tourism
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Same as national
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Check current permit limits/quotas before applying; seasonal-driven market
jurisdictions.key_notes: Iconic beach resort area with seasonal permit caps
jurisdictions.penalties: Same as national (fines, revocation)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Larnaca and Limassol also apply local tourist taxes; verify with municipal/community council

jurisdictions.license_required: No license; CPR registration of host required
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (automatic reporting to Skat/DAC7)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Caps apply to primary residence (helårsbolig); room-only rentals exempt
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 70 nights/year via reporting platforms; 30 nights without; municipalities may allow up to 100
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Tax-free up to ~31,200 DKK; excess income taxed as capital income (~33.6% on 60%)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Exclusive STR use requires hotel-type permits, fire safety, building permits
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Not mandated; Airbnb host protection up to 5,000,000 DKK (not insurance)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms report host income to Skat; flag/block listings exceeding 70-day cap
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Housing associations/co-ops (ejerforening, andelsforening) may set stricter or zero limits
jurisdictions.key_notes: Holiday homes (sommerhuse) treated separately with no night cap; tax-free up to ~40,000 DKK
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines and back taxes; new 2026 agreement raises fines and municipal enforcement powers
jurisdictions.additional_context: Denmark was first country to get Airbnb to report income directly to tax authority
jurisdictions.source: thelocal.dk; Retsinformation (Lov om udlejning af fast ejendom til ferie- og fritidsformål); investropa.com

jurisdictions.license_required: No license; CPR registration required
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (automatic Skat reporting)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: 70-day cap applies to primary residence; room-only (host present) unlimited
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 70 nights/year for entire home (not raised to 100)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Per national rules (tax-free allowance then capital income tax)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Many buildings/HOAs forbid STR; aparthotels classified as hotels exempt
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb auto-blocks bookings beyond 70-day cap; new rules require data-sharing on 'shadow hotels'
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Building bylaws (ejerforening) may impose stricter limits than national cap
jurisdictions.key_notes: Enforcement focused on commercial-scale 'shadow hotel' operators
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines, back taxes; 2026 agreement gives municipality power to order listing removal/blocking
jurisdictions.additional_context: Mayor described commercial conversions as 'insult to ordinary Copenhageners'

jurisdictions.license_required: No license; CPR registration required
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (Skat reporting)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Per national rules
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Building/HOA restrictions may apply
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Set to gain power to require Airbnb data and order listing removal under 2026 agreement
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Targeted alongside Copenhagen for stronger enforcement of illegal rentals
jurisdictions.key_notes: Named with Copenhagen as a city getting expanded crackdown powers
jurisdictions.penalties: Raised fines per 2026 political agreement
jurisdictions.additional_context: Issue flagged via prevalence of key deposit boxes signaling Airbnb use

jurisdictions.license_required: No STR-specific license for individuals
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes; declare income to Skatteverket (data-sharing with Airbnb)
jurisdictions.fees: None for individuals; small fee if registering a business (Bolagsverket)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Not required; renting whole property/sublet needs landlord or co-op board permission
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Tax-free up to 40,000 SEK/year; remainder taxable (owners get extra 20% deduction)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Hotel/hostel-like operation requires hotel business permissions
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb shares income data with Skatteverket under DAC7
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Rent cap: furnished sublet max ~15% above own rent; bostadsrätt/co-op board approval needed for whole-unit lets
jurisdictions.effective_date: VAT threshold raised to SEK 120,000 on 1 Jan 2025
jurisdictions.key_notes: Unauthorized subletting risks eviction or forced sale of bostadsrätt
jurisdictions.penalties: Eviction, forced sale; excess rent may be reclaimed by tenant
jurisdictions.additional_context: ~95,000 active listings; strong vacation-cottage (stuga) culture

jurisdictions.license_required: No STR-specific license
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (Skatteverket)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Not required, but whole-unit lets need landlord/co-op board approval
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No statutory cap; co-op boards often impose limits (e.g. 90 days)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Per national rules
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Many central co-op associations ban or restrict STR
jurisdictions.density_rules: High de facto restrictions in Södermalm, Norrmalm, Östermalm, Gamla Stan
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Per national DAC7 reporting
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Condo associations independently restrict STR; landmark case where a host was banned from letting
jurisdictions.key_notes: ~6,800 listings; ~62% occupancy; restrictions driven by housing scarcity and noise complaints
jurisdictions.penalties: Eviction; forced sale risk for bostadsrätt holders
jurisdictions.additional_context: Older buildings with sound issues and organized residents push back on 'hotel' use

jurisdictions.license_required: No STR-specific license
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (Skatteverket)

jurisdictions.license_required: No national STR license; business/Brønnøysund registration if commercial
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (Skatteetaten); VAT if turnover exceeds NOK 50,000 / business activity

jurisdictions.penalties: Association warnings, fines, legal action; business income taxed up to 50.6%
jurisdictions.additional_context: Government developing better municipal tracking tools for illegal STR
jurisdictions.source: investropa.com (Norway); thelocal.no; lovdata.no; skatteetaten.no; Airbnb Norway tax guide

jurisdictions.license_required: No national license; business registration if commercial

jurisdictions.license_required: Home accommodation (heimagisting) registration with District Commissioner; business license if exceeding thresholds

jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Income cap 2,000,000 ISK/year; exceeding 90 days or income cap reclassifies as business
jurisdictions.key_notes: Governed by Act No. 85/2007 on Restaurants, Accommodation and Entertainment; the '90/2M rule'
jurisdictions.penalties: Administrative fines up to 1,000,000 ISK per violation; deregistration; denial of re-registration
jurisdictions.additional_context: 2026 reform makes business licenses 5-year/renewable only if commercially zoned; aims to protect housing supply

jurisdictions.license_required: Heimagisting registration with District Commissioner of Greater Reykjavík
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (Ríkisskattstjóri)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes; primary residence plus optionally one rural property
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 90 days/year combined; individual stays max 30 days
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 5 rooms or 10 guests per property
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Per national rules (capital income; 11% VAT above threshold)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Strictest enforcement; new business STR in residential housing prohibited; commercial licenses only for commercial-zoned property
jurisdictions.density_rules: Business entities barred from operating STR in residential housing in Capital Region
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration number must appear in marketing/listings
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: District Commissioner of Greater Reykjavík has explicit authority to issue fines
jurisdictions.key_notes: Targets downtown buildings converted entirely into tourist accommodation
jurisdictions.penalties: Administrative fines up to 1,000,000 ISK per violation; deregistration; re-registration denial
jurisdictions.additional_context: Reforms respond to housing shortage worsened by volcanic eruptions and tourism boom

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes - NTAK registration number
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.fees: NTAK registration free; flat-rate annual tax applies
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None nationally; municipalities may set day caps
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Flat-rate annual tax increased sharply from 2025; tourist tax ~4% in many areas
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Municipalities empowered to set local restrictions
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Valid NTAK registration number must be displayed on listings
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Online NTAK registration via Hungarian Citizen Portal; notify changes within portal
jurisdictions.key_notes: Light-touch regime tightened progressively; STR income taxes raised sharply
jurisdictions.penalties: Administrative fines and possible temporary closure
jurisdictions.additional_context: EU Regulation 2024/1028 platform data-sharing applies from May 2026

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes - NTAK registration; moratorium on new registrations
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.fees: Flat-rate annual tax raised from HUF 38,400 to HUF 150,000
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Districts may cap rental days per year
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Tourist tax ~4% of accommodation price
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: District-level restrictions; new registrations frozen citywide
jurisdictions.density_rules: Districts target buildings where STRs exceed 50% of units
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: NTAK number must be displayed; unregistered listings flagged
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: No new STR registrations Jan 1 2025 - Dec 31 2026; pre-2025 registrations may continue if compliant
jurisdictions.key_notes: Two-year moratorium on new STR registrations through end of 2026
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines of several million forints; joint inspections by govt, police, tax
jurisdictions.additional_context: Budapest has more Airbnb units than hotel rooms; Airbnb ~40% of guest nights

jurisdictions.license_required: N/A - rentals prohibited
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: N/A
jurisdictions.tax_rate: N/A
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Complete ban on private and other tourist accommodation
jurisdictions.density_rules: Ban driven by ~8% of housing stock used as STR
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Listings prohibited; joint enforcement inspections
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Decree 26/2024 sets usable accommodation days to 0; upheld by Supreme Court (Kúria) decision Köf.5.023/2025/4
jurisdictions.key_notes: First district-wide total STR ban in Hungary, following local referendum
jurisdictions.penalties: Individuals up to HUF 200,000; companies up to HUF 2,000,000; closure up to 45 days
jurisdictions.additional_context: Owners limited to self-use, long-term rental, or sale

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes - tourism classification certificate from Ministry of Tourism
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.fees: Classification certificate application has no fee
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 10% income tax on net rental income (20% flat-rate expense deduction)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Heritage/cultural zones (e.g. Sibiu, Brasov, Bucharest) may require special permits or prohibit STR
jurisdictions.density_rules: Mayors empowered to limit number of STRs in their areas
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: EU unique registration number via SITUR to be required; platforms verify and delist non-compliant
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Classification certificate must be displayed in hall and kitchen; neighbor/owners' association consent required
jurisdictions.key_notes: Certificate from Ministry of Tourism; phone number of tourism control direction must be displayed
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines of 10,000-40,000 lei (~€2,009-€8,037) for unauthorised listings
jurisdictions.additional_context: EU Regulation 2024/1028 / SITUR registration applies from May 20, 2026; ANAF crackdown on ~23,000 undeclared hosts

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes - tourism classification certificate (renewed periodically)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 10% income tax on net rental income
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Historic/heritage structures require additional documentation
jurisdictions.density_rules: No 'one host, one home' rule; no maximum number of listings
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: EU registration number display and platform verification from May 20, 2026
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Register property via Ministry of Economy portal; condominium association rules may restrict STR
jurisdictions.key_notes: Classification certificate is the key requirement authorizing paid guests
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines of 10,000-40,000 lei (~€2,000-€8,000) for operating without classification
jurisdictions.additional_context: Majority of inventory is entire-home apartments owned by non-resident hosts

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes - municipal categorization/registration under Tourism Act (Class B)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes - ESTI and NRA registration
jurisdictions.fees: ~20 BGN per bed (municipal registration); categorization BGN 50-250
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No national maximum stay; STR understood as under 30 days
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Simplified registration for guest rooms/apartments up to 10 rooms
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 10% income tax; patent tax option; VAT 9% (categorized) or 20% (uncategorized); Art. 97a VAT on platform commission
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Cannot categorize if part of an already-categorized hotel complex
jurisdictions.min_stay: No fixed national maximum; cities may apply local rules
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Not a national requirement; liability insurance encouraged
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms verify registration number and delist invalid listings (from May 20, 2026)
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: ESTI guest reporting mandatory since 2019; report each stay; non-EU guests registered within 5 days
jurisdictions.key_notes: Regulated as tourist accommodation, not casual rental; monthly municipal usage reporting
jurisdictions.penalties: Substantial fines; e.g. BGN 3,000 for non-reporting even if unused
jurisdictions.additional_context: EU Regulation 2024/1028 fully applicable from May 20, 2026; ESTI to align with EU framework

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes - municipal Class B registration
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes - ESTI and NRA
jurisdictions.fees: ~20 BGN per bed registration; patent tax ~25 BGN per bed/year (some sources cite BGN 200/room)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Patent tax set by municipal council; tourist tax per overnight stay
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unknown
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Same as national; platform verification from May 2026
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Patent tax declaration filed by 31 January; paid in four instalments
jurisdictions.key_notes: Capital is largest STR market (~4,400 active listings)
jurisdictions.penalties: Same as national framework
jurisdictions.additional_context: Saturated neighborhoods include Tsentar, Oborishte, Lozenets

jurisdictions.license_required: No specific STR license for occasional hosts; professional operators register as business
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes - income reporting; business registration if professional
jurisdictions.fees: No license fee; business registration ~€20-200 state fees
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Income tax (22%); VAT threshold €40,000 annual turnover
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Apartment association (korteriühistu) rules may restrict
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: EU Regulation 2024/1028 data-sharing applies from May 2026
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Guest registration to Police and Border Guard Board within 24 hours for stays under 30 days; safety equipment (smoke detectors, fire extinguisher) required
jurisdictions.key_notes: Tourism Act governs accommodation services; private occasional hosting may fall outside Act
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines and potential legal action depending on violation
jurisdictions.additional_context: Estonia digital tax system; Airbnb shares data with tax authority

jurisdictions.license_required: No specific per-unit STR license; must meet accommodation establishment requirements
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.fees: No city licensing fee; safety equipment compliance typically under €200
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Income tax per national rules; tourist/city tax proposed historically
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Old Town (Vanalinn) under active discussion for future limitations
jurisdictions.min_stay: No mandatory minimum (hosts typically set ~2 nights)
jurisdictions.density_rules: Officials aim to reduce STR in Old Town to preserve residents
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Municipal permits/notifications may be required; EU data-sharing from May 2026
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Guest ID data submitted to Police and Border Guard Board within 24 hours; no city-level night reporting
jurisdictions.key_notes: Old Town is the zone with genuine regulatory uncertainty
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines and potential legal action; depends on violation
jurisdictions.additional_context: Tallinn ~40% of all Estonian Airbnb listings; some Old Town buildings host up to 16 guests

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes - business certificate or registered individual activity for accommodation services
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.fees: Business certificate fee set by municipality per number of days
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Business certificate (municipal, per-day, capped at €45,000/yr, excess at PIT); or individual activity at PIT 15%/20%; VAT with no registration threshold for accommodation
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unknown
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: EU Regulation 2024/1028 data-sharing applies from May 2026; Airbnb shares host data with VMI
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Hygiene passport may be required in certain cases; compulsory health insurance charge with accommodation business certificate
jurisdictions.key_notes: No STR-specific rules; rental income not treated as active business income unless accommodation services provided
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown
jurisdictions.additional_context: VMI intensifying scrutiny of platform hosts; sector historically lightly regulated

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes - business certificate or registered individual activity (national framework)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.fees: Business certificate fee set by municipality
jurisdictions.tax_rate: City tax €2 per person per night for stays under one month; income tax per national rules
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unknown
jurisdictions.min_stay: Unknown (city tax applies to stays under one month)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb automatically collects and remits Vilnius city tax since Feb 2020
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: City tax collected from tourists during booking; refunds via municipality if exempt
jurisdictions.key_notes: First Baltic/CEE city with Airbnb automatic tourist tax collection agreement
jurisdictions.penalties: Recovery of arrears (5-year limitation); municipal enforcement
jurisdictions.additional_context: Bulk of Lithuania's Airbnb accommodations registered in Vilnius

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes - registration as furnished tourist accommodation
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes - must reside at least 8 months/year for legal Airbnb listing
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 90 days/year primary residence (Le Meur cap adopted, effective 1 Jan 2026)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Taxe de séjour applies
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Secondary residences require change-of-use permit; most requests denied
jurisdictions.density_rules: Rental observatory monitors prices (cap ~20% above median); co-owners must declare to municipality
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Display registration number
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Strict pro-housing stance under Payan administration
jurisdictions.key_notes: Among most restrictive French cities for secondary residences
jurisdictions.penalties: Per national Le Meur framework
jurisdictions.additional_context: High-demand 'tense zone'

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes - online notification to Toerisme Vlaanderen; registration number issued
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes - income tax; municipal tourist tax
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No - applies to all tourist accommodation, occasional or professional
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Income tax; municipal tourist tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Must meet safety/quality standards under Logiesdecreet
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Only properties with valid registration number may advertise; platforms must share listing data
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Flemish Decree on tourist accommodation (Logiesdecreet) in force since 2017 (decree of 5 Feb 2016); rules tightened late 2025
jurisdictions.key_notes: Occasional hosts face fewer inspections; ~40% of accommodations found non-compliant
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €25,000 and suspension of registration for serious violations
jurisdictions.additional_context: Covers Antwerp, Ghent and other Flemish cities

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — registration in Registro de Turismo de Andalucía (RTA)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — income tax; guest reporting
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Per national rules
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Yes — new tourist accommodation banned in 43 saturated neighbourhoods
jurisdictions.min_stay: Tourist use short-stay; seasonal 32+ days separate
jurisdictions.density_rules: Municipal moratorium on new licenses in saturated zones (also Fuengirola, Manilva)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Display RTA number; platform delisting of non-compliant
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Andalusian licenses have 'carácter real' — tied to the unit, transfer with property; acquired rights protected
jurisdictions.key_notes: Junta de Andalucía referred state registry dispute to Supreme Court
jurisdictions.penalties: Per Andalusian tourism/housing law
jurisdictions.additional_context: Costa del Sol remains profitable but higher entry thresholds and tight controls

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — AL/RNAL registration via BUE
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Finanças registration; Porto tourist tax collection
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Per AL national rules (max 9 rooms/27 guests)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Tourist tax €3/person/night (from Dec 2024); IRS +15pts in containment zones
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Yes — 6 central parishes suspended/blocked for new AL
jurisdictions.density_rules: Containment in central parishes; Vila Nova de Gaia suspended new registrations in several districts
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: RNAL number in ads; platform delisting of non-compliant
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: New AL still possible in non-saturated areas (Boavista, Campanhã, Lordelo)
jurisdictions.key_notes: More accessible than Lisbon in 2026; UNESCO historic centre protected from tourist monoculture
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €40,000; registration cancellation
jurisdictions.additional_context: Investors shifting from Lisbon to Porto and secondary cities

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — MTA licence (locality-specific criteria may apply)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — same as national
jurisdictions.fees: Same as national (may vary by locality criteria)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: 2 per bedroom; max 10 per unit (as national)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Potential locality-specific licensing criteria under 2026 Regulations
jurisdictions.density_rules: MTA may set differing rules for high-demand localities; local councils may impose by-laws
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Same as national
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Rules may eventually differ from quieter residential zones; same 24/7 contact requirement
jurisdictions.key_notes: Identified as likely first localities for divergent criteria; specific local rules not yet published
jurisdictions.penalties: Same as national (3-year disqualification for unlicensed operation)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Locality-specific criteria must be published in Government Gazette and on MTA website

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Deputy Ministry registration; subject to local quotas/moratoriums
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — same as national
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Same as national

jurisdictions.density_rules: Ayia Napa and Protaras introduced temporary moratoriums on new permits during peak summer months (since 2022)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Same as national
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Check current limits before applying; iconic beach-resort district with seasonal demand

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Deputy Ministry registration; secondary municipal inspection in some cases
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — same as national
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Same as national plus possible Limassol-specific tourist tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Strict standards enforced in urban core and coastal strip
jurisdictions.density_rules: Stricter standards coordinated between municipality, district and Deputy Ministry
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Same as national

jurisdictions.key_notes: Major business/cultural hub; tighter enforcement in tourist areas

jurisdictions.additional_context: Many landlords use property management firms for ongoing compliance

jurisdictions.license_required: Home accommodation registration with Icelandic Housing Agency; District Commissioner of Greater Reykjavík handles licensing
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (national rules)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Domicile-based home hosting (plus one additional property)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Financial/capital income for home hosting; business + VAT + lodging tax above limits
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: 2024 reform restricts business entities from STR in residential housing in Capital Region
jurisdictions.density_rules: 2M ISK income cap; max 2 properties per individual

jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Commercial STR licenses limited to commercially-zoned units; downtown conversions targeted

jurisdictions.penalties: Administrative fines up to 1,000,000 ISK per violation; delisting

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — notification (届出) registration with local government; registration number must be displayed
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — rental income taxable and must be declared
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — but a licensed manager required if owner not present
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Min. 3.3 m² floor space per guest
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Income tax applies; 20.42% withholding for non-residents
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Treated like residential property; local zoning applies; ~99% of condo bylaws prohibit minpaku
jurisdictions.min_stay: None under standard minpaku (Tokku/Special Zone requires 2-night min)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Listings must display registration number; platforms remove unregistered listings
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Bi-monthly guest reporting to prefectural governor (15th of even months); fire-safety conformity certificate required; exceeding 180 days requires a hotel/ryokan (Simple Lodging) license
jurisdictions.key_notes: Private Lodging Business Act (住宅宿泊事業法 / Minpaku Law); ~33,618 registered properties as of July 2025
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to JPY 1,000,000 and/or up to 6 months imprisonment; first criminal prosecution Jan 2026
jurisdictions.additional_context: Three legal paths: standard minpaku (180-day), Hotel Business Act Simple Lodging (year-round), and Special Zone/Tokku minpaku (year-round, 2-night min)
jurisdictions.source: MLIT Minpaku Portal; japan-realestate-analytics.com; lodgecompliance.com; keycafe.com; getproofsnap.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — minpaku license under national law plus stricter local ordinance
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — income taxable
jurisdictions.fees: Government application fees are minor (~¥10,000–¥30,000), but fire safety compliance upgrades and professional Administrative Scrivener (Gyoseishoshi) filing fees typically range from ¥650,000 to ¥1,000,000+ depending on the building's age.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No, but absent-owner properties require nearby caretaker
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: ~60 days/year in residential zones (Jan 15/16 – Mar 15/16 only); 180 days if owner resident or certified machiya
jurisdictions.tax_rate: National rental income tax; 10% consumption tax above ¥10m revenue; Kyoto City Accommodation Tax per guest/night: <¥6,000 is ¥200, ¥6,000–¥19,999 is ¥400, ¥20,000–¥49,999 is ¥1,000, ¥50,000–¥99,999 is ¥4,000, and ≥¥100,000 is ¥10,000.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Residential-zone operation banned during peak tourist seasons; commercial zones more permissive
jurisdictions.min_stay: Previously 2+ nights; later eased in many areas
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration number display required
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Manager must be able to reach property within 10 minutes to handle complaints; certified machiya townhouses may operate year-round
jurisdictions.key_notes: Among Japan's strictest local minpaku regimes due to overtourism backlash
jurisdictions.penalties: National penalties apply (up to JPY 1,000,000 / 6 months prison)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Residential-area minpaku effectively non-viable outside the winter window; machiya certification grants year-round flexibility

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — minpaku notification plus ward-specific ordinances
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — income taxable
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No, but designated manager required if owner absent
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 180 days national cap; many wards restrict to weekends/holidays only (e.g., Shinjuku, Nerima, Bunkyo); Toshima 120 days from April 2026; Shibuya school holidays only
jurisdictions.tax_rate: National income tax; Tokyo accommodation tax applies
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Residential enclaves heavily time-restricted or off-limits; Chuo-ku bans weekday rentals
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration number display required
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Rules vary by ward and change frequently; neighbor notification often required; Shinjuku suspended 12 operators in 2025-26 enforcement
jurisdictions.key_notes: Fragmented ward-by-ward regime; commercial/mixed-use districts more permissive than residential
jurisdictions.penalties: National penalties apply (up to JPY 1,000,000 / 6 months prison)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Ota Ward offered Tokku (Special Zone) year-round minpaku, but new applications frozen since late 2025

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — registration as Foreign Tourist Urban Homestay Business (외국인관광도시민박업) under Tourism Promotion Act, or rural/hanok equivalents
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — business registration certificate required; mandatory on Airbnb since Oct 2024 (existing listings by Oct 16, 2025)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — host generally must reside in the property
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No fixed annual day cap; legal framework restricts guest eligibility instead
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Income tax plus VAT depending on income and registration status
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Only residential property types allowed (single/multi-family homes, apartments, townhouses); officetels and goshiwons banned in all areas
jurisdictions.min_stay: No national minimum for licensed homestays; ~30 days for unlicensed residential rentals
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must collect accommodation license info; ~30,000 unlicensed listings (incl. officetels) being delisted from Oct 2025
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Standard urban homestay business legally serves only FOREIGN tourists — hosting Korean nationals is otherwise illegal except via licensed pilot platforms (e.g., Wehome regulatory sandbox); fire-safety inspection required

jurisdictions.key_notes: No single nationwide STR statute; governed by Tourism Promotion Act, Building Act, Residence Act; gov't considering legalizing domestic home-sharing
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to KRW 10,000,000 for owners; tenant eviction; possible visa complications for foreigners
jurisdictions.additional_context: 2025 reforms eased rules on buildings over 30 years old if structurally safe; ~5,000 registered home-sharing businesses in 2024; majority of Airbnb listings historically unlicensed
jurisdictions.source: Korea Herald; Airbnb Help Center (art. 1387); bambooroutes.com; wehome.me; staysmartguide.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Foreign Tourist Urban Homestay Business or Hanokstay registration with district office
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — business registration required
jurisdictions.fees: Signboard support up to KRW 100,000 offered; fee details Unknown
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Income tax plus VAT; accommodation tax may apply
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Residential housing only; officetels excluded
jurisdictions.min_stay: No national minimum for licensed homestays
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: License submission required; ~20% of ~23,591 listings licensed as of July 2025 — weak enforcement historically
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Hanokstay (traditional Korean house) businesses open to both Koreans and foreigners; Wehome regulatory sandbox allows legal domestic home-sharing in Seoul/Busan

jurisdictions.penalties: National penalties apply (fines up to KRW 10,000,000)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Note: separate Aug 2025 rule designated all 25 Seoul districts as Foreign Land Transaction Permit Zones, affecting foreign property purchases (not STR operation directly)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — operators need a Short-term Rental License (短租许可证); local authority approval generally required

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — requires owner consent and community rule compliance
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Unknown

jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — registration required for STRs
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Unknown
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unknown

jurisdictions.additional_context: Some small guesthouses/homestays lack qualifications to host foreigners

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — hotel or 'minsu' (民宿/B&B) license required; unlicensed home sharing prohibited
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — corporate/commercial registration required before operation
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — minsu must be part of a family's home/self-used residence run as a family sideline
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Generally max 5 guest rooms (up to 15 for special tourism features); total floor area under 150 sqm
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Highly restrictive; local county/city governments designate limited permitted areas for minsu
jurisdictions.min_stay: Stays of 30 days or longer fall under Rental Housing Market Development and Regulation Act

jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Hosts bear compliance responsibility; advertising unlicensed lodging is itself an offense
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Governed by Act for the Development of Tourism and Regulations for the Management of Home Stay Facilities
jurisdictions.effective_date: Regulations for Management of Home Stay Facilities issued December 2001; Article 55 amended January 2017
jurisdictions.key_notes: Minsu legal framework central; unlicensed apartment/whole-home STRs broadly prohibited
jurisdictions.penalties: Unlicensed operation up to NT$100,000–NT$500,000; advertising fines NT$30,000–NT$300,000; B&B failure to register NT$60,000–NT$300,000; utilities may be cut off
jurisdictions.additional_context: Central oversight by Tourism Administration; day-to-day enforcement by local governments

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — hotel or minsu license; minsu generally not permitted in dense urban core
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Urban location severely limits minsu eligibility; whole-apartment STRs largely unlicensable
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: City ordered Airbnb to remove ~95% of ~4,200 short-term listings (Dec 2015)
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Among highest enforcement nationwide for illegal accommodations
jurisdictions.key_notes: Active short-term rentals roughly 12% of hotel-room supply; most listings deemed illegal
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines under Act for the Development of Tourism; Taipei among top cities for illegal-accommodation fines
jurisdictions.additional_context: Demand driven by shortage of affordable downtown hotel rooms

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — hotel or guesthouse license under Hotel and Guesthouse Accommodation Ordinance (Cap. 349) for stays under 28 days
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Property Tax applies to rental income (no separate STR tax registry noted)

jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: No licensed premises in industrial buildings or basements; lease conditions and Deed of Mutual Covenant must permit use
jurisdictions.min_stay: Stays of 28+ continuous days exempt (Hotel and Guesthouse Accommodation (Exclusion) Order)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms not legally obligated to verify listings; compliance rests with hosts
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Premises must meet building and fire safety standards; renewal needs Certificate of Compliance
jurisdictions.effective_date: Hotel and Guesthouse Accommodation Ordinance (Cap. 349) in force
jurisdictions.key_notes: Effectively bans typical Airbnb-style short-term letting of unlicensed residential flats
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating unlicensed guesthouse a criminal offense; max HK$200,000 fine and 2 years imprisonment
jurisdictions.additional_context: HAD operates 'Hong Kong Licensed Hotels and Guesthouses' app for verification and reporting

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — hotel license for stays under 30 days
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (income tax; VAT if applicable)
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None (but stays under 30 days require hotel license)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Non-hotel exemption: up to ~8 rooms / 30 guests
jurisdictions.tax_rate: House & Land Tax ~12.5%; progressive personal income tax; 5% withholding (15% if remitted abroad)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Condo units largely barred from commercial/short-term use under Condominium Act

jurisdictions.additional_context: Enforcement uneven but intensifying in Bangkok, Phuket, Pattaya; draft national STR registration framework proposed 2025
jurisdictions.source: rumavi.com; thailand-business-news.com; thethailandadvisor.com (citing Hotel Act B.E. 2547)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — hotel license for stays under 30 days
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (income tax; VAT if applicable)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Per national rules
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Condos restricted to residential use; daily rentals not permitted without hotel license
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Under government scrutiny

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to THB 20,000; imprisonment up to 1 year

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — hotel license for stays under 30 days
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (income tax; VAT if applicable)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Per national rules
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Condos restricted to residential use; many buildings ban under-30-day rentals via juristic-person rules

jurisdictions.fees: IRAS tenancy stamp duty (tenant usually pays)

jurisdictions.license_required: No — STR/Airbnb hosting not permitted
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — declare rental income to IRAS
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: HDB sets occupancy caps by flat type
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Income tax on rental income (progressive)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Public housing — transient/tourist accommodation prohibited

jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Flats may only be rented to foreigners holding valid passes; not to tourists

jurisdictions.license_required: Varies by local council; national licensing framework still in draft

jurisdictions.fees: Unknown (local-council business premise license fees vary)

jurisdictions.license_required: Special planning permission needed to exceed proposed night cap

jurisdictions.tax_rate: Per national rules
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Subject to local council zoning and strata by-laws

jurisdictions.penalties: Per applicable council by-laws / SMA fines

jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — NPWPD/NPWP registration required

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Pondok Wisata (homestay) or TDUP/Villa licence (KBLI 55193) plus NIB

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — NIB via OSS plus appropriate accommodation licence

jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Subject to national zoning; building management rules may restrict STRs

jurisdictions.license_required: No mandatory municipal permit for single-property hosts, but commercial operations (managing multiple properties or running consistently as a business) require an operating license (Aviso de Operación).

jurisdictions.tax_rate: Progressive national income tax up to 25% on net rental profit; 7% value-added tax (ITBMS) may apply for commercial operations based on turnover. Annual property tax (Impuesto de Bienes Inmuebles) based on cadastral value.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Fewer zoning restrictions than Panama City; allowed in tourism/residential zones. Strata/PH (Propiedad Horizontal) bylaws and HOA rules frequently restrict or prohibit STRs to maintain residential quiet.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (the national 45-day minimum stay rule in Law 80 of 2012 only applies within the District of Panama, leaving beach/rural zones like Playa Venao exempt).

jurisdictions.platform_obligations: No formal platform reporting or verification regime currently enforced.

jurisdictions.additional_context: Condo developments and gated communities have strict internal bylaws. Verify building PH rules before purchasing or listing properties.

jurisdictions.platform_obligations: RNT number required on listings
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Additional Tourism Secretariat permit required beyond standard RNT process
jurisdictions.key_notes: Airbnb specifies hosts must register and obtain permission from the Tourism Secretariat for properties in the archipelago
jurisdictions.penalties: Same as national baseline (fines, RNT suspension)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Distinct verification process; Cartagena island areas (Barú, Islas del Rosario, Tierrabomba) also subject to extra District Mayor verification

jurisdictions.license_required: No national STR-specific license; municipal operating license (Ley 28976) may apply for business-like operations
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — RUC with SUNAT for regular rental income
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Non-residents 5% withholding on gross; residents taxed under 'Renta de Primera Categoría' rules
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Set at municipal/district level; building/condominium rules often restrict STRs
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: No explicit platform compliance mandate; responsibility primarily on hosts
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Since Dec 10, 2024 (Supreme Decree 011-2024-IN) hosts must report foreign-guest data to Migraciones via web portal
jurisdictions.key_notes: No universal STR license; enforcement light; tax declaration to SUNAT expected; STR commonly 'alquiler temporal/vacacional'
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines for failure to report foreign guests; standard tax penalties for undeclared income; no penalties for natural-person landlords under guest-reporting rule
jurisdictions.additional_context: ~45,000 active listings nationwide; Lima ~27,000, Cusco ~7,500, Arequipa ~3,800
jurisdictions.source: lodgecompliance.com/countries/peru; airbnb.com/help/article/3184; thelatinvestor.com/blogs/news/peru-airbnb; assets.airbnb.com Peru Tax Guide 2025

jurisdictions.license_required: Operating license required where allowed; STR effectively prohibited in residential multifamily zones (Ordinance 561/MM amending 342/MM)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — RUC with SUNAT
jurisdictions.tax_rate: National SUNAT rates (5% non-resident; first-category for residents)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Multifamily buildings in residential zones prohibited from use as temporary/rotating residence; STR permitted only in hospitality-compatible zones with license
jurisdictions.min_stay: No national minimum; restriction targets 'temporary or rotating residence' use
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None specific
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Hospitality use in compatible zones requires functioning license per Ordinance 593/MM (2022); building safety requirements (extinguishers, smoke detectors)
