jurisdictions.key_notes: District ordinance bans STR-type use of multifamily homes in residential zones; legality debated as possible infringement of property rights
jurisdictions.penalties: Municipal closure/sanctions for non-compliant land use
jurisdictions.additional_context: Miraflores historically held ~45% of Lima Airbnb listings; serves as regulatory bellwether for other districts

jurisdictions.license_required: Registration with Ministry of Tourism for lodging rented under 120 days (Ley 20.352); mandatory rollout in 2026
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — DGI registration for rental income
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Non-residents flat 12% on rental income (IRNR); residents per IRPF/IRAE
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Limited at national level; some local zoning may apply
jurisdictions.min_stay: Market-driven; framework applies to lodging under 120 days
jurisdictions.density_rules: None nationally; no cap on number of properties per host
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platform compliance/registration provisions rolling out in 2026
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Each property requires individual registration once Mintur system fully operational; framework applies to individuals and entities
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ley 20.352 enacted September 2024; registration system rolling out 2026
jurisdictions.key_notes: New national registration framework being implemented; historically very low compliance/enforcement; foreigners may operate secondary homes
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown (framework under implementation)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Investor-friendly; foreigners have same property rights as locals; coastal market highly seasonal

jurisdictions.license_required: National registration (Ley 20.352) applies; local registration may apply; possible cap on licenses per host
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — DGI registration for rental income
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Non-residents flat 12% (IRNR); property tax under 1% of cadastral value
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Lenient at city level; zoning may apply in some areas
jurisdictions.min_stay: Market-driven; longer stays common in high season
jurisdictions.density_rules: Possible limit on number of licenses per host (reported)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration numbers expected to be displayed on listings
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Very low compliance observed — roughly 1% of properties hold official licenses; condo/gated-community rules may restrict STR
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ley 20.352 (2024); local enforcement minimal as of 2026
jurisdictions.key_notes: Operator-friendly, minimal registration enforcement; building-level/HOA restrictions are main practical constraint
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown — minimal enforcement
jurisdictions.additional_context: ~3,300–4,200 active listings; extreme seasonality (Dec–Feb peak, up to 90% occupancy)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Registro de Turismo (SITURIN) plus municipal Licencia Única Anual de Funcionamiento (LUAF)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — RUC or RIMPE with SRI
jurisdictions.fees: Tourism registration free (siturin.turismo.gob.ec); LUAF fees set by municipality
jurisdictions.tax_rate: VAT (IVA) ~15%; income tax (Impuesto a la Renta) per SRI; IVA reduced to 8% on national holidays for domestic tourists
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Condominium assembly authorization required for tourist use
jurisdictions.min_stay: Temporary/habitual lodging for economic compensation (no fixed cap stated)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Rules apply regardless of promotion medium; SRI ordered ~9,000 platform hosts to regularize (May 2026)
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Guest registry must be kept by host for six months; registration produces individualized QR certificate to display
jurisdictions.effective_date: Reglamento de Alojamiento Turístico en Inmuebles Habitacionales published Sept 21, 2023
jurisdictions.key_notes: Applies to continental Ecuador only — Galápagos governed by its own special regime; ~17,000 hosts targeted for formalization
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown; SRI requires authorized sales receipts and tax declarations
jurisdictions.additional_context: Registration enables tourism credit (5% interest) and IVA-reduction benefits; SRI notified 9,000 hosts to regularize RUC in May 2026

jurisdictions.license_required: Governed by Galápagos special regime (régimen especial), separate from continental rules
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — RUC/RIMPE with SRI
jurisdictions.tax_rate: VAT (IVA) ~15%
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: 97% of islands are protected national park; special environmental controls
jurisdictions.density_rules: UNESCO has recommended a zero-growth tourism approach; growth controls debated
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown — continental reglamento expressly excludes Galápagos
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: STR growth raising over-tourism and sustainability concerns; tension with traditional hotels over uneven standards
jurisdictions.key_notes: Continental STR reglamento explicitly does not apply; islands follow own special-regime normativa
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown
jurisdictions.additional_context: Airbnb listings grew from 56 (2015) to over 1,300 today vs ~300 legal hotels; ~300,000 annual visitors

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Conditional Use Verification (CUV), code-enforcement inspection, and a short-term residential rental business license required before operating.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — transient lodging tax registration required (collected/remitted; platforms collect for some bookings).
jurisdictions.fees: $500 annual business license fee per residential unit (City of Las Vegas). No fee for the CUV review itself. (Note: unincorporated Clark County uses a separate, higher fee schedule.)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — City of Las Vegas allows owner-occupied properties only; owner must reside on-site and be present during each day of a rental.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Unknown (City of Las Vegas does not specify a clear annual night cap in sourced materials; owner-occupancy is the limiting factor).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Generally two guests per bedroom (occupancy posted on permit); 'party' defined as a gathering exceeding maximum occupancy.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Transient lodging/occupancy tax rose from 12% to 13% in 2025 (unincorporated Clark County rate reported around 13.38%).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Must meet zoning eligibility; spacing buffer between licensed STRs (City uses a 660-foot buffer per its STR license map). Many HOAs (Summerlin, The Ridges, etc.) prohibit rentals under 31 days.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as rental of 31 consecutive days or fewer (i.e., stays of 31 days or less are STRs).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Spacing/buffer between licensed STRs (660-foot buffer cited for City of Las Vegas); unincorporated Clark County caps total active licenses around 1% of residential housing stock and currently has a moratorium on new licenses.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — minimum $500,000 general liability coverage from an insurer licensed in Nevada.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Hosting platforms/'accommodations facilitators' are recognized in the ordinance; platforms automatically collect transient tax for many bookings. Applicants must list all platforms used.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Multi-step process (CUV → inspection → business license); inspection checks smoke/CO detectors, egress, fire extinguishers, emergency instructions. Local 24-hour emergency contact must be posted outside the property. City actively monitors listings and enforces owner-occupancy.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Governed by state AB363 (2021) framework; City TOT increase effective 2025; proposed 2025 amendments to LVMC Chapter 6.75 pending. Specific original effective date Unknown.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Jurisdiction is critical: City of Las Vegas (owner-occupied only) vs. unincorporated Clark County (historic residential ban + moratorium on new licenses) vs. Henderson/North Las Vegas, each with separate rules.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to $5,000 cited for violations; owner-occupancy violations have resulted in license revocations and $10,000+ fines; a 2024 case reported a $180,000 fine for an illegal STR.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Applies to the City of Las Vegas. Surrounding jurisdictions differ substantially; verify exact parcel jurisdiction before operating.
jurisdictions.source: City of Las Vegas (lasvegasnevada.gov) STR page and proposed 2025 ordinance amendment packet; Hostaway; Nevada Real Estate Group; Proper Insurance; 5StarSTR (industry trackers).

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — City Home Sharing Registration/STR permit, plus a state DBPR Vacation Rental License and (for some categories) a Business Tax Receipt.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register with Florida Dept. of Revenue and Orange County for sales/tourist development taxes.
jurisdictions.fees: Initial city permit $275 (first year); annual renewal reported at $100–$125 (renewal requires interior inspection). State DBPR vacation rental license ~$50/year.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes for home-sharing — host must live on-site and be present during stays; property must be occupied by owner/long-term tenant at least 51% of the year.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Unknown (no explicit annual night cap; limited instead by owner-occupancy and one-booking-at-a-time rule).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Home-sharing: only one booking at a time, no more than four guests total, and no more than 50% of bedrooms rented.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Approx. 12.5% combined: 6% FL state sales tax + 0.5% Orange County discretionary surtax + 6% Orange County Tourist Development Tax (sources cite 12–14.5% depending on county).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: All residential (R) zoning districts allow only home-sharing arrangements; whole-home/entire-home lodging requires separate approvals in appropriate zones. HOA written permission may be needed.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as rental of less than 30 days.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Unknown (no explicit density/cap rule identified for Orlando).
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown (safety compliance required; specific insurance minimum not clearly sourced for Orlando city).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Under 2025 Florida changes, advertising platforms must collect/remit state and applicable local taxes; hosts still often responsible for remittance/registration. Listing must display registration.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Property must pass code enforcement inspection (smoke detectors, CO alarms, fire extinguishers, emergency exit plans). City transitioned from a full STR ban (pre-2018) to two permitted categories (owner-occupied home sharing and bed & breakfast).
jurisdictions.effective_date: City home-sharing ordinance ~July 1, 2021; statewide SB280 vetoed in 2024, leaving local control intact.
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of Florida's most restrictive STR markets — effectively limits investor/whole-home rentals; many investors use Osceola/Polk counties instead.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines for unregistered listings/non-compliance (specific amounts not clearly sourced); state may penalize unregistered listings.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Orange County (Orlando), Osceola County (Kissimmee), and Polk County have differing rules; Osceola restricts STRs largely to designated tourist zones.
jurisdictions.source: City of Orlando (orlando.gov) Home Sharing Registration and STR factsheet; Florida DBPR/Chapter 509 framework; Minut; The Offer Sheet; FunStay Florida (industry trackers).

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — STR permit (STR-C Classic, STR-OO Owner-Occupied, STR-LE Lodging Exempt, or new STR-Temporary) required, plus a city business license for permittee and qualified owner's representative.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — collect/remit applicable city lodging tax and STR excise tax (to City of Aspen as of Jan 2025) plus other STR taxes to Colorado Dept. of Revenue.
jurisdictions.fees: STR-Classic permit ~$394/year (also nonrefundable fee to hold a waitlist spot); Owner-Occupied (120-day max) permit under ~$150. STR excise tax tiered on assessor home value (e.g., 0.05%–0.07% by night tier).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Depends on permit type — STR-OO requires owner's primary residence (capped 120 nights/yr); STR-C available to non-owner-occupied or owner-occupied units.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: STR-Owner-Occupied capped at 120 nights/year; STR-Classic has no annual night cap (but zone caps apply).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Two occupants per bedroom plus two; studios limited to three total.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Voter-approved additional STR tax of 5% or 10% (by type) on top of existing sales and lodging taxes; ≥70% of STR-tax receipts go to affordable housing.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unlimited STR-C permits in downtown core/lodging zones; capped quotas in residential zone districts (caps set at ~75% of pre-moratorium STR counts; 8 of 14 residential zones at/over cap).
jurisdictions.min_stay: Minimum 4 nights per stay; STR defined as use under 30 days.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Capped number of STR-C permits in certain residential zones with waitlists; permits non-transferable (terminate on sale). History-of-use requirement (STR activity between May 11, 2017–May 11, 2022).
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown (not clearly specified in sourced materials).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Permit number must appear in all third-party advertisements; as of 2025 amendments, platforms must mandate permit numbers in listings and remove non-compliant ads.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Qualified Owner's Representative (QOR) required if permittee not local/available in person. Permit, business license, and Good Neighbor Guide must be posted in unit. Permits with one year of zero tax filings deemed abandoned. Permittee must hold ≥10% ownership; LLCs must name a natural person.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Core program adopted June 2022 (Ordinance #9, Series 2022); amendments approved Nov 18, 2025 (Ordinance #08, Series 2025); city remit changes effective Jan 2025.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Nov 2025 amendments streamlined renewals, removed HOA affidavit for renewals, added emergency/building-permit tax exemptions, and created STR-Temporary permit for pending reservations after a sale.
jurisdictions.penalties: Permits terminated/ineligible for renewal if $0 tax revenue; enforcement via Aspen 311 Connect; specific monetary fines not clearly sourced.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Pitkin County (surrounding) has separate STR rules also capping occupancy at 120 nights/year; Snowmass Village has its own regulations.
jurisdictions.source: City of Aspen (aspen.gov) STR and lodging-tax pages and Nov 2025 council update; Aspen Times; Aspen Journalism; Rent Responsibly; STRProfitMap (industry trackers).

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — STVR certificate required before operating, plus a Business Tax Certificate obtained through the Revenue Department.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Georgia state sales tax registration and local hotel/motel excise tax registration; Business Tax Certificate required.
jurisdictions.fees: STVR permit reported at ~$150/year (Savannah and Chatham County). Zoning Confirmation Letter request fee $50.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Depends — owner-occupied permits available; in the Streetcar district TN-2 parcels require two or more dwelling units, one owner-occupied. Non-owner-occupied STVRs subject to per-ward caps.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Unknown (no explicit annual night cap; STVR defined by stay length of 30 days or less).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 4 adults for units with ≤2 bedrooms; for units with 3+ bedrooms, max 2 adults per bedroom.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Total ~15%: 8% local hotel/motel excise tax (increased from 6% effective Sept 1, 2023) + 7% state.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Permitted only within the STVR Overlay District (Downtown, Victorian, and Streetcar/Mid-City local historic districts) plus certain Business/Agriculture zoning classes (B-C, B-N, B-L, A-1). Mixed-use districts.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STVR defined as rental of an entire dwelling unit for 30 days or less (rooms within a unit regulated separately as bed-and-breakfast homestay).
jurisdictions.density_rules: 20% per-ward cap on non-owner-occupied STVRs in most residential areas; once a cap is reached new STVRs allowed only if owner-occupied.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of insurance specifically indicating the property is used as an STVR (not just general property insurance).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: City uses Rentalscape software to scan listings/identify unpermitted properties; exemplar rental agreement and adjacent-owner notification required for new applicants.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Designated rental agent must be available 24/7 and respond within ~2 hours. New applicants must notify adjacent property owners. Condos must show declaration permits/does-not-prohibit STRs. Certificates not auto-transferable — new owners must apply within 6 months of purchase.
jurisdictions.effective_date: STVR ordinance approved by City Council 8/3/2017 and 9/28/2017; overlay implemented with NEWZO Sept 1, 2019; hotel/motel tax increase effective Sept 1, 2023.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Among Georgia's most comprehensive STR regimes; per-ward cap creates scarcity. Mayor has signaled possible future penalty increases.
jurisdictions.penalties: 1st violation $500; 2nd $750 (within 12 months); 3rd $1,000 (within 12 months).
jurisdictions.additional_context: Properties with a PIN starting with '2' fall under City of Savannah jurisdiction; PIN starting with '1' under Chatham County (separate rules).
jurisdictions.source: City of Savannah (savannahga.gov) STVR pages and ordinance; Savannah municipal code; The Offer Sheet; Awning; BNBCalc; Golden Homes/TeamHiltonHead (industry trackers).

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — STR operational permit from Dept. of Planning, Preservation, and Sustainability plus a city business license; fire safety inspection required before issuance.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — South Carolina sales tax license and accommodations tax registration (city and county).
jurisdictions.fees: STR application fee $350; renewal $250. Business license renewed annually (by April 30); operational permit renewed annually on the issuance anniversary.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes for residential STRs — property must be owner's primary residence (verified via the 4% Legal Residence tax exemption); a full-time resident must sleep on-site each night guests are present. Investment properties are ineligible.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Unknown (no explicit annual night cap; limited by owner-occupancy requirement). STR defined as stays of 1–29 consecutive days.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Maximum 4 adults per STR unit regardless of size/number of bedrooms.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Among the highest in the nation; includes 6% SC state sales tax plus city and county accommodations taxes (exact combined total not fully sourced).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Three residential categories tied to location (Old & Historic District, other downtown, and outlying areas like West Ashley/James/Johns/Daniel Islands), all owner-occupied; one Commercial category in designated commercial zones; STR Overlay Zone in Cannonborough-Elliotborough. STRs prohibited in the 'Point' neighborhood.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as any rental under 30 days (1–29 consecutive days).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Only one STR unit per residential property; one-year holding period for units previously occupied by a long-term tenant; permits non-transferable (new owner must reapply). (Commercial STR permits reportedly capped, e.g., ~400 with waitlist per one tracker.)
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — liability insurance reported with a minimum of $1,000,000 coverage (per industry tracker).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: STR permit number must be listed on all advertisements/booking platforms (Airbnb, Vrbo, FlipKey, etc.); city actively monitors platforms for unpermitted listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Multi-step process: application + documentation (floor plan, site plan, parking, photos) → 15-day public notice posting + 5-day appeal window → fire safety inspection → business license → permit. At least one designated off-street parking space required. Local 24/7 contact able to respond within 30 minutes. Owner must reside ≥183 days/year. No events (weddings/parties).
jurisdictions.effective_date: Current owner-occupancy ordinance effective per recent updates; North Charleston cap amendment (separate jurisdiction) passed Feb 27, 2025. Specific City of Charleston original effective date Unknown.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Whole-house STRs are effectively banned in the City of Charleston; only owner-occupied portions/accessory units (or commercial-zone properties) qualify. Three full-time enforcement officers cited.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to $500 per zoning/noise/refuse violation; a 4th Notice of Violation voids permitting/licensing (North Charleston). One tracker cites City of Charleston fines up to $1,087/day for unlicensed operation; repeat violations risk permit revocation and court summons.
jurisdictions.additional_context: North Charleston is a separate municipality with its own ordinance (60-permit-per-Council-district cap; owner-occupied room rentals exempt). Surrounding counties differ.
jurisdictions.source: City of Charleston (charleston-sc.gov) STR permit and ordinance pages; City of North Charleston; Proper Insurance; The Offer Sheet; HomeTeam Luxury Rentals; TeamWork Properties (industry trackers).

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a Tourist Residency (TR) Permit issued by the City of Gatlinburg, plus city and county business licenses ($15 each). Annual fire/building inspection required before issuance.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register with the Tennessee Department of Revenue for a tax ID and set up state/local sales and lodging tax accounts; county lodging tax filed with Sevier County Trustee.
jurisdictions.fees: TR Permit $200 for 2 or fewer bedrooms, plus $75 per additional bedroom; renewed annually at same schedule. City and county business licenses $15 each.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — permit type does not require primary residence; both owner-occupied and non-owner-occupied STRs are permitted.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No annual night cap; each individual stay must be 89 days or fewer (STRs defined as rentals of 89 days or fewer).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Commonly cited max of 12 people; properties exceeding 3 stories, 5,000 sq ft, or 12 occupants require sprinkler systems.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Approximately 12.75% combined (7% state sales + 2.75% local sales + 3% city/hotel lodging), plus Sevier County lodging tax. Airbnb auto-collects 9.75%; hosts self-remit the 3% city portion; Vrbo hosts self-collect all.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs prohibited in R-1A (Low-Density Residential) and R-2A (Medium Density Residential) zones; permitted in R-3 High Density Residential, Tourism Development Zones, and Commercial (C-1, C-2). Zoning must be verified before applying.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Unknown (no city-imposed minimum identified; max per stay is 89 days)
jurisdictions.density_rules: No cap on the number of permits/licenses; over 6,000 permitted units. Density governed by zoning only.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown — proof of insurance is sometimes requested; $1,000,000 liability coverage is recommended (not confirmed as mandatory).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb automatically collects and remits sales taxes (9.75%); Vrbo does not collect taxes. No evidence of active platform monitoring by the city.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Annual fire and safety inspections required (smoke detectors, CO detectors, fire extinguishers, clear escape routes, posted emergency contact, visible address signage). Permits not transferable upon sale.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Framework stable 2024 through 2026; no recent amendments to city ordinances reported.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Tennessee's Short-Term Rental Unit Act preempts local outright bans based on use/classification, keeping STRs legal. STR properties not used as a principal residence may be reclassified from 25% residential to 40% commercial assessment for property tax.
jurisdictions.penalties: Up to $50 per day per violation; repeated violations may lead to permit suspension or revocation. State law allows permanent loss of STR rights after three documented violations of local laws.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Investor-friendly market; companion state bills SB 104 / HB 109 under consideration could further restrict local authority but no immediate impact expected as of 2026.
jurisdictions.source: City of Gatlinburg (gatlinburgtn.gov); strcityregs.com; strrequirements.com; theshorttermshop.com; redawning.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a city-issued Vacation Home Rental (VHR) permit is required for all rentals of fewer than 30 consecutive days; renewed annually within 30 days prior to expiration.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT) applies and must be collected/remitted.
jurisdictions.fees: $281 application fee at online submittal and for every renewal period (figure cited for Hosted Rental application).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No (Measure T's permanent-resident exception was struck down as unconstitutional in 2025; current ordinance does not require primary residency).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Unknown (no specific annual night cap identified; STR defined as fewer than 30 consecutive days).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Two adults per bedroom; up to five children aged 13 and under do not count toward the limit. Minimum renter age is 25.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: TOT applies (specific rate not stated in sources).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: VHRs allowed in residential areas again under the amended ordinance, subject to permitting. Tourist Core / commercial / recreational areas regulated under Tourist Core Area Plan and not subject to the 900-permit residential cap.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Rentals of fewer than 30 consecutive days are classed as short-term (i.e., 30-day minimum to be exempt from VHR rules).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Hard cap of no more than 900 VHR permits in residential areas; prior 150-foot buffer between VHRs removed. A waitlist opens once the cap is reached.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Advertising platforms must include 'family friendly' language for VHRs in residential areas; valid permit required to advertise rentals under 30 days.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Requires 24/7 local property manager, in-person or virtual guest check-in with good-neighbor contract, indoor noise monitoring, exterior video cameras, animal-resistant/bear-box trash storage, 10 p.m. outdoor amplified-music/pool-spa cutoff. Weddings, receptions, parties, and commercial activities prohibited. Permits not transferable in a sale (transfer into a family trust for estate planning is allowed).
jurisdictions.effective_date: Current amended ordinance (2026-1203) effective April 23, 2026, 12:00 a.m.; builds on the 2025 ordinance (Ord. 2025-1200) effective July 17, 2025.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Attached condominiums eligible unless HOA rules prohibit VHRs (apartments excluded). Permit-denial appeals go to an independent hearing officer. Measure T (2018) had banned residential STRs from 2021 until struck down in court in 2025.
jurisdictions.penalties: Administrative citations for noise, parking, occupancy, or trash complaints (owner and occupants both liable); repeat violators have permits revoked. Enforced 24/7 by SLT Police Department Community Services Division.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Ordinance passed 3-2 on March 24, 2026; Measure T litigation reportedly still working through the courts. Note nearby Nevada (Douglas County/Tahoe Township) has a separate 600-permit cap and El Dorado County areas use a 500-foot buffer.
jurisdictions.source: City of South Lake Tahoe (cityofslt.gov); Tahoe Daily Tribune; South Tahoe Now; MyNews4

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a city business license is required to operate any rental; no separate STR permit, but zoning must allow the use.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — set up local accommodations tax accounts with the city, register with the SC Department of Revenue for state lodging tax, and create a Horry County hospitality fee account.
jurisdictions.fees: Business license fee is gross-receipts based (city fee schedule); renewed annually. Contact city for renewal fee details.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Approximately 10% on bookings (local accommodations tax) plus SC state lodging tax and Horry County hospitality fee.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Highly restrictive: STRs prohibited in all residential zones beginning with 'R' except the RMV (Residential Multifamily Visitor) zone; otherwise STRs are treated as a commercial use allowed mainly in commercial districts. 'Short-term' defined locally as any rental of less than 90 days (differs from SC's 30-day definition).
jurisdictions.min_stay: Rentals of less than 90 days are classed as short-term under city zoning.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Fewer than ~30 houses in traditional residential zones are grandfathered (consistently used as STRs prior to existing zoning); properties not already in an STR-allowed zone cannot be converted.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb collects lodging taxes for the state, Myrtle Beach, and Horry County; Vrbo collects state lodging tax and Myrtle Beach local accommodations tax. If platforms don't collect, the host must remit.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: STRs must comply with zoning, business-license ordinance, and applicable trash/parking/noise/occupancy rules. A 2024/2025 conversion-overlay ordinance bans converting STR buildings (hotels/STRs with more than two units) to long-term rentals within a ~114-block overlay east of Kings Highway.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Conversion overlay ordinance (2024-069) effective immediately upon passage (reported Jan 2025); zoning framework ongoing.
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of the most restrictive STR markets in South Carolina. Operators are exempt from collecting accommodations tax if renting fewer than 15 days/year or renting six or fewer bedrooms in an owner-occupied home without using STR marketplaces.
jurisdictions.penalties: Zoning code and business-license violations are misdemeanors with fines up to $500 and/or 30 days upon conviction, per violation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Distinct from neighboring North Myrtle Beach, which requires only a business license with no special STR zoning/permit process. Verify zoning before purchase.
jurisdictions.source: City of Myrtle Beach (cityofmyrtlebeach.com); strcityregs.com; strprofitmap.com; Avalara MyLodgeTax

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a Nightly Rental License from the Park City Finance Department is required for any rental under 30 days; property-specific and non-transferable. Properties outside city limits require a Summit County license.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must maintain a Utah State Tax Commission sales tax account and register for transient room taxes.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual base business license fee plus a per-unit nightly-rental add-on fee; varies by property type and renewed annually. Specific amounts not stated.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Unknown (no night cap; STR defined as under 30 days).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Set by local code and building/fire code; the jurisdiction's/fire marshal's capacity determination controls (no universal bedroom-based formula).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: State Sales Tax 4.85% + State Transient Room Tax up to 4.25% + Summit County Transient Room Tax up to 3%, plus Park City's Resort Communities Sales Tax portion. (Statewide STR taxes commonly total ~10-14% depending on locale.)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Permitted only in designated zones, primarily resort/tourist areas (e.g., Old Town, Canyons Village). Prohibited in some residential areas (e.g., Prospector; Meadows Estates Phase 1A and 1B prohibited outright). Some zones (e.g., Western Historic Residential–Low Density) limit permits to a small number under conditional use.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Rentals of fewer than 30 days are classed as nightly/short-term.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Permit allocations capped in certain zones (e.g., ~12 properties under conditional use in the Western Historic Residential–Low Density Zone); otherwise governed by zoning overlays.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — operators must maintain active liability insurance covering guest injury or damage.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb/Vrbo) often collect and remit taxes automatically, but owners remain legally responsible; license number must be displayed on all online listings/advertisements.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Requires verified zoning eligibility, building/safety inspection before listing, a 24/7 responsible local contact, written management plan (guest policies, trash, parking, emergency procedures), life-safety equipment (smoke/CO detectors, extinguishers, egress), posted license number, and annual renewal. Nightly rentals governed by Municipal Code sections 4-2-1 and 4-5-3.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Land Management Code amendments tightening residential restrictions ongoing through 2025-2026; verify current zoning via the city's interactive STR map.
jurisdictions.key_notes: HOA/CC&R rules can prohibit or further limit nightly rentals even where city zoning allows them; municipal permits do not override private covenants. Property must hold a valid license before being listed.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a license can trigger fines or suspension; enforcement is reportedly strict (daily fines cited). Owners are liable for noise, occupancy, and other violations.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Highly localized: Park City Municipal (zip 84060) vs. unincorporated Summit County / Snyderville Basin (zip 84098) have distinct rules; ADUs not permitted for STR use in the Basin.
jurisdictions.source: Skyrun Park City guide; strprofitmap.com; Hostaway; Mullin Real Estate; insideparkcityrealestate.com (City of Park City Municipal Code references)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — national CIN (Codice Identificativo Nazionale) from the Ministry of Tourism's BDSR database is mandatory, plus a regional CIR (Codice Identificativo Regionale) for Lazio. Business activity (entrepreneurial form) additionally requires a SCIA filing with the local SUAP/municipality.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register guests via the Alloggiati Web police portal; income reporting under cedolare secca or ordinary/VAT regime; tourist tax (contributo di soggiorno) must be collected and remitted to the Comune.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown (no fixed CIN/CIR application fee identified in sources).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Unknown (affitti brevi defined as rentals of less than 30 days; EU rules cover stays under one year).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (legal capacity enforced via inspections; over-capacity rentals have been fined).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Cedolare secca flat tax: 21% on the first property, 26% from the second property (entrepreneurial/VAT treatment generally from the third property under 2026 rules). Separate Rome tourist tax of ~€3-€10 per person/night by accommodation category, with short-term rentals reportedly around €5.50-€6 flat, capped at 10 consecutive nights.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unknown for citywide STR zoning; CIN/CIR registration and SCIA (where business) govern operation. National key-box ban applies (see compliance).
jurisdictions.min_stay: Less than 30 days defines a short-term/tourist rental (locazione turistica).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Unknown (no formal Rome-wide permit cap identified; Lazio reportedly considering Tuscany-style restrictions in 2026).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: CIN must appear in all listings and advertisements; platforms cannot legally list properties without it. Airbnb applies a 21% withholding on qualifying bookings (Booking.com optional opt-in). From May 20, 2026, EU Regulation 2024/1028 requires platforms to verify CIN codes and remove non-compliant listings, with monthly booking-data transmission to authorities; DAC7 reporting cross-checked by the tax agency.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Mandatory safety equipment: working smoke detectors, carbon monoxide detectors, and serviced fire extinguishers. In-person guest identification is mandatory and self-check-in via key boxes is prohibited (Council of State, Nov 2025); Rome treats key boxes on public spaces as illegal and has removed them. Guest details reported via Alloggiati Web within 24 hours of check-in.
jurisdictions.effective_date: CIN mandatory from January 1, 2025 (BDSR live since Sept 3, 2024); EU Reg. 2024/1028 obligations from May 20, 2026; 2026 Finance Law tax updates in effect/under debate.
jurisdictions.key_notes: In Rome, hosts can rent up to ~three properties before being classified as a business (triggering SCIA/VAT and further obligations). Tourist tax applies up to 10 consecutive nights; children under 10 and Rome residents exempt.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a CIN: fines €800-€8,000. Failure to display the CIN in ads/outside the property: €500-€5,000. Renting more than the allowed number of properties without a SCIA: €2,000-€10,000. Key-box violations on public structures (per related city rules): €100-€400 plus removal costs.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Heavy enforcement in Rome, especially after Jubilee 2025; the Department of Tourism identified ~12,000 unregistered lodgings and joint inspections with the Guardia di Finanza have fined non-compliant operators. CIN requirement reportedly removed ~20-30% of Italian listings.
jurisdictions.source: Ministry of Tourism BDSR (bdsr.ministeroturismo.gov.it); taxing.it; tuscanhouses.com; The Local Italy; getproofsnap.com; idealista.it; Euronews; lodgecompliance.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — national CIN (Codice Identificativo Nazionale) via the BDSR portal plus the Veneto regional CIR; a SCIA filing with the municipality is required if renting more than 120 days/year.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — registration for tourist tax (via Venice's IDS portal) and guest reporting via Alloggiati Web; CIN linked to tax records.
jurisdictions.fees: No national fee for the CIN itself (free via Ministry of Tourism portal); indirect costs for safety equipment/documentation. Specific Venice SCIA fees: Unknown.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — secondary homes and investment properties may operate short-term rentals provided they complete registration and obtain a CIN.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 120 days/year for owners who do not sign the municipal regulation; those renting beyond 120 days must file a SCIA and sign the municipal conduct regulation.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Cedolare secca flat tax of 21% on income for a first property, rising to 26% for additional properties (2-4); 5+ properties treated as a business (IRPEF + VAT). Tourist tax €3.50 per person/night for up to 5 nights.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Tighter zoning enforcement in the historic center; high-saturation neighborhoods include San Marco, San Polo (Rialto), Cannaregio and Dorsoduro; policy aims to manage residential-to-tourist conversion without an outright ban.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Italy's major art cities (including Venice) are implementing/considering minimum two-night stays; not definitively confirmed for Venice citywide.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Venice is considering limits on the total number of properties per host in certain neighborhoods; hosts can rent a maximum of three short-term properties.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Not strictly required by national law but strongly recommended; liability cover advised.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must share data with Italian authorities; listings without a CIN can be hidden/delisted; Airbnb collects/remits tourist tax for qualifying rentals.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Hosts must display the CIN on all listings and outside the property, report guests to police via Alloggiati Web within 24 hours, collect tourist tax, and (for >120 days) provide in-person reception, separate-waste bags, and a 24-hour emergency contact. Key boxes/lockboxes were banned nationwide late 2024, partially relaxed by a May 2025 court ruling.
jurisdictions.effective_date: National CIN mandatory from 2025-01-01; Venice municipal 120-day regulation approved by council October 2024 with a 120-day buffer period, in force through end of 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Three regulatory layers: Veneto regional registration (CIR), national CIN (Ministry of Tourism), and Venice municipal rules that impose stricter requirements above 120 rental days/year. In Veneto the CIR must be displayed outside the door on a white plaque reading 'locazione turistica' or on the building's button panel.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines can start as high as €3,000 for unregistered rentals; national CIN non-compliance €800–€8,000 (no CIN) and €500–€5,000 (failure to display); tourist-tax non-payment €500–€2,000; permit revocation for serious/repeat violations.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Venice is one of Italy's most regulated STR markets; the day-tripper access fee (€5) runs on selected peak days (e.g., 3 April–26 July 2026) but is separate from STR rules. Median entire-home nightly price ~€190 in early 2026.
jurisdictions.source: Comune di Venezia 2025 regulation announcement; investropa.com (Venice Airbnb 2026; Italy rule changes Sept 2025); airbtics.com Venice; campaignforalivingvenice.org (Oct 2024); thelocal.it (Jan 2026); idealista.it (2026 tourist tax).

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — national CIN plus Campania regional code (referred to as CIR / CUSR — Codice Univoco Strutture Ricettive) obtained via the regional tourism platform; a SCIA must be submitted to the Naples municipality.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — registration for tourist tax with the Naples municipality and guest data reporting via the state police Alloggiati Web portal.
jurisdictions.fees: No national fee for the CIN. Specific Naples municipal SCIA fees: Unknown (note: nearby Sorrento charges a €150 fee plus blueprints/technical reports).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — but rentals are generally limited to entire properties (not individual rooms) unless the owner resides on-site.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No municipal night cap confirmed; Campania allows renting up to four units without incorporating as a business.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Cedolare secca 21% (first property), 26% (properties 2-4), business/VAT for 5+. Tourist tax from 1 March 2025: short-term rentals €5 per person/night, applicable up to 14 nights (earlier rate was €3).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: No confirmed citywide zoning ban; Naples is reported to be exploring limits/quotas and applies additional fire-safety certifications for historic buildings; possible seasonal restrictions in peak summer.
jurisdictions.min_stay: No Naples-specific minimum-stay confirmed; written contract required for stays under 30 days.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Campania caps non-professional hosts at four properties; no Naples-specific building density rule confirmed.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Not strictly required by national law; recommended. No additional services beyond final cleaning/linen permitted or property is reclassified as 'extra-hotel'.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must verify CIN and share data with authorities; listings without a CIN may be hidden; CIR/CUSR must appear on all listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Notify municipality by certified email (PEC); obtain CUSR/CIR; display code on listings; submit SCIA; provide written rental contract; report guests to police within 24 hours; collect tourist tax remitted quarterly; upload maximum prices to the regional system by October 1. National safety equipment (smoke/CO/gas detectors, fire extinguishers) mandatory.
jurisdictions.effective_date: National CIN mandatory from 2025-01-01; revised Naples tourist tax effective 2025-03-01 (Council Resolution No. 572, 9 Dec 2024).
jurisdictions.key_notes: Strong tourist demand (~9,900 Airbnb listings, ~4M+ arrivals annually). Cannot provide hotel-style services without reclassification. Campania reportedly planning new restrictions modelled on Tuscany's 2025 law — watch for 2026 updates.
jurisdictions.penalties: National CIN non-compliance €800–€8,000 (no CIN), €500–€5,000 (display failure), €600–€6,000 (safety violations); CIR display failure fines up to ~€2,500; tax non-compliance penalties; municipal crackdown on illegal rentals ongoing.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Some sources cite an older/legacy €3 per-person tourist-tax figure; the current municipal rate for short-term rentals is €5 per person/night (from March 2025) for up to 14 nights.
jurisdictions.source: thelocal.it (Jan 2026 regional rules; June 2025); bnbcalc.com Naples; airbtics.com Naples; idealista.it (2026 tourist tax); immobiliare.it (Naples tourist tax); travelandtourworld.com; taxing.it (CIR/CIN).

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — national CIN plus Campania regional code (CIR / CUSR) via the regional tourism platform; SCIA/notification to the relevant municipality. Some municipalities (e.g., Sorrento) require detailed blueprints and technical reports.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — tourist tax registration with the relevant municipality and guest reporting via Alloggiati Web.
jurisdictions.fees: No national CIN fee. Sorrento requires a €150 fee plus blueprints/technical reports; other Amalfi municipalities' fees: Unknown.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — but Campania limits non-professional hosts and entire-property rules generally apply.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No region-wide night cap confirmed; Campania permits up to four units without incorporating. Individual coastal municipalities enforce their own limits.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Cedolare secca 21% (first property), 26% (2-4), business/VAT for 5+. Tourist tax varies by municipality — e.g., town of Amalfi (high season ~1 April–3 Nov): €3.00 per person/night for stays under 5 nights, €1.50 for stays of 5+ nights. Sorrento and other towns set their own rates.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Municipality-specific; coastal towns enforce their own rules and limits. UNESCO heritage protections apply along the coast.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Municipality-specific; Unknown for the coast as a whole.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Campania caps non-professional hosts at four properties; town-level rules vary.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Not strictly required by national law; recommended. No services beyond final cleaning/linen to retain short-term classification.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must verify and share CIN data; CIR/CUSR must be displayed on listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: The 'Amalfi Coast' spans multiple municipalities (Amalfi, Positano, Ravello, Sorrento, etc.), each with its own ordinance, tax rate and procedures; hosts must verify rules for the specific town. National safety equipment mandatory; guest reporting within 24 hours.
jurisdictions.effective_date: National CIN mandatory from 2025-01-01; Amalfi tourist tax rates current for 2026 high season.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Short, busy seasons mean costs are often passed through quickly to guests. As a multi-municipality coastal region, no single unified STR ordinance exists — treat each town separately.
jurisdictions.penalties: National CIN non-compliance €800–€8,000; display/safety fines €500–€6,000; CIR display failure up to ~€2,500; municipal penalties vary.
jurisdictions.additional_context: This record aggregates the Campania regional framework plus representative municipal tourist-tax data (town of Amalfi and Sorrento); rates and caps differ town by town along the coast.
jurisdictions.source: thelocal.it (Jan 2026; June 2025 regional rules); estatefy.com (Campania); trippz.com (Amalfi tourist tax); idealista.it (2026 tourist tax); travelandtourworld.com; taxing.it.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Andalusian regional licence VFT/VUT (Vivienda con Fines Turísticos / Vivienda de Uso Turístico) registered in the Registro de Turismo de Andalucía (RTA), plus the national VUD/NRU registration number (mandatory since 1 July 2025). A first-occupation licence (licencia de primera ocupación) is required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: No specific municipal STR tourist tax in Seville/Andalusia, but national registration (VUD/NRU) and guest data reporting via SES.HOSPEDAJES are mandatory; income is taxable.
jurisdictions.fees: First-occupation licence approx. €400–€800; VFT registration via responsible declaration (declaración responsable). Specific RTA fee: Unknown.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — but in new tourist rentals within shared buildings, community-of-owners approval (3/5 / 60% majority) is required since 3 April 2025.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No annual night cap; the Andalusian definition of holiday rental is stays under 2 months (national 'short-term' threshold under 60 days).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: No dedicated municipal tourist tax in Seville; rental income subject to Spanish income tax (rate depends on residency status). Specific rate: Unknown.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: City cap: no more than 10% of total homes per neighborhood (across Seville's 108 neighborhoods) may be tourist apartments; saturated areas receive no new licences. In the historic center 'ring of protection', tourist rentals are generally only permitted in detached, single-family homes — not in apartments/condominiums.
jurisdictions.density_rules: 10% cap on tourist homes per neighborhood; saturated zones frozen to new licences.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Liability insurance recommended; Andalusia decree sets technical comfort/safety/equipment standards. Strict requirement: Unknown.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Since 1 July 2025 platforms must verify the national VUD/NRU number before listing; properties without it removed (typically within 48 hours). Licence number must appear on all listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Three-layer system: national VUD/NRU registration + SES.HOSPEDAJES guest reporting; Andalusian VFT/VUT registration (Decree 28/2016 and amendments, Decreto 31/2024); plus municipal Seville cap and HOA 3/5 community veto. Guest data must be sent to the Ministry of the Interior within 24 hours.
jurisdictions.effective_date: National VUD/NRU enforced from 1 July 2025; HOA approval requirement from 3 April 2025; Seville 10% neighborhood cap in force (2024–2025).
jurisdictions.key_notes: As of May 2026, ~42% of Sevilla communities have voted to ban VFT activity (Junta de Andalucía). Over 10,300 Andalusian licences withdrawn since 2024 (incl. Sevilla); city withdrew VFT licences for technical non-compliance and proposed cutting water to ~5,000 illegal flats. Inspections intensify during Semana Santa and Feria de Abril.
jurisdictions.penalties: Andalusia fines roughly €2,000–€150,000 (and up to €600,000 under the 2026 Sustainable Tourism Bill); national rules up to €60,000; consequences include suspension/forced closure and licence revocation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Existing licensed rentals from before 3 April 2025 are exempt from retroactive HOA approval. VFT licence has 'carácter real' — tied to the property; a new owner typically re-registers the change of titular.
jurisdictions.source: etias.com (Seville 10% cap); Tripadvisor Seville board (Jan 2026, citing city rules); buvivo.com (2026); getproofsnap.com (2026); travelnest.com; spaineasy.com; lpaspain.com; mikenaumannimmobilien.com; aplaceinthesun.com.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Balearic ETV licence (Estancia Turística de Viviendas) required for all rentals under 30 days, plus the national VUD/NRU registration. However, no new ETV licences are being issued under the moratorium, and in Palma apartment lets in residential buildings are prohibited entirely.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — national VUD/NRU registration and SES.HOSPEDAJES guest reporting; Balearic Sustainable Tourism Tax (ecotasa) applies to overnight stays.
jurisdictions.fees: Balearic licence fees among the highest in Spain — reportedly up to ~€14,000 depending on island/property type. Specific Palma figures: Unknown.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No general primary-residence rule, but some saturated zones (e.g., Playa de Palma) historically allowed villa-type rentals only if the owner lives in the property, capped at 60 days/year.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: In certain zones (e.g., Playa de Palma) villa-type rentals are capped at 60 days/year; apartments in residential buildings are banned outright in Palma.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Balearic Sustainable Tourism Tax (ecotasa) applies per person/night (seasonally variable); exact STR rate: Unknown. Rental income subject to Spanish income tax.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Palma sets its own rules separate from the island zoning plan: renting apartments in residential buildings is forbidden; detached/villa-type houses may be allowed in permitted zones subject to strict conditions. Santa Catalina, La Lonja and Playa de Palma designated tourism-saturation zones.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Short-term defined as under 30 days (ETV); without an ETV, only stays of 1 month+ allowed under the Urban Leasing Law (LAU).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Palma capped at ~639 registered holiday homes; one person may hold ETV licences for a maximum of 3 properties; island-wide bed-exchange/quota system frozen.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Mandatory/recommended homeowner insurance discussed for Balearic owners (2025); specific STR insurance requirement: Unknown.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must display each property's official ETV number and verify the national VUD/NRU; unlicensed listings removed and penalized. Spain fined Airbnb and removed 20,000+ Balearic flats from platforms.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: ETV licence is attached to the property (non-transferable to another home); the only legal route to operate is buying a property that already holds a valid ETV. Community-of-owners approval (3/5 majority) needed for new lets in shared buildings; guest data to SES.HOSPEDAJES within 24 hours.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Balearic ETV moratorium in place since 11 February 2022 (originally through 2026, signals point to indefinite extension); Palma apartment ban and ~639 cap in force; national VUD/NRU from 1 July 2025.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Palma bans all new short-stay licences (no new ETVs after 2025) and prohibits apartment-building holiday lets entirely. Consell de Mallorca opened a temporary pool of 1,069 home rental places via public draw (applications 23 Mar–8 Apr 2026), but Palma's restrictions on new licences continue.
jurisdictions.penalties: Renting without a valid ETV is illegal; fines start at €5,000 and can reach €50,000+ (Palma sources cite up to €40,000 for advertising/renting unlicensed homes); regional fines can be higher.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Status marked 'Banned' for the dominant apartment market in Palma; properties holding pre-existing ETV licences in permitted zones (mostly detached houses) may continue operating. The licence has become a tradeable premium asset.
jurisdictions.source: healthplanspain.com (Palma 2026 ban, 639 cap); co-ownership-property.com (2026); reiderstadinvest.com; imperial-properties.com (2026 ETV guide); checkinscan.net; helencummins.com; aplaceinthesun.com; mallorcaowners.com.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — an ETV (Estancia Turística en Viviendas) tourist license from the Consell de Eivissa is mandatory; renting without one is illegal. Long-term rentals over one month do not require it.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register with the Spanish Tax Agency (IVA), the regional/island Eco Tax, and obtain the national Registro de Alquiler (NRA) number mandatory since July 1, 2025.
jurisdictions.fees: Historically ~€600 application plus per-bed bed-acquisition fees (e.g., ~€4,300 for 1 bed up to ~€21,934 for 5 beds); new licenses currently not processed due to moratorium.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No primary-residence requirement; however even renting a spare room while living in the home is considered tourist rental requiring a license.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: In rural areas, only pre-1956 properties may rent up to 60 days/year (max three rooms) under the 2024 PTI modification; otherwise license-dependent.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Capped to the number of authorized beds/places on the license; overcrowding prohibited.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Sustainable Tourism (Eco) Tax rising from €4 to €6 per person/night in peak season; plus applicable IVA and a local 2% Ecotasa cited by some sources.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Apartment lets banned in Ibiza town; rural tourist rentals cut by ~80% under 2024 PTI; PGOU zoning distinguishes residential vs. consolidated tourist areas.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Short-term defined as stays not exceeding ~31 days/3 months; long-term (non-tourist) rentals must exceed one month.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Tourist carrying-capacity limits and 'bed' caps; new-bed moratorium since Feb 2022 freezing new licenses.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Liability insurance is mandatory in many Spanish communities; treat as required (verify locally).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration/NRA number must be displayed on Airbnb, Booking, Vrbo, etc.; non-display can trigger listing removal; platforms remit Eco Tax in some cases.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Since Dec 2, 2024, all guests (incl. minors) must be pre-registered in the SES Hospedajes system; from April 3, 2025, 60% of building co-owners must approve new tourist rentals.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Moratorium since Feb 2022 (due to end 2026); guest-registration Dec 2, 2024; NRA registry July 1, 2025; Eco Tax increase Jan 1, 2025.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A four-year moratorium freezes issuance of new tourist licenses across the Balearics; existing licenses may be sold with the property. STR listings reportedly dropped ~50% in 2025.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines for illegal rental range €20,000–€40,000, up to €400,000 in the Balearics for severe violations; immediate listing removal and closure orders.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Driven by overtourism and housing pressure; the Anti-Intrusion Office collected ~€1.1M in penalties over one year per reports.
jurisdictions.source: letusibiza.com; ibiza-inside.com; ibizaprestige.com; bnbcalc.com; aplaceinthesun.com; holidu.co.uk

jurisdictions.license_required: Mandatory declaration/registration at the town hall with a 13-character registration number required for all furnished tourist rentals; secondary residences require a 'change of use' commercial authorization.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — town-hall registration plus SIRET number via Guichet Unique; income reported under BIC; national declaration required.
jurisdictions.fees: Town-hall declaration is free; SIRET registration free; costs arise from change-of-use compensation requirements.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Not strictly, but primary residences (occupied ≥8 months/year) get simpler treatment; secondary residences face change-of-use and compensation rules.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 120 days/year for primary residences; Nice can reduce to 90 days under the Le Meur law; secondary residences with change-of-use authorization can rent year-round.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Tourist tax (taxe de séjour) collected per guest per night by the city, often via the platform; income tax under BIC (allowances reduced to 50%/30% from 2025).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Quotas being introduced in four sectors — Old Nice, city centre, Riquier-Port-Mont-Boron, and the west coast — aiming to halve furnished-tourist-rental permits.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Seasonal rental contracts cannot exceed 90 days for the same tenant; mobility leases run 1–10 months (min 30+ days).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Sector quotas; change-of-use authorization valid 6 years, one per household; compensation required from the second property rented by the same owner.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown (advisable but not confirmed mandatory at municipal level).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb) must block listings exceeding day limits and report host income to French tax authorities under DAC7; registration number must appear in listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Co-ownership bans on tourist rentals can now be adopted by a two-thirds majority (vs. unanimity); many Nice condominiums are voting bans even where City Hall approves.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Declaration mandatory since 2017; Le Meur law published Nov 20, 2024 with measures phasing in through 2025–2026 (national declaration deadline May 20, 2026).
jurisdictions.key_notes: Among France's strictest Riviera regimes; co-ownership private rules can override municipal approval, and quotas target the most touristic sectors.
jurisdictions.penalties: Up to €5,000 for renting without declaration in a registration municipality; up to €10,000 for exceeding the day cap; €15,000 civil fine and up to €50,000 without change-of-use.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Nice receives ~5M visitors annually; rules aim to preserve long-term housing.
jurisdictions.source: hostaway.com; agence-winter.com; nice-vue-mer.fr; monsieurfolke.com; cabinet-roche.com; service-public.gouv.fr

jurisdictions.license_required: Mandatory declaration of furnished tourist accommodation to the Cannes town hall; single room in a primary residence and hotel-type accommodation are exempt; change of use applies for secondary residences.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — town-hall declaration plus SIRET via Guichet Unique; income declared under BIC.
jurisdictions.fees: Town-hall declaration free; compliance costs arise from classification and any change-of-use compensation.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No, but primary residences benefit from the 120-day allowance; secondary residences can be rented year-round once declared.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 120 days/year for primary residences (can be reduced to 90 under the Le Meur law given Riviera tourist pressure); secondary residences year-round if declared.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Tourist tax collected per night (mobility-lease guests exempt); income tax under BIC with 2025 reduced allowances (50% classified / 30% unclassified).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Le Meur law allows quotas and primary-residence-reserved zones in the PLU; Cannes is reportedly considering Airbnb quotas starting 2026.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Short-term defined as ≤90 days in France; no specific Cannes minimum confirmed.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Quotas under consideration for 2026; not yet formally adopted as of reporting.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration number required on listings; platforms report income under DAC7 and must enforce day caps.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Co-ownership rules can prohibit tourist rentals (two-thirds majority); owner must inform the syndic; DPE energy rating (A–E) required for new seasonal rentals.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Le Meur law (Nov 19/20, 2024) phasing in 2025–2026; national declaration deadline May 20, 2026; specific Cannes quota measures expected 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Exact local measures still being defined; tourist pressure makes a 90-day cap and quotas likely. ~17,000 Airbnb listings reported.
jurisdictions.penalties: National framework: up to €10,000 for failure to register, €15,000 for exceeding day cap, up to €20,000 for false registration numbers.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Glamorous festival city with high seasonal demand; Le Meur law gives the mayor expanded regulatory tools.

jurisdictions.license_required: Mandatory registration with the town hall before renting; secondary residences (or >120 days) require a change-of-use authorization plus compensation.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — town-hall registration/tele-declaration plus SIRET via Guichet Unique; income declared under BIC.
jurisdictions.fees: Registration is administrative; significant cost arises from the compensation obligation (providing an equivalent housing unit).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No, but only primary residences benefit from the day-cap allowance without change of use; secondary homes need change-of-use + compensation.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Reduced from 120 to 90 days/year for primary residences effective January 1, 2026 (decided June 26, 2025).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Tourist tax collected per local rules; income tax under BIC with 2025 reduced allowances (50% classified / 30% unclassified).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Strictest in city center (Presqu'île, Croix-Rousse, Vieux-Lyon); compensation required from the first square meter in these zones.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Short-term defined as ≤90 days nationally; same tenant stay cannot exceed 90 consecutive days for a primary residence.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Compensation rule: convert another property of equivalent size to residential use, within the same district in central zones; properties >35m² outside the hypercenter require compensation.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration number required on listings; platforms report income under DAC7; city actively monitors listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Co-ownership bylaws may prohibit tourist rentals (two-thirds majority); DPE A–E required for new seasonal rentals; ~14,000+ properties registered, ~75% primary residences.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Tele-declaration since 2018; stricter Metropolis rules 2022; 90-day cap effective January 1, 2026 under the Le Meur-Echaniz law.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Lyon's 120-day cap was the highest in Europe; reduced to 90 days from 2026 to combat housing pressure as STRs reached ~one-third of overnight stays in 2023.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €5,000 per property for change-of-use/registration non-compliance; national framework adds up to €15,000 for day-cap breaches and €20,000 for false numbers.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Driven by housing-market pressure in central neighborhoods; regular city inspections and legal proceedings for violators.

jurisdictions.license_required: AMA (Arithmos Mitroou Akiniton / Property Registry Number) required via the AADE Short-Term Rental Registry before listing on any platform; one AMA per property/unit. No freeze in force yet (under government consideration).
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — AMA registration through AADE; foreign owners must obtain a Greek tax number (AFM); annual income reporting through the AADE STR portal.
jurisdictions.fees: Compliance costs ~€300–€600/year (liability insurance, fire safety equipment, pest control certificates).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: From October 1, 2025, only properties designated as 'primary use' (proper residential dwellings) may be registered; basements, storage, garages excluded.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No annual day cap; short-term defined as any lease up to 59 days (per lease, not per year); 60+ days treated as long-term.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Climate Crisis Resilience Fee €8/night high season (Apr–Oct), up from €1.50 (~€2 winter); villas >80m² €15/night; income tax 15–45%; 13% VAT and Hospitality Fee if operating 3+ properties or as a legal entity.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: No registration freeze active yet in Santorini, but the government is preparing to extend the new-AMA freeze to Santorini (alongside Thessaloniki, Paros, Chania, Halkidiki).
jurisdictions.min_stay: No national minimum stay; 59-day threshold separates short-term from long-term.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Individuals limited to two STR properties (income as rental); three or more triggers business/legal-entity treatment with VAT. Geographic freeze under consideration.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — civil liability insurance from a licensed Greek insurer mandatory since October 1, 2025 (Law 5170/2025); platform 'AirCover' not recognized.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: AMA must be displayed on every listing (Airbnb, Booking.com, Vrbo); platforms remove listings without a valid AMA; DAC7/EU Reg 2024/1028 reporting to AADE (from May 2026).
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Law 5170/2025 mandates fire extinguishers, smoke detectors, emergency lighting/signage, natural lighting/ventilation, electrical and pest-control certificates; non-compliant properties delisted automatically.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Law 5170/2025 effective October 1, 2025; Climate fee increase January 1, 2025; EU Regulation 2024/1028 from May 20, 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Santorini faces a €20/person cruise-visitor tax and possible future STR permit freeze; it remains one of Greece's highest-earning STR markets (~€44,000/yr average per host).
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines €5,000–€20,000 (up to €20,000 for repeat offenses); operating in restricted/frozen areas: fine of 50% of income, min €20,000; AMA revocation and delisting for non-compliance.
jurisdictions.additional_context: South Aegean is among Greece's most listing-dense regions; a proposed rule would cancel (non-transfer) the AMA on sale, inheritance, or gift, which is contested by POMIDA and STAMA.
jurisdictions.source: hostaway.com; buygreece.us; getproofsnap.com; greekcitytimes.com; keeptalkinggreece.com; argophilia.com; investropa.com; expatsgreece.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — every short-term rental must obtain an AMA (Property Registry Number) from AADE; the AMA must be displayed on all listings.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — registration with AADE (Greek tax authority) and an AFM (tax number); platforms report under DAC7.
jurisdictions.fees: No fixed national AMA fee documented; hosts bear costs for safety/insurance certifications. Unknown for exact municipal fee.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — but as of Oct 1, 2025, non-primary-use spaces (storage rooms, garages) can no longer be registered as STRs.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No firm enacted cap for Mykonos as of June 2026; a national 90-day cap (60 days on small islands) has been discussed/drafted but not confirmed enacted.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (no specific occupancy cap sourced; properties must meet lighting/ventilation/fire standards).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Income tax progressive (reports cite ~15–45%); short-term rental managers with 3+ properties trigger 13% VAT. Climate Crisis Resilience Fee €1.50–€8/night (Apr–Oct), €0.50–€2/night (Nov–Mar); detached houses >80m²: €15 (Apr–Oct) / €4 (Nov–Mar).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Mykonos identified as an 'oversaturated/red-zone' island under a draft Special Spatial Planning Framework that would freeze new STR permits; not all measures finalized as of June 2026.
jurisdictions.min_stay: No minimum-stay floor; rentals under 60 days are classified as short-term (60+ days = ordinary residential lease, no AMA needed).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Planned freeze on new permits in oversaturated islands including Mykonos; framework targets reducing permitted tourist bed capacity (drafts mention up to ~30%). Not all enacted.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — civil liability insurance from a Greece-licensed insurer is mandatory before renting (effective Oct 1, 2025).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb, Booking.com, Vrbo) must report host/property data to AADE under DAC7 (annually by Jan 31); EU Regulation 2024/1028 requires unique registration numbers on listings from May 20, 2026; platforms must delist properties whose AMA is revoked.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Must submit Short-Term Stay Declarations (by the 20th of the month following tenant departure); meet fire/electrical safety standards (electrician's declaration, fire extinguishers, smoke detectors) and hold an Energy Performance Certificate. AADE cross-checks platform data vs. tax declarations.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Core safety/insurance framework (Law 5170/2025) effective Oct 1, 2025; climate fee structure from Jan 1, 2025/2026; EU registration number requirement from May 20, 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Mykonos is among islands flagged for a proposed new-permit freeze; an additional €20/person cruise (day-visitor) tax applies to Mykonos. Regulation is national (AADE) with island-specific overlays.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines €5,000–€20,000 for non-registration/non-compliance (doubling for second offense within a year, quadrupling for third); revocation of AMA and automatic delisting. In restricted zones, fine equal to 50% of income with a €20,000 minimum.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Greek STR revenues reached ~€973M in 2025. Restrictions emphasize geographic targeting over blanket bans; Athens districts 1–3 freeze extended through 2026 and Thessaloniki's 1st municipal community freeze begins 2026.
jurisdictions.source: AADE (aade.gr/en/short-term-rental); GTP Headlines (news.gtp.gr, Sept 2025 & Jan 2026); Travel And Tour World (Apr 2026); buygreece.us (May 2026); costaluzlawyers.com; bnbnews.gr

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — hosts must register in the State Tourism Registry (RETUR-Q) and obtain a state operating license via the Quintana Roo Tax Administration Service (SATQ); registration is per property.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — hosts must register for an RFC (Federal Taxpayers Registry) with SAT; providing the RFC to platforms secures the lowest withholding rates.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown for exact RETUR-Q/license fees. Non-registration penalties up to 100,000 pesos.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — secondary homes and pure investment properties may operate; the framework treats all owners equally regardless of residency.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None — Quintana Roo imposes no statewide minimum-stay requirement or maximum nights-per-year cap as of early 2026 (HOA rules may apply).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: State Lodging Tax (ISH) reported at 5–6% (some sources cite 3–4%); plus federal ISR (income tax, up to ~35% for non-residents/25% flat withholding) and 16% IVA on furnished rentals. Airbnb auto-collects/remits ISH in Quintana Roo.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Verify property is in an area where STRs are permitted; new 2025 Tourism Law empowers municipalities (including Tulum) to set their own licensing rules, zones, and fees — local Tulum guidelines may still be developing.
jurisdictions.min_stay: No statewide minimum; market practice varies (many 2-night minimums; a segment at 30+ nights for digital nomads).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Unknown (no enacted density cap; municipality-level rules pending under 2025 law).
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown (no explicit STR insurance mandate sourced; Civil Protection safety guidelines apply). Typical home policy ~0.2–0.6% of replacement value.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb, Booking.com) began requiring Quintana Roo hosts to register with RETUR-Q from Nov 2025; platforms collect/remit ISH automatically; non-compliant listings may be delisted; cooperation with state tax authority to ensure tax collection.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Comply with Civil Protection safety standards; declare full income to SAT and pay ISR; display registration on listings. Treated as commercial activity under Mexican law.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Revamped Quintana Roo Tourism Law effective August 2025; RETUR-Q registration mandatory/strictly enforced from late 2025.
jurisdictions.key_notes: RETUR-Q registration is the single most important requirement; ~24,000 active STR units statewide with Tulum a hotspot (~8,000+ Tulum listings). Foreigners in the coastal restricted zone must hold property via fideicomiso.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to 100,000 pesos (~$5,500–$5,900 USD) for failure to register; possible delisting and closure of operations.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Regulatory power devolved to municipalities under the 2025 law, so each municipality (Tulum, Cancún/Benito Juárez, Playa del Carmen/Solidaridad, etc.) is to craft its own licensing rules, safety standards and fees — landscape may fragment over 2026.
jurisdictions.source: Quintana Roo Tourism Law via mayamirealestatemx.com, mexlaw.com, thelatinvestor.com (Tulum, Jan–Apr 2026); airbtics.com; gettransfer.com; airbnb.com/help/article/2834

jurisdictions.license_required: No STR-specific license; hosts operating as a business should obtain a municipal operating license (licencia de funcionamiento) from the Los Cabos Treasury and register for the state lodging tax.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register for an RFC with SAT and register for the BCS state lodging tax; standard SAT business/tax compliance applies.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown for exact municipal license fee (documents gathered via Los Cabos Treasury office).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — owners of secondary homes and investment properties can legally operate STRs; no residency requirement.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None sourced (no municipal night cap published as of H1 2026).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: BCS state lodging tax (ISH) 4% on lodging (raised from 3%); Airbnb is not exempt and auto-adds/remits ISH for Baja California Sur. Plus federal ISR and 16% IVA on furnished rentals.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: No officially published neighborhood bans or municipal restricted zones as of H1 2026; restrictions are building-by-building (HOA rules), e.g., Pedregal and certain beachfront complexes.
jurisdictions.min_stay: No municipal minimum; HOA/condo rules may set minimum stays.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb automatically adds and remits the BCS lodging tax to the state; platforms must withhold ISR/IVA and report under SAT digital-platform rules.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Provide RFC to Airbnb to obtain lowest withholding; file monthly VAT and monthly/annual ISR; issue facturas; comply with HOA policies. Enforcement has focused more on tax collection than shutting down individual listings.
jurisdictions.effective_date: BCS 4% lodging tax increase approved (1% rise from prior 3%); Airbnb ruled non-exempt. Exact enforcement timing not precisely dated in sources.
