jurisdictions.key_notes: No special STR license exists at municipal level; main constraints are HOA rules and tax compliance. Separate BCS state 'Embrace It' tourist fee (~488 pesos/~US$28) for foreign arrivals staying 24h+, plus a 4% lodging tax funding the Los Cabos Tourism Board.
jurisdictions.penalties: Penalties from SAT for tax non-compliance (back taxes, fines); no municipal STR-specific penalty schedule sourced.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Coastal restricted zone — foreigners hold property via fideicomiso. ~4,877 active listings tracked; ~57% average occupancy; ~93% of guests are international. Federal cruise tax rising (US$5 now → $10 in July 2026).
jurisdictions.source: thelatinvestor.com (Cabo San Lucas, Apr 2026); thecabosun.com; Mexico News Daily (Feb 2026); airbnb.com/help/article/2834; airbtics.com (Los Cabos, Dec 2025)

jurisdictions.license_required: No STR-specific state license historically; a new municipal business-license/registration requirement for STR platforms is planned under the 2026 income law (proposal to city council Aug 27, 2025) — hosts may need to register with the city and pay an annual business license fee.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — RFC with SAT; standard SAT tax obligations; Jalisco state lodging tax applies via platforms.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual municipal business license fee anticipated under 2026 framework (amount Unknown).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None sourced; Jalisco legislative proposals have discussed a possible 180-night cap (Mexico City-style) but not enacted.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Jalisco state Lodging Tax (ISH) — Airbnb help page states 5% of listing price (incl. cleaning fees) for Jalisco; historically 3% when introduced in 2018. Puerto Vallarta municipal lodging tax rose to 5% from Jan 2026 (third consecutive annual increase, up from 4%). Plus federal ISR and 16% IVA on furnished rentals.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unknown (no published neighborhood bans for Puerto Vallarta; HOA rules may apply).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb collects/remits the Jalisco lodging tax on bookings and withholds ISR/IVA under SAT digital-platform rules; the proposed municipal tax would target hosting platforms directly.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Provide RFC to Airbnb for lowest withholding; declare income to SAT; issue facturas. New municipal scheme would require city registration and annual license fee; non-compliance could lead to fines or restrictions.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Municipal lodging tax 5% from Jan 2026; proposed platform/business tax to take effect in 2026 if approved into the city's income law.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Note conflicting lodging-tax figures: Airbnb cites 5% Jalisco state lodging tax while Puerto Vallarta's municipal lodging tax also rose to 5% in 2026 — these are framed as overlapping state/municipal levies in differing sources.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines or operating restrictions for non-compliance under the proposed municipal framework (specifics Unknown); SAT penalties for tax non-compliance.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Coastal restricted zone — foreigners hold property via fideicomiso. Puerto Vallarta is moving to tax STRs like hotels for competitive fairness; tax burden on guests/hosts rising year-over-year.
jurisdictions.source: vallartadaily.com (lodging tax 2026, Nov 2025); williamhutt.mx/BeachPleasePVR (Aug 2025); airbnb.com/help/article/2288; thelatinvestor.com (Guadalajara/Jalisco, Apr 2026)

jurisdictions.license_required: No statewide license; hosts/platforms responsible for the levy must register with the State Revenue Office (SRO) short stay levy system. Some local councils operate separate registration/permit schemes (distinct from SRO).
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — for direct bookings, the owner must register for the short stay levy with the SRO and lodge returns; platforms register separately and need not register individual properties.
jurisdictions.fees: No registration fee documented; the cost is the 7.5% levy itself. Local council registration/permit fees may apply (amounts vary; Unknown).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — but the owner's/renter's principal place of residence is exempt from the levy (i.e., levy targets non-primary-residence short stays).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No statewide night cap as of 2025–2026 (proposed but not enacted); individual councils may introduce their own night limits, typically targeting unhosted investment properties.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (no statewide occupancy cap; set by planning/local laws if any).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 7.5% Short Stay Levy on the total booking fee (including accommodation charge, cleaning fees, GST and the levy itself), excluding credit card fees. Applies to stays under 28 consecutive days.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Councils may regulate short stays through planning and local laws; owners corporations (OCs) can ban short-stay use of lots via a special resolution (75% owner approval).
jurisdictions.min_stay: Levy applies to stays of less than 28 consecutive days (28+ days not subject to the levy and falls outside the short-stay definition).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Unknown statewide; left to local councils.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown (no statewide STR insurance mandate sourced).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb, Stayz, Booking.com) must register for the levy and collect/remit it on all bookings made through their platform; platforms do not register individual properties. Airbnb auto-collects/remits since Jan 1, 2025.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Lodgement frequency depends on total booking fees: quarterly if $75,000+ annually, otherwise annual; if annual lodgers exceed $75,000 they must switch to quarterly. Direct bookings = host self-lodges via SRO portal.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Short Stay Levy Act 2024 — levy effective 1 January 2025.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Short stay defined as less than 28 consecutive days where guests do not live there. Existing council-specific STR charges removed once state levy took effect. Revenue funds Homes Victoria (social/affordable housing, 25% to regional Victoria).
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown for specific levy penalty amounts (standard SRO non-lodgement/non-payment penalties and interest apply).
jurisdictions.additional_context: Victoria has ~48,000–63,000 STR properties (much of it entire homes). The Victorian Liberal opposition pledged to repeal the levy if it wins the 2026 state election. Other states/territories (Tasmania 5%, ACT 5% from July 2025) followed Victoria's model.
jurisdictions.source: Victorian State Revenue Office (sro.vic.gov.au, updated Jan 2026); Parliament of Victoria; Airbnb (help/article/3819); hostaway.com; lodgify.com; mlslegal.com.au (Jan 2026)

jurisdictions.license_required: No dedicated STR licence; property must be in the correct council rates category (short-term accommodation), which serves as the annual rental accommodation classification. Development approval (Material Change of Use) may be required for unhosted properties in residential zones.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: No state STR levy. All income must be declared to the ATO; platforms auto-report via the Sharing Economy Reporting Regime (SERR). GST registration only if turnover exceeds AUD $75,000 and supplying commercial-residential premises.
jurisdictions.fees: No mandatory STR registration fee. MCU development application fees apply where approval is needed. Unknown exact figures.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No. Both hosted and unhosted (whole-home) STRs permitted. Owner-occupiers living on-site hosting four or fewer guests are generally exempt from formal planning approval.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None. Operation permitted 365 days per year with no citywide night cap.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Owner-occupier home-based business exemption applies for four or fewer guests; otherwise Unknown / set via planning conditions.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: No state short-stay levy (unlike Victoria's 7.5%). GST 10% only applies to commercial-residential supply above threshold; standard residential STRs are input-taxed.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: In low, medium and high-density residential zones, short-term accommodation is typically assessable, requiring an MCU application. Code-assessable applications take ~35 business days; impact-assessable involve public notification and can take months. 'Party house' controls apply in mapped areas.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Short-term accommodation defined as paid stays under three months (less than 3 consecutive months).
jurisdictions.density_rules: No citywide density caps. Body corporates may restrict rentals shorter than 30 days through by-laws (per 2020 Qld court decision), depending on the governing strata Act.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: $10M public liability insurance recommended/expected for hosts.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: No current platform delisting mandate; platforms auto-report income via SERR. A proposed statewide registration scheme could require platforms to list only registered properties (not yet enacted).
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Queensland smoke alarm laws require interconnected photoelectric alarms in every bedroom, hallway and on each storey (all dwellings must comply by 1 January 2027). Pool safety certificates required if a pool/spa is present.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ongoing; framework under Planning Act 2016 / Planning Regulation 2017 and Gold Coast City Plan. Smoke alarm deadline 1 January 2027.
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of Australia's most STR-friendly markets — no night cap, no state levy, no mandatory registration. Distinction between hosted (generally exempt) and unhosted (likely needs MCU) stays is the key compliance factor. As of May 2024, 11,430 STR properties were voluntarily registered.
jurisdictions.penalties: Councils can issue compliance notices, infringements and prosecute local-law breaches, often up to 50 penalty units (~AUD $8,345 at 2025–26 rates). Operating without required DA is a development offence under the Planning Act 2016.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Queensland Government is weighing a unified STR registration scheme and standardised code of conduct, but it is not yet enacted. Brisbane proposed a permit scheme that the Lord Mayor announced in May 2026 will not proceed.
jurisdictions.source: Gold Coast City Council (goldcoast.qld.gov.au); Houst Gold Coast guide; Houst Queensland guide; Switch Solutions state-by-state guide; Hometime Australia guide (2026)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — two-tier: a City of Vancouver Short-Term Rental Business Licence AND a B.C. Provincial Short-Term Rental Registration number. Both numbers must be displayed on all listings.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes for taxes — B.C. provincial accommodation levy (3%) and Vancouver hotel/MRDT tax (2%) apply; federal GST (5%) applies if annual revenue exceeds CAD $30,000. Platforms collect and remit provincial/city taxes automatically.
jurisdictions.fees: City business licence ~CAD $1,108/year (prorated first year) plus provincial registration ~CAD $77/year (combined ~CAD $1,185). CAD $111 late penalty for city renewals after Dec 31.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes. STRs are only permitted in the operator's principal residence (the home they live in most of the year). Investment/second properties do not qualify. Secondary/laneway/basement suites only if the operator lives in that specific unit.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No nightly cap when operating from principal residence; STR defined as stays under 90 consecutive days.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (governed by building/safety standards rather than a stated guest cap).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Provincial accommodation levy 3% + Vancouver hotel tax 2%; federal GST 5% above CAD $30,000 revenue.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Principal-residence-only model; cannot rent both main house and a secondary suite. Strata corporation written approval required before applying (most multi-unit properties are stratified).
jurisdictions.min_stay: No stated minimum; short-term defined as under 90 consecutive days (changed from under 30 days in October 2024 to align with provincial law).
jurisdictions.density_rules: No explicit density quota, but principal-residence rule effectively limits one STR per host. Strata bylaws may further restrict or prohibit STRs.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Hosts must confirm insurance allows STR use and provides adequate coverage; review policies before listing.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms are legally required to share monthly listing data with local governments and must remove/delist listings lacking a valid provincial registration number at the local government's request.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Must confirm legal dwelling unit, provide 24/7 guest contact, install interconnected smoke alarms (every floor and bedroom), CO detectors where gas appliances exist, fire extinguisher on each floor, posted fire plan, and complete a fire safety self-certification. Renew both licences annually by Dec 31.
jurisdictions.effective_date: City bylaw aligned with B.C. Short-Term Rental Accommodations Act; 90-day definition effective October 2024.
jurisdictions.key_notes: As of April 2026, ~4,969 active listings with 3,248 city business licences issued for 2026 and 69 suspended. Enforcement is active and data-driven. By-law 4450 governs licensing.
jurisdictions.penalties: City non-compliance fines up to CAD $1,000 per offence; operating without a city licence ~CAD $1,000/day; operating without provincial registration ~CAD $5,000/day under provincial enforcement. Renting a non-principal residence triggers licence revocation. Maximum municipal ticketing fines raised to $3,000 per infraction per day; regional district bylaw prosecution max raised to $50,000.
jurisdictions.additional_context: STRs permitted year-round provided all requirements are met; the principal-residence rule is intended to protect long-term housing supply.
jurisdictions.source: City of Vancouver (vancouver.ca); Province of British Columbia (gov.bc.ca); liv.rent (2026); HostStarter (2026)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — provincial CITQ (Corporation de l'industrie touristique du Québec) registration certificate/number required for all stays of 31 days or less, PLUS a Montreal municipal host permit. CITQ number must be displayed in all listings.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — lodging tax (3.5%) on stays of 31 nights or fewer (Airbnb collects/remits for platform bookings; direct bookings require registration with Revenu Québec). GST (5%) and QST (~9.975%) required if earnings exceed $30,000/year.
jurisdictions.fees: Montreal host permit ~CAD $300. CITQ annual fees (2026): ~$54/year for a primary-residence establishment; ~$156/year for a general tourist accommodation establishment.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes, under the general municipal rules — only the principal residence may be rented short-term in most boroughs. Non-primary (commercial) properties require an authorized commercial zone and a borough certificate of occupancy.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Short-term rental of a principal residence in residential areas is permitted only during a seasonal window of June 10 to September 10 each year (~92 days) in most boroughs.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown / set by CITQ classification and safety standards.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Lodging tax 3.5%; GST 5% + QST ~9.975% above $30,000 annual revenue.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Borough-specific. Using a main residence for tourist rentals is prohibited in Lachine, Saint-Laurent and Saint-Léonard. Commercial STRs require authorized commercial zones. Central tourist boroughs (e.g., Ville-Marie/Old Montreal) have stricter rules; Old Montreal limited to the June 10–Sept 10 window.
jurisdictions.min_stay: No minimum; tourist accommodation = stays of 31 days or less (rentals over 31 days fall under Tribunal administratif du logement rules).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Controlled via borough zoning designating permitted vs restricted STR zones rather than explicit density caps. Condo declarations/bylaws may also restrict.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — CITQ registration requires proof of civil liability insurance ($2,000,000 per event is the provincial standard).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must verify a valid CITQ registration certificate before displaying a listing and check its validity; the registration number must appear in all advertising.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Must display CITQ certificate at the building's main entrance. Montreal permit application is online, requires identity verification at an Accès Montréal office, photo ID and recent provincial Notice of Assessment, plus a signed notice of compliance used for the CITQ application. Update statements required within 30 days of changes.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Provincial framework under Bill 25 (effective Sept 1, 2023) / Tourist Accommodation Act; current Montreal seasonal-window bylaw in force for 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Dual provincial + municipal compliance makes Montreal one of Canada's more complex STR markets. City has more than doubled inspectors and uses 311/online public reporting for enforcement.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without CITQ classification: fines $2,500–$25,000 first offence and $5,000–$50,000 for repeat offences (some sources cite individual fines starting at $5,000). Fines up to $50,000 for violations; persistent offenders face legal injunctions and permanent CITQ suspension.
jurisdictions.additional_context: The room rental of a principal residence is subject to the same rules as renting the whole principal residence. Quebec City and other municipalities follow the same provincial CITQ system with their own zoning.
jurisdictions.source: Ville de Montréal (montreal.ca); CITQ (citq.qc.ca); Lodgify Montreal/Quebec guides (2026); LendCity Canada guide (2026)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — District of Tofino Business Licence AND a B.C. Provincial Short-Term Rental Registration number. Both numbers must be displayed on all online listings.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — PST (8%) and MRDT (3%) registration required via BC Ministry of Finance; federal GST (5%) required if annual revenue exceeds CAD $30,000. Platforms collect and remit standard sales/lodging taxes automatically.
jurisdictions.fees: District business licence fee varies by classification and bedroom count (typically ranges from CAD $250 to CAD $1,050/year) plus BC provincial registration fee (~CAD $77/year).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — Tofino adopted the provincial principal residence requirement. STRs are generally restricted to the host's primary residence (the home they live in for the majority of the year) plus one secondary suite or accessory dwelling unit on the same property.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Maximum of 3 bedrooms for guests and up to 6 overnight guests total.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: PST 8% + MRDT 3% (additional local room tax); federal GST 5% above CAD $30,000 revenue.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed only in specific zones under Tofino Zoning Bylaw No. 770 where short-term rentals are explicitly permitted (typically R1, R4, RA1, RA2, RA3, A1, A2, A3, A4, and designated CD-EL or condo zones like Fred Tibbs). Prohibited in strata buildings that ban STRs.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Short-term rental defined as stays under 90 consecutive days (aligned with provincial law).
jurisdictions.density_rules: No explicit density spacing rule, but principal residence rules and zoning classifications restrict operations to one site per operator.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of commercial general liability insurance is required as part of the business licence application.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: License and provincial registration numbers must be shown on all online listings (Airbnb, Vrbo, etc.). Platforms share monthly data with the province/district and must remove non-compliant listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Hosts must sign a 'Good Neighbour Agreement', provide a detailed floor plan showing escape routes, and designate a local contact manager who is available 24/7 and can respond to the property within 2 hours.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Provincial principal residence requirements adopted in Tofino effective November 1, 2024; updated licensing bylaws apply for 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Business licences expire annually on December 31 and must be renewed. Non-compliant STRs are ineligible for federal income tax deductions on rental expenses.
jurisdictions.penalties: Municipal fines up to CAD $1,000 per offence per day under District bylaws; provincial non-compliance fines up to CAD $5,000 per day; potential licence revocation and listing removal.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Strata corporations may ban or heavily restrict STRs. Tofino actively monitors listings via automated scraping software to enforce compliance.
jurisdictions.source: District of Tofino (tofino.ca); Province of British Columbia (gov.bc.ca); bnbcalc.com Tofino guide; liftylife.ca Tofino regulations

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — mandatory registration in the Registro Nacional de Turismo (RNT) via Confecámaras/Chamber of Commerce for all short-term tourist rentals (under 30 days). For island/Barú-area establishments in the Cartagena district, the Chamber of Commerce verifies land use with the District Mayor's Office.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — RUT (tax registry) required; merchants must be registered with the local Chamber of Commerce; ongoing local ICA (industry & commerce) tax filings via Cartagena's municipal portal; parafiscal contribution to FONTUR (a percentage of gross income).
jurisdictions.fees: RNT registration itself is free (no significant upfront fee) but must be renewed annually; budget for ongoing local ICA tax obligations and FONTUR parafiscal contributions.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — Colombia uses a registration-based (not residency-based) framework. Secondary homes and investment properties may operate as STRs once RNT-registered.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No national day cap; short-term rentals are those under 30 days. Building horizontal-property rules cannot restrict stays over 30 days.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (room/bed capacity declared in RNT; no stated national guest cap).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown specific rate (subject to local ICA tax, FONTUR parafiscal contribution, and applicable national taxes). Exact figures Unknown.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Building 'propiedad horizontal' (horizontal property) regulations can restrict or fully prohibit STRs unless daily rentals are expressly authorized in the public deed (escritura/Reglamento de Propiedad Horizontal). Common friction in Centro Histórico, Getsemaní and Bocagrande. Island areas require land-use verification.
jurisdictions.min_stay: No minimum; tourism rentals defined as stays under 30 days.
jurisdictions.density_rules: No national density cap; no legal limit on number of properties one operator can list (each must be individually registered). Building-level/HOA restrictions effectively limit supply in some complexes.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — since January 2022, all short-term rental operators must carry civil liability insurance covering guest damages/accidents.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb, Booking.com) must verify RNT registration and only list properties with a valid RNT number; hosts without an RNT number cannot list (enforced since Aug 23, 2022). Platforms may face penalties for non-compliant listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Guest registration mandatory via the Tarjeta de Registro de Alojamiento (TRA); SIRE/TRA reporting required. Hosts must accept a code of conduct including child-protection commitments. Comply with health, hygiene and safety standards.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Framework under Law 300 of 1996, Law 2068 of 2020, Decree 1074 of 2015 and Decree 1836 of 2021; platform RNT enforcement from August 23, 2022; stricter RNT verification introduced late 2025.
jurisdictions.key_notes: RNT renewal window is annual, January 1–March 31; late renewal results in automatic suspension. Cartagena had ~16,300 active listings as of first-half 2026. Cartagena district registrations may take up to 15 business days vs ~5 elsewhere.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without TRA/SIRE registration can lead to fines up to 300 million COP (~$80,000 USD) and RNT suspension for five years. SIC enforcement, platform delisting and possible closure orders for non-compliance. Decree 2438 of 2015 also imposes sanctions for failure to register or providing false information.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Stricter RNT requirements introduced in late 2025 require platforms to verify host registration, potentially slowing market growth in 2026. No official government 'no-Airbnb zones,' but building-level bans exist in popular tourist neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.source: Airbnb Help (Colombia/Cartagena, Law 2068 & Decree 1836); TheLatinvestor Cartagena/Colombia (2026); IR Global; uhabogados; LodgeCompliance Colombia

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — hosts generally need a BMC (Brihan Mumbai Municipal Corporation) trade/lodging license under the Mumbai Municipal Corporation Act, 1888 for B&Bs/lodging houses, and registration under the Maharashtra Shops and Establishments Act (registration/renewal done online via the BMC website). A Housing Society NOC is also required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — STR income is taxable; GST registration required if annual turnover exceeds Rs. 20 lakhs. Property tax may be reassessed for STR use.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown specific BMC license fee amounts.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No explicit primary-residence requirement, but cooperative housing society (CHS) bylaws frequently restrict or ban short-term rentals, and a society NOC is mandatory.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No statutory day cap. Stays typically range from one night to under three months; no nationwide maximum-stay rule.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: GST applies above Rs. 20 lakh turnover (rate Unknown/varies). Property used for STR may incur a tax rate between residential and commercial rates.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Cooperative housing societies can lawfully restrict/prohibit STRs (upheld by Bombay High Court, Dec 20, 2024, under the Maharashtra Co-operative Societies Act). Residential vs commercial property designation affects permitted use.
jurisdictions.min_stay: No statutory minimum; popular stays are 4–7 days for business/trade travelers.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Unknown / governed by individual housing society bylaws rather than municipal density caps.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Listings managed via platforms (Airbnb, OYO, StayVista); hosts must hold requisite registrations. A proposed BMC Short-Term Rental Licensing Policy would formalize platform-listing licensing requirements (not yet fully enacted).
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: For foreign guests, hosts must report guest details to local police / FRRO within 24 hours of check-in (Form C via FRRO portal). Society NOC and police intimation about guests are mandatory. Health/safety compliance overseen by the Executive Health Officer.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Existing licensing under MMC Act 1888 and Maharashtra Shops & Establishments Act; BMC/MCGM Short-Term Rental Licensing Policy is pending/under development.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Bombay High Court (Dec 2024) upheld housing societies' authority to ban STRs and ruled operating without a valid B&B license unlawful. Proposed BMC STR policy envisages 10-year licenses with penalties of 50%–100% of revenue for non-compliance.
jurisdictions.penalties: Under the proposed BMC policy, non-compliance penalties would range from 50% to 100% of revenue. Operating without a valid license has been ruled unlawful by the Bombay High Court.
jurisdictions.additional_context: India has no national STR law; legality depends on four layers — state tourism rules, municipal (BMC) rules, housing society bylaws, and lease subletting clauses. Maharashtra operates a voluntary Bed & Breakfast scheme via MTDC.
jurisdictions.source: Airbnb Help Center (Maharashtra); MCGM/BMC Short Term Rental Licensing Policy (portal.mcgm.gov.in); Real Estate Law Journal (Bombay HC, Dec 2024); ownpropertyabroad (2026); Airbtics

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes. Either a standard minpaku notification (届出) under the Private Lodging Business Act, a tokku (special zone) minpaku certification, or a hotel/ryokan license. As of ~October 2025, Osaka City suspended acceptance of NEW special-zone (tokku) minpaku applications.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes. Rental income is taxable and must be declared; operators must register with local government.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown (registration/notification and fire-safety compliance costs apply; varies by scheme)
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Standard minpaku: 180 nights/year (counted April 1–March 31). Tokku (special zone) minpaku: no annual cap (365-day operation) but new applications suspended.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Minimum floor area of 3.3 m² per guest under the Minpaku Law
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown (national income tax on rental income; Osaka also levies an accommodation tax). Exact STR-specific rate not sourced.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Minpaku permitted in all 24 Osaka City wards; tokku scheme historically concentrated heavily in Osaka (~90% of national total). Local ordinances and buffer zones near schools/hospitals may apply.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Tokku (special zone) minpaku: minimum 2 nights (max 9). Standard minpaku: no national minimum.
jurisdictions.density_rules: City froze new tokku applications in late 2025 partly due to nuisance/violation density (e.g., 17 facilities packed into a ~500 m² area of Naniwa Ward cited).
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Recommended/advisable; not explicitly confirmed as mandatory in sourced material
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Operators must hold a registration number; unlicensed operation is enforced. Platform-specific listing obligations not detailed in sources.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Owner-absent properties (or guest area >50 m²) require a registered third-party management company; neighbor notification required; fire-safety and hygiene standards apply. Foreign owners typically use management companies (15–20%+ fees).
jurisdictions.effective_date: Minpaku Law effective June 2018; tokku scheme since 2016; Osaka City new-application freeze announced ~October 2025.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Osaka Governor Yoshimura announced intent to end the tokku minpaku program entirely, citing sufficient hotel supply and community backlash; existing tokku licenses becoming more valuable. ~99% of condo bylaws nationally prohibit STR regardless of permits.
jurisdictions.penalties: Unlicensed operation faces fines up to ¥1 million; possible revocation of registration; local governments conduct on-site inspections.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Osaka received ~400 complaints in FY2024 (noise, garbage, illegal STR). 'Yami minpaku' (black-market rentals) remain a concern. Japan had ~30,318 registered minpaku units as of March 2025.
jurisdictions.source: e-housing.jp; japan-realestate-analytics.com; akiyajapan.com; realgaijin.substack.com; aiaig.com; mailmate.jp

jurisdictions.license_required: No mandatory national STR license currently in force. A proposed national Short-Term Residential Accommodation (STRA) framework would require business licenses from local councils, but as of early 2026 draft guidelines are not yet finalized or enforceable.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (income tax). Rental income is taxable; hosts declare in annual personal/corporate income tax return.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown (no national fee yet; proposed council business-licensing fees pending)
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None (no national day cap; building bylaws may restrict)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Individual: progressive personal income tax up to 30% on net income. Tourism tax framework applies (hosts no longer collect tourism tax directly). Local Assessment Tax and Quit Rent may apply.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STR may be treated as commercial use under the National Land Code and local zoning; running STR may be deemed a 'material change of use' requiring planning permission depending on the council.
jurisdictions.density_rules: None at national level; building-level (strata) rules govern
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown (proposed STRA/Tourism Industry Act amendments expected to cover insurance)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb maintains a voluntary Code of Conduct for Malaysia STRA. Proposed amendments to Tourism Industry Act 1992 would add platform registration and data-sharing obligations.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Strata by-laws are decisive: Management Corporations/Joint Management Bodies can prohibit STR via house rules. Federal Court (Innab Salil v Verve Suites) upheld such bans; a 2025 Court of Appeal ruling clarified that statutory powers do not automatically permit blanket bans, but sufficiently supported by-laws can still prohibit STR.
jurisdictions.effective_date: No national STR law yet in force; STRA framework first announced 2024, draft presented to Cabinet 2025; Tourism Industry Act amendments expected late 2025–early 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: No specific law outright bans Airbnb in KL; market operates in a transitional, mixed regulatory environment governed by local councils and strata bylaws. AirROI classifies KL regulation level as 'Low'.
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown (no national STR penalty regime yet; planned Tourism Tribunal/court for disputes)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Airbnb reported RM11 billion guest spending in Malaysia in 2024 (~RM9.2bil GDP contribution, ~93,600 jobs). Foreigners can own strata property generally above ~RM1,000,000 in KL. ~1,600–22,000 active KL listings depending on dataset.
jurisdictions.source: iproperty.com.my; boongiap.com.my; rdslawpartners.com; nst.com.my; airbnb.com/help; airroi.com; goikrameshloo.com

jurisdictions.license_required: No. Formal STR licenses are not required nationally. Hosts may be subject to local zoning and may register with local authorities, but enforcement is light; ~0% of Cusco listings hold STR licenses.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes. Tax registration with SUNAT is expected; rental income (first-category income) is taxable.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None (no national day cap; no government-designated restricted zones)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown (first-category rental income taxation via SUNAT; exact STR rate not sourced)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Generally lenient at city level; some zoning rules may apply. Historic-center areas face stricter neighbor pressure and noise enforcement (not a formal ban). Building/HOA bylaws can prohibit STR.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Advised (liability insurance recommended, especially in multi-unit buildings); not mandatory
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Hosts must collect and submit foreign-guest information to the Migrations Office (Migraciones) via web portal.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Since December 10, 2024 (Supreme Decree N° 011-2024-IN), all lodging/leasing establishments must collect and report foreign-guest travel/identity data to immigration. Building management committees responsible for emergency/fire plans.
jurisdictions.key_notes: No officially published neighborhood bans or government-designated restricted zones for STR anywhere in Peru as of H1 2026. Building-level HOA rules are the most common restriction.
jurisdictions.penalties: DS 011-2024-IN establishes no penalties for natural persons (individuals) acting as landlords for the foreign-guest reporting requirement.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Cusco has ~4,000–7,500 active listings (varies by source). Strong seasonality tied to dry season and Inti Raymi (June). Peru has ~45,000 active Airbnb listings nationally.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes. Alojamento Local (AL) registration is mandatory to operate legally; an AL registration number is issued via the municipality through the national registry (RNAL) on the Balcão Único Eletrónico.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes. All AL operations must register with the Tax Authority (AT/Finanças); digital receipts and annual tax declarations required. Non-residents must register for VAT.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 9 rooms / 27 guests per AL; renting rooms in own home limited to 3 bedrooms. Temporary beds allowed but not exceeding 50% of permanent beds (e.g., +2 living-room guests and up to 2 extra beds for children under 12).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Income subject to AL tax treatment. CEAL extraordinary contribution (15%) was abolished retroactively to Dec 31, 2023. Tourist tax varies by municipality (e.g., Loulé €1/person/night low season, €2 high season, max 5 nights, under-16s exempt).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Property must be on urban (not rustic) land and have a building-use classification compatible with tourism accommodation. Municipalities may create 'contention'/containment zones restricting new AL.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Unknown (no national minimum for AL; typically stays under 30 days)
jurisdictions.density_rules: Local councils may designate containment zones and suspend new registrations for up to 1 year. Councils with >1,000 AL units must decide policy within 12 months. As of 2026, no binding freeze or regional containment regime enacted in the Algarve; Loulé and Lagos have monitored/discussed density in historic areas.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes. Civil liability insurance is mandatory (minimum ~€75,000 recommended); insurance expiry now published in RNAL and lack of valid insurance can cause cancellation.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: AL number required for listing on platforms (Airbnb/Booking). Foreign-guest stay declarations now handled via the SIBA platform under AIMA (following dissolution of SEF).
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Under Decree-Law No. 76/2024 (effective Nov 1, 2024): AL licenses no longer expire after 5 years; automatic expiry for inactivity abolished; licenses transferable nationwide (though some municipalities constrain transfers in containment zones); condominium approval no longer needed except for hostels. Fire safety (extinguishers, fire blankets, signage, smoke detectors) and an information book required.
jurisdictions.effective_date: AL regime since 2014; major reform Decree-Law No. 76/2024 effective November 1, 2024; municipal implementation/enforcement developments through 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Faro (regional capital) has not introduced specific AL restrictions as of 2026. Most Algarve STR concentrated in Albufeira, Loulé/Vilamoura, Portimão, Lagos, Tavira; new AL applications broadly admissible region-wide.
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown (lack of valid insurance can lead to AL cancellation; general AL non-compliance carries legal/tax consequences)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Algarve had over 40,000 registered AL units by end of 2025 (~one-third of Portugal's total AL supply).
jurisdictions.source: sovereigngroup.com; oceanhorizon.pt; exclusivealgarvevillas.com; airnest-reim.com; portugalbuyersagent.com; buypropertyinalgarve.com

jurisdictions.license_required: No single national/cantonal STR permit. Hosts must register guest stays with the Cantonal Zurich Police. A nationwide listing-registration-number requirement is expected to apply in many locations from May 2026.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes for tax purposes (income tax on rental earnings; VAT may become mandatory once activity/revenue grows).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Unknown (rule context relates to residential-zone use restrictions)
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Conflicting sources. Some report a maximum of 90 rental days/year in residential zones since January 2024 (anti-misappropriation of housing) and a revised building/zoning code restricting commercial STR in residential zones since 2024; other sources state Zurich imposes no strict annual night limit and that a 90-day cap is being pushed via a planned municipal popular initiative. Verify current city ordinance.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: City/accommodation tax in Canton of Zurich: reported as CHF 3.50 per person per night for stays of 30 nights or shorter (an earlier CHF 2.50 figure also appears in sources). Income tax levied at federal, cantonal and municipal levels (progressive).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Revised building and zoning code restricts commercial short-term rentals in residential zones (since 2024); city monitors impact on housing availability.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Unknown (no Zurich-specific minimum sourced; note Interlaken requires 5-night minimum in residential zones, not Zurich)
jurisdictions.density_rules: Measures aimed at preventing misappropriation of living space in residential zones; 90-day limit promoted as a model instrument.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb automatically collects and remits the Canton of Zurich city tax. Platforms may be required to share transaction data with Swiss authorities on request. Registration-number display expected in many Swiss locations from May 2026.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Hosts must report guest stays (especially guests with residence abroad) to the Cantonal Zurich Police; failure to report can result in administrative penalties. Tenancy law (Swiss Code of Obligations) and lease conditions apply to subletting.
jurisdictions.effective_date: City tax framework from August 1, 2018; residential-zone commercial STR restrictions / reported 90-day cap from January 2024; national registration-number requirement expected May 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Switzerland has no single national STR law; rules are layered across federal, cantonal and municipal levels. Zurich is comparatively less restrictive than Geneva (which caps at 90 days/year), but tightening is underway.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating an unlicensed/non-compliant STR in Switzerland can result in fines, mandatory closure orders, and back-payment of unpaid tourist taxes; administrative penalties for failure to report guests.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Zurich STR averages ~59% occupancy at ~CHF 140/night (early 2026). Geneva caps rentals at 90 days/year and is particularly active in enforcement.
jurisdictions.source: hostaway.com; propertyowner.ch; theupperkey.com; airbnb.com/help (Switzerland); ownpropertyabroad.com; investropa.com; lodgecompliance.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — all private residences offering tourist accommodation must register and obtain a tourist accommodation license from the Zanzibar Commission for Tourism (ZCT). Hosts also need company/business registration (ZBPRA/BRELA) and a TIN where operating commercially.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — tax registration with the Tanzania Revenue Authority (TRA) for a TIN and applicable Zanzibar/Union taxes (VAT, hotel/tourism levy).
jurisdictions.fees: Registration fee TSH 150,000 for Mjini Magharibi (Zanzibar Urban West) Region and TSH 250,000 for other regions (per 2024 regulations). Industry trackers cite broader annual ZCT tourist accommodation license fees ranging roughly TZS 500,000–2,000,000 (~$200–$800) depending on property size/type; verify directly with ZCT.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — no formal residency requirement; secondary homes and investment properties may legally operate as STRs, and entire-home listings are common in beach markets.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None — no published annual night cap; market data shows many hosts operating 271–365 nights/year.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: VAT 18% on platform service fees (collected/remitted by platforms such as Airbnb/Booking.com); a 2% Digital Service Tax applies to non-resident platforms. A specific Hotel/Tourism Levy also applies to accommodation. Exact host income tax rates: Unknown.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Generally lenient at the city level, but zoning rules may apply to some areas; Stone Town is a UNESCO World Heritage Site with heritage/cultural protections affecting restoration and use.
jurisdictions.density_rules: No published per-host property cap; however, there may be limits on the number of licenses a single host can hold, and running multiple units triggers formal business/accommodation licensing expectations.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: No — no nationwide mandate for STR liability insurance, though it is strongly recommended.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb, Booking.com, Vrbo) are required to collect and remit 18% VAT on their service fees to TRA and are subject to the 2% Digital Service Tax; they are not directly responsible for individual listing compliance and are not currently required to share host/guest data with authorities.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Process: visit local municipal office/ZCT, submit documents (proof of ownership or landlord consent letter; business license if commercial), pay fees, and pass a ZCT inspection for safety/quality standards. ZCT conducts regular inspections.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Non-hotel/private-residence registration regulations announced by the Zanzibar Minister of Tourism and Antiquities on 2024-07-04.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Regulations announced in July 2024 require all private residences used for tourist lodging—applying to both locals and diaspora—to register and license with ZCT; intended to ensure tourist safety and quality standards. Market remains operator-friendly with low observed registration compliance.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines, license revocation, or closure of the property for non-compliance; licenses can be revoked if obtained fraudulently, used for prohibited activities, or if terms are violated.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Zanzibar accounts for roughly 70% of Tanzania's STR listings; estimated ~3,500 active national listings in early 2026. Tenants must obtain written landlord consent to sublet for STR. Foreign hotel-scale investors may register with ZIPA (high capital thresholds apply to large developments, not typical single STRs).
jurisdictions.source: Zanzibar Commission for Tourism (zct.co.tz / zanzibartourism.go.tz); zanzibarluxproperties.com and ATTA (atta.travel) reporting on the July 2024 private-accommodation regulations; lodgecompliance.com (Tanzania STR guide); theafricanvestor.com (2026 market/legal analysis); AirROI (2026 data); gerpatsolutions.co.tz / rive.co.tz (licensing process).

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes for stays under 30 days — a hotel license under the Hotel Act B.E. 2547 (2004) is required to legally rent for fewer than 30 nights. Exemption: 'non-hotel accommodation' with no more than 8 rooms and no more than 30 guests is exempt from a full hotel license but must still register with the local district office. Condominium units generally do not qualify for the exemption and cannot legally be rented daily/weekly.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — rental income must be declared to the Thai Revenue Department; foreign self-managing owners typically need a Thai company and work permit. TM30 immigration registration is also mandatory.
jurisdictions.fees: Hotel license application fees are calculated by the local District/Provincial Office based on total hotel area (case-by-case). Non-hotel registration is a simpler/cheaper district-office process. Specific amounts: Unknown.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No annual cap, but the regulatory line is the 30-day stay threshold: rentals under 30 consecutive days fall under the Hotel Act; 30+ day rentals are exempt and legal without a hotel license.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Non-hotel exemption capped at 30 guests (and 8 rooms) per premise; exceeding requires a full hotel license.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown for specific rates. Rental income is subject to Thai personal/corporate income tax and withholding rules; tax evasion carries Revenue Department surcharges of 50–200% of unpaid tax plus 1.5% monthly interest.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Owners must verify that the property's location/zoning permits short-term rentals; condominium by-laws frequently prohibit short stays regardless of national law.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Effectively 30 nights for license-free operation — to operate without a hotel license, rentals of 30+ consecutive days are the safe compliant route.
jurisdictions.density_rules: The 8-room / 30-guest non-hotel threshold cannot be circumvented by splitting a building into smaller units; the law covers that loophole. Condo buildings are not treated as a single qualifying 'premise.'
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Same legal rules apply to all platforms (Airbnb, Booking.com, Agoda); platforms operate legally but listing on them does not exempt a host from the Hotel Act. No specific platform data-sharing mandate cited.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Hosts must report foreign guest details to Thai Immigration within 24 hours of arrival via TM30 (online portal/app, in person, or registered mail); failure risks THB 800–2,000 per unreported guest and can block guests' 90-day reporting/visa extensions. Self-managing owners working on-site require a work permit and a Thai limited company.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Hotel Act B.E. 2547 (2004); current non-hotel exemption thresholds (8 rooms/30 guests) effective 2023-10-29 (Ministerial Regulation No. 2 B.E. 2566), with non-hotel registration valid 5 years.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Pattaya is one of Thailand's most actively policed markets. Enforcement intensified through 2025–2026 with multi-agency (DOPA, police, immigration) raids; the Interior Ministry directed local administration in Pattaya to aggressively pursue legal action against foreign-owned condos offering daily rentals. A draft 'Accommodation Act' that could create a legal pathway for condo STRs was under Council of State consultation but, as of early 2026, had not been enacted in the Royal Gazette.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a required hotel license breaches the Hotel Act: fines around THB 20,000 plus THB 10,000/day of continued violation and up to 1 year imprisonment. Some sources cite landlord fines up to THB 100,000 and/or imprisonment. Tax penalties are separate (50–200% surcharge + 1.5% monthly interest).
jurisdictions.additional_context: Pattaya had ~9,671 Airbnb listings with ~61% average occupancy (Airbtics). Early-2026 court judgment confirmed only monthly (30+ day) rentals are exempt from the Hotel Act; condominium juristic persons have passed internal bans on daily rentals. Enforcement particularly targets foreign-owned condo portfolios rather than single private owners.
jurisdictions.source: Hotel Act B.E. 2547 and Ministerial Regulation No. 2 B.E. 2566 (2023) as analyzed by Tilleke & Gibbins, PKF Thailand, Bangkok Global Law, FRANK Legal & Tax; Formichella & Sritawat / Lexology (2026 enforcement analysis); rumavi.com (2026 STR guide); thethailandadvisor.com; pattayaprestigeproperties.com; airbtics.com (market data).

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a valid Vacation Rental License is required at all times while advertised or rented; license expires one year from initial registration and must be renewed annually.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — operators must collect and remit Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT) and Tourism Business Improvement District (TBID) monthly.
jurisdictions.fees: Reported at $599 for a two-year permit by an industry tracker; an older source cites $550. Treat exact current fee as Unknown pending city confirmation.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — primary residence not required, but an owner/entity may not hold more than two licenses (those holding more as of Sept 16, 2021 are grandfathered).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No explicit annual day cap; rentals defined as terms of 28 or fewer consecutive days.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: One person per 200 sq ft, with a maximum of 16 guests.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 13% total (TOT + TBID) of gross receipts, remitted monthly.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Eligibility tied to zoning (Ordinance 2023-518 §4.01.110): single-family units allowed in R-L, R-1, R-3 and commercial zones; properties with ADUs are not eligible; apartments (>4 units single ownership) not eligible.
jurisdictions.min_stay: No minimum stay specified; short-term defined as 28 or fewer consecutive days.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Citywide cap of 1,500 active vacation rental licenses; maximum of two licenses per owner/entity (grandfathering pre-existing holders).
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of current liability insurance (premises liability) required with application.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: License number must be included in all advertisements; license must be posted within the property. (No specific platform data-sharing mandate sourced.)
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Only properties in zip code 92315 fall under City rules; properties outside register with San Bernardino County. Requires home inspection (every two years), annual owner/agent certification via 25-question exam (100% pass), 24/7 complaint response agent, exterior informational sign, and signed Good Neighbor Policy. Off-street paved parking required for new units; gravel/no-parking grandfathering expired Jan 1, 2026.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ordinance No. 2023-518 (building on the ordinance adopted 8/16/21); fine reductions and 60-day warning system effective Jan 18, 2024.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Revisions including a new fine table, quiet-hour adjustments and trash enforcement were slated for adoption in early 2026 — verify current status.
jurisdictions.penalties: Renting/advertising without a valid license: $1,500 first violation, $2,500 second within 12 months, $5,000 third within 12 months; permit revocation possible.
jurisdictions.additional_context: License automatically expires upon sale/change of ownership; new owner must reapply. Family transfers have separate provisions.
jurisdictions.source: City of Big Bear Lake (bigbearlake.gov / citybigbearlake.com); getchalet.com; strprofitmap.com; San Bernardino County STR site (str.sbcounty.gov).

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Short-Term Rental Permit issued by San Bernardino County Land Use Services is required (Joshua Tree is unincorporated; county rules apply).
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — hosts must register for a Transient Occupancy Tax Registration Certificate and collect/remit TOT (plus TBID).
jurisdictions.fees: Annual permit fee required (county updated user fees via Resolution No. 25113 adopted May 25, 2023); exact current dollar amount Unknown. A $150 one-time credit is available for installing an outdoor noise monitoring device.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — but permits capped at two per individual/entity/trust/LLC (those with more than two are grandfathered, cannot obtain additional).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No explicit annual day cap; STR defined as stays of 30 days or less.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Based on bedrooms/square footage — sleeping areas must provide at least 70 sq ft for one person and 50 sq ft per person for more than one.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 7% TOT plus 2% TBID = 9% combined (per industry trackers); county TOT is 7%.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed in residential zones and some commercial zones where residential use is permitted; county mountain/desert area rules govern. Density caps apply in some county/neighboring jurisdictions.
jurisdictions.min_stay: No minimum stay sourced; STR defined as 30 days or less.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Neighborhood density measures and a two-permit-per-owner cap; a 45-day pause on new permits was imposed in 2022 (historical). Current density specifics: Unknown.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — liability insurance with minimum $500,000 per occurrence coverage reported by industry trackers.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Approved permit must be displayed at the property and included in all online/offline listings; hosts responsible for tax compliance even if platforms (e.g., Airbnb) facilitate collection.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Application via San Bernardino County Land Use Services (EZ Online Permitting); requires operational plan, exterior inspection, 24/7 complaint contact, on-site parking, trash service, posted permit and Good Neighbor brochure, and surrounding-owner notification waiting periods. 24/7 complaint hotline 1-833-SBC-STR1. Health/safety: smoke & CO detectors, Class 2A:10B fire extinguisher, bedroom egress.
jurisdictions.effective_date: San Bernardino County STR ordinance (Ch. 84.28); two-permit cap and other changes took effect July 28, 2022; fee resolution May 25, 2023.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Heavily oversupplied desert market; enforcement strengthened in recent years. Permit reported as roughly a two-year operating authorization requiring renewal by one source vs. annual by others — verify.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines reported up to $1,000 per day for unpermitted activity; exceeding occupancy can result in expulsion, fines, or both; permit denial/revocation for violations.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Desert water-conservation considerations; new ownership must be verified via recorded grant deed before applying.
jurisdictions.source: San Bernardino County (str.sbcounty.gov, str.sbcounty.gov/getting-started); getchalet.com; theoffersheet.com; jtgar.com; airbtics.com.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a Business Tax Certificate (BTC) and a Certified Property Authorization (and/or TOT Certificate) are required before renting transiently.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — operators must obtain a Business Tax Certificate and TOT/Certified Property authorization and remit TOT/TBID monthly.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown (Business Tax Certificate fee and certified-property fees apply; specific amounts not sourced).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No annual day cap; even one night per year of transient rental triggers TOT/BTC obligations. Transient stay = less than 31 days.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (governed by zoning/building code; not specified in sources).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 13% TOT plus 1% TBID (14% total) on total gross rents including nonrefundable fees (e.g., cleaning, pet fees).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Transient rentals limited to certain zones only — Commercial, Residential Multi-Family 2, Resort, and Specific Plan zones. Generally NOT permitted in Residential Single Family, Rural Residential, or Residential Multi-Family 1 zones.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Transient defined as less than 31 days; no separate minimum-stay rule sourced.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Unknown (controlled primarily through zoning eligibility rather than a numeric cap).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: If a reservation company collects and remits TOT/TBID on its bookings, the owner only remits on directly-handled bookings; owner remains responsible for compliance.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Renew BTC annually (calendar-year basis); TOT/TBID filed monthly (zero returns required). Use the Town's TOT Property Search Tool/Zoning Reference Guide to confirm an address is zoned for transient rentals. Illegal rental = renting in prohibited zones or without BTC/TOT certificate. Anonymous TOT complaint hotline (760) 965-3670.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Town of Mammoth Lakes Municipal Code §3.12.040 (TOT) and Title 5 Ch. 5.40; longstanding zoning restrictions (no single recent date sourced).
jurisdictions.key_notes: Single-family residential transient rentals have historically been prohibited in most residential zones; the Town has periodically studied expanding allowed areas but had not broadly legalized SFR transient rentals as of sources reviewed — verify current status.
jurisdictions.penalties: Town monitors listing platforms and imposes fines for illegal/unpermitted nightly rentals; specific fine schedule Unknown.
jurisdictions.additional_context: TOT accounts for ~60% of the Town's General Fund; payments processed via Xpressbillpay.com.
jurisdictions.source: Town of Mammoth Lakes official site (townofmammothlakes.ca.gov /503, /201; Transient Rental FAQ); destinationmammoth.com; mammothpropertymanagement.com.

jurisdictions.license_required: No dedicated STR permit/registry; a City business tax certificate is required for rental activity. Oakland has no purpose-built STR ordinance — STRs are governed by zoning and TOT rules.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — operators must register with the city's Revenue Bureau and remit Transient Occupancy Tax on stays of 30 days or less.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown (business tax certificate fees apply; no STR-specific fee).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Only relevant to the limited Bed & Breakfast exception, which requires the property to be the owner-operator's primary residence.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No day cap; instead, rentals under 30 consecutive days are generally prohibited outside narrow exceptions.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Bed & Breakfast exception: no more than two bedrooms for transient occupancy; otherwise governed by building/fire code. General STR occupancy limit: Unknown.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 14% Transient Occupancy Tax on stays of 30 days or less (one of the highest in California). Separate 18.5% parking tax if parking charged separately.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Planning Code (Title 17) defines occupancy of 30+ days as 'Permanent Residential Activity'; rentals under 30 days are prohibited in most residential zones unless they qualify as a 'Bed and Breakfast Residential Activity.' ADUs/secondary units cannot be rented under 30 days (Planning Code §17.103.080). Downtown/commercial zones may allow shorter stays with a Conditional Use Permit.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Effectively 30 consecutive days minimum in most residential zones; some sources note 7-day-or-less rentals prohibited as 'transient habitation.'
jurisdictions.density_rules: Unknown (no numeric STR cap; restriction is via zoning).
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Hosts must comply with health, safety and insurance requirements per general guidance; no specific STR insurance mandate sourced (treat as Unknown/standard).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: City pushes platforms to comply with zoning and assist enforcement against illegal listings; Airbnb may collect/remit TOT but hosts remain responsible.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Bed & Breakfast exception is extremely limited (owner-occupied, ≤2 transient bedrooms, single stays not exceeding ~15 consecutive days). Must maintain guest records (≥2 years), functioning smoke/CO detectors, and meet California Building Code/habitability standards. Good Neighbor expectations on noise/parking.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Existing zoning (Planning Code Title 17) and TOT (Municipal Code Ch. 4.24) govern; no dedicated STR ordinance adopted as of mid-2026. New regulations reportedly in development.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Among the most restrictive in California; sub-30-day rentals are effectively banned in most residential areas. New STR rules were reportedly being prepared — verify current status.
jurisdictions.penalties: Non-compliance may result in fines, back taxes, interest, and penalties; operating unlicensed/illegal rentals violates city ordinances.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Properties outside Oakland fall under separate Alameda County rules.
jurisdictions.source: City of Oakland (oaklandca.gov; Municode Ch. 4.24 & Title 17); steadily.com; proper.insure; rentbumper.com; bnbcalc.com.

jurisdictions.license_required: No special STR permit or public registry currently required; hosts must register their business (Business Tax Certificate) and register/identify the STR per Municipal Code Title 20.80 (Part 2.5). (Sources differ on whether a 'permit/registration number' must appear on listings.)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — hosts must obtain a Business Tax Certificate and collect/remit Transient Occupancy Tax for stays of 30 days or less.
jurisdictions.fees: Business tax certificate fees apply (city business tax); exact STR-specific fee Unknown.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Some sources indicate the property must be the host's primary residence with at least 60 consecutive days of owner occupancy annually; the city ordinance basis varies by source — verify in Title 20.80.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 180 days per calendar year when the host is NOT present; no cap when the host is present during stays.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Code max of 10 transient occupants overall. When host present: up to 3 guests in single-family/mobile home (2 for multi-family per some sources). When host absent: max 2 in a studio, 3 in a one-bedroom, plus 2 per additional bedroom. (Sources conflict — confirm in Title 20.80 Part 2.5.)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 10% city TOT (some sources note an additional Santa Clara County component); Airbnb/Vrbo generally collect and remit.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed in single-family homes, two-family homes, multi-family dwellings, mobile homes, live/work units, and guest houses; NOT permitted in Accessory Dwelling Units (ADUs). Governed by Zoning Code Title 20.
jurisdictions.min_stay: No minimum stay specified; STR/TOT applies to stays of 30 days or less.
jurisdictions.density_rules: No numeric density cap sourced (limits are via the 180-day unhosted cap and occupancy rules).
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Not mandated by city ordinance (recommended best practice).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb collects and remits TOT on hosts' behalf in San José; hosts remain responsible for confirming remittance. Listings/registration handled per city rules; if host absent, written neighbor notification with local contact required.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Must designate a local contact able to respond quickly to complaints (often cited as within ~1 hour); keep records for ~3 years after each transient occupancy period (TOT payment and compliance documentation), available to city manager/attorney/auditor. Meet building/housing/fire safety codes (smoke/CO detectors, egress) and parking standards. HOAs/CC&Rs/leases may further restrict.
jurisdictions.effective_date: San José Municipal Code Title 20, Section 20.80 (Part 2.5) — Transient Occupancy in Residential Use; no single recent adoption date sourced.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Relatively permissive among Bay Area cities; primary-residence and exact occupancy details are reported inconsistently across sources and should be confirmed against Title 20.80 Part 2.5.
jurisdictions.penalties: Possible fines, administrative action, or platform delisting; tax delays incur escalating penalties. Enforcement is largely complaint-driven.
jurisdictions.additional_context: City regulations take precedence over Santa Clara County rules within city limits. State STR laws (AB 537 fee transparency, SB 644 cancellation) also apply.
jurisdictions.source: City of San José (sanjoseca.gov; Municode Title 20.80 / Ch. 17.20); steadily.com; truvi.com; lodgify.com; airbtics.com; strprofitmap.com.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — both a vacation rental permit (zoning permit) and an annual Vacation Rental License are required in unincorporated areas; many cities (Sonoma, Santa Rosa, Healdsburg, Windsor) ban new STRs
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must obtain a Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT) number from the County Auditor-Controller Treasurer-Tax Collector
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown (permit + license fees apply; consult Permit Sonoma fee schedule)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No for whole-house vacation rentals, but licensee must be a natural-person property owner (not an LLC/corporation); hosted rentals are an alternative in some cities
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No countywide cap on unincorporated whole-house rentals; varies by city (e.g., Sebastopol 30 days/yr, Petaluma 90 days/yr for whole-house)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Tied to permitted bedroom count and septic capacity (Unknown specific formula)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: TOT applies (California wine country TOT typically ~10–14%); exact Sonoma rate Unknown — verify with Tax Collector
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed only in specified zones (AR, RR, PCRR, LEA, DA, RRD, and existing SFRs in C2/LC/K); ineligible in LIA, R1, R2, R3, PC; Williamson Act parcels prohibited; multi-unit and ADU/JADU properties excluded
jurisdictions.min_stay: Vacation rental = 30 days or less (rentals over 30 days are not regulated as vacation rentals)
jurisdictions.density_rules: Exclusion zones (X district) and 5% cap zones limit concentration; many cap zones have reached capacity
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown (not confirmed as mandatory in sources reviewed)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Permit number must be added to the rental listing
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Owner must use a Certified Vacation Rental Property Manager located within 30 miles; site plan, floor plan, septic proof required; license renews annually
jurisdictions.effective_date: August 2023 (current ordinance/business license program); first permit ordinance 2009; coastal zone deadline extended to April 11, 2025
jurisdictions.key_notes: Permit/license is lost when property is sold; new owner must reapply. ~2,300–2,400 STRs operate in unincorporated county. Coastal zone has separate, less restrictive treatment pending Coastal Commission action.
jurisdictions.penalties: 24-hour complaint hotline; fines start at $1,500 for a first violation
jurisdictions.additional_context: Highly restrictive; only ~9% of 2025 residential sales qualified for permits. Cities of Sonoma, Santa Rosa, Healdsburg, Windsor effectively ban new STRs.
jurisdictions.source: Permit Sonoma (permitsonoma.org/vacationrentals); Sonoma County Municode Art. VIII Vacation Rental Licenses; Anderson Zeigler law firm; BNBCalc

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Florida DBPR vacation rental license (dwelling or condo), Miami-Dade County Certificate of Use, plus City of Miami Certificate of Use and Business Tax Receipt
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register with Florida Dept. of Revenue (sales tax) and obtain a Miami-Dade Tourist Tax account; County and City Business Tax Receipts required
jurisdictions.fees: DBPR license ~$170/yr ($90 semi-annual, $50 application); Miami-Dade County CU $139.44 plus inspection $97.84 + $17.42 surcharge (~$246.78 total); City of Miami fees additional
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: In Estate or Low Density Residential zones, the responsible party must reside on-site more than six months per year
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Unknown (no specific annual night cap identified)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Miami-Dade County: maximum 2 persons per bedroom plus 2 additional persons
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown exact combined rate (6% FL sales tax + Miami-Dade tourist/convention development taxes; verify current rate)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: City of Miami permits STRs only in specific transect zones (e.g., T5-L, T6-L, and CI-HD); single-family homes and duplexes in T3 and T4-R are not eligible (per City of Miami v. Airbnb)
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as less than 30 days, rented more than 3 times per year
jurisdictions.density_rules: If more than 25% of units in a building are used for STR-lodging, the building must convert from R-2 to R-1 (hotel) classification under the Florida Building Code
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — Miami-Dade County CU application requires insurance coverage for liability to transient occupants/invitees
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Certificate of Use must be obtained prior to listing/advertising on any peer-to-peer platform; permit numbers should appear on listings
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: CU requires a scheduled inspection within 10 business days; certificate must be posted conspicuously on-site with responsible party info and max occupancy; CU renewed annually
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ongoing; 2025 City of Miami created separate 'Apartment Hotel' category for T4/T5/CI-HD zones
jurisdictions.key_notes: Three layers of jurisdiction (State, Miami-Dade County, City of Miami). Pool safety features required where children under 6 may use property. 24/7 responsible party required.
jurisdictions.penalties: Civil penalties under Ch. 8CC; operating without a CU escalates to $2,500 (3rd offense/24 months); liens may be placed; violation fee $231.90 plus double the CU cost
jurisdictions.additional_context: Legal disputes between Airbnb and the City of Miami resulted in courts upholding the city's ability to ban STRs in specific residential neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.source: Miami-Dade County (miamidade.gov Short-Term Vacation Rentals, Sec. 33-28); City of Miami (miami.gov); Proper Insurance FL guide; Guestable

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Florida DBPR Vacation Rental license; City of Tampa Business Tax Receipt; Hillsborough County Business Tax Receipt (no dedicated City STR registration program)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Hillsborough County Tourist Development Tax account (self-administered by county) plus Florida Dept. of Revenue sales tax registration
jurisdictions.fees: DBPR application ~$150; Hillsborough County BTR ~$45/yr; City of Tampa BTR additional
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No annual day cap; Hillsborough County imposes a 7-night zoning threshold — rentals under 7 nights restricted to commercial/lodging/certain mixed-use zones
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (no City-specific occupancy cap; standard building/fire code applies)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: ~11–13.5% combined (6% FL sales tax + Hillsborough discretionary surtax + 6% Hillsborough Tourist Development Tax); note Hillsborough temporarily suspended certain surtaxes effective Jan 1, 2025
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Rentals under 7 nights limited to commercial, lodging, and certain mixed-use zones per Hillsborough County Land Development Code; 7+ night rentals allowed in residential zones with standard licensing
jurisdictions.min_stay: Effectively 7 nights in residential zones (county zoning rule); no minimum in commercial/lodging zones
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Recommended — homeowner's insurance with fire, hazard and liability coverage of at least $1 million per occurrence (commonly cited but not strictly mandated by City)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Add DBPR license number to all platform listings (Airbnb, VRBO, etc.)
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: City of Tampa has no separate STR ordinance; governed by Hillsborough County Land Development Code and standard City business licensing; noise ordinance enforced via Code Enforcement
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ongoing; status confirmed as of April 2026 (no dedicated City STR registration program)
jurisdictions.key_notes: Florida Statute 509.032(7)(b) preempts local prohibition of STRs. SB 280 (statewide registry bill) was vetoed June 2024. HOA/condo declarations frequently impose 30/90/180-day minimums that override the county 7-night rule.
jurisdictions.penalties: Active enforcement with potential daily fines; historical example of a Thonotosassa owner fined thousands for nightly rentals in a residential zone (2019)
jurisdictions.additional_context: One of Florida's lighter-touch major-metro STR markets at the city level; the county 7-night rule and HOA restrictions are the main constraints.
jurisdictions.source: Chalet (getchalet.com Tampa guide); STRProfitMap; Vello; Lodgify FL guide; Minut FL guide

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — City of Key West Business Tax Receipt plus a special Transient License for rentals under 30 days; Florida DBPR vacation rental license; Monroe County BTR also recommended; manager license required
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Florida Dept. of Revenue sales tax ID and City/County Business Tax Receipts; corporations/LLCs/DBAs must register with the State before a BTR is issued
jurisdictions.fees: Vacation rental manager license $110; City business taxes range $23.15 (non-transient residential) to higher amounts; STR BTR fee not separately codified — consult Licensing Division
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Transient (weekly) rentals limited to 26 weeks total per year, then monthly rentals only (for transient-licensed properties)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Based on square footage; verified during inspection (specific formula Unknown)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 12.5% total (state and county)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Transient rentals allowed only in eligible zones and only with a Transient License; property must qualify; see Code Sections 122-1371 and Chapter 122
jurisdictions.min_stay: 30-day minimum (28 days for non-transient) unless a Transient License is held; transient = 28 days or less
jurisdictions.density_rules: Finite/capped supply of transient licenses; effective moratorium on new transient licenses — most are bought/sold with the property
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown (high hurricane/salt-air costs noted but mandate not confirmed)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: City strongly recommends public verify license validity; only licensed/validated properties may legally advertise
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Annual fire/life-safety inspection required for transient renewals; manager license required; building code compliance verified
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ongoing; key event: Truman Annex special transient licenses expired Dec 22, 2025 (per 2005 settlement / 20-year agreement)
jurisdictions.key_notes: In Dec 2025 the city revoked transient licenses and medallions for ~291 Truman Annex properties and is defending the revocations in court. Licenses are NOT universally transferable on sale — verify before purchase. One of the most restrictive STR markets in the US.
jurisdictions.penalties: Strict enforcement by Code Compliance; daily fines reaching thousands of dollars, liens, and possible criminal charges for persistent offenders
jurisdictions.additional_context: Median Key West STR revenue cited around $73,500 (ADR ~$438, ~50% occupancy); transient-licensed properties command large premiums due to scarcity.
jurisdictions.source: City of Key West FAQs (cityofkeywest-fl.gov); Keys Weekly (Truman Annex); Chalet (getchalet.com Key West); Moving to the Florida Keys

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — City of Destin Short-Term Rental Registration (single-family, townhomes, duplexes, triplexes — NOT condos), City of Destin Business Tax Receipt, and Florida DBPR vacation rental dwelling/condo license
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Florida Dept. of Revenue Annual Resale Certificate (sales tax) and Okaloosa County Tourist Development Tax registration; Okaloosa County BTR
