jurisdictions.fees: STR registration based on size: <2,499 sq ft $500, 2,500–4,999 sq ft $600, 5,000+ sq ft $700 (a $200/unit figure also appears in some city documents); late fees $100 after Mar 31/Apr 30 and $500 after Jun 1; Change of Use application $2,000 where required
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No annual day cap (STR defined as 1 day to 6 months)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: 2 persons per legal bedroom plus 4 additional persons; overall cap of 24 persons per house regardless of bedrooms; occupancy limits enforced 10pm–7am
jurisdictions.tax_rate: ~12–13% combined (6% FL state sales tax + ~1% Okaloosa discretionary surtax + 5–6% Okaloosa County Tourist Development Tax)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed only in specific zoning districts — city is divided into 20+ zones with ~13 permitting STRs; condos/apartments exempt from registration but must comply with other rules
jurisdictions.density_rules: Unknown (no density cap; grandfathering provisions exist for pre-existing uses)
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown (not confirmed as mandatory in sources reviewed)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb/VRBO) collect/remit certain taxes; city-issued annual sticker/decal and required signage must be displayed
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Annual registration (Jan 1, due Mar 1); operating an STR is a commercial activity requiring a 'Change of Use' process with Planning/Engineering/Building inspections; notarized Affidavit of Bedrooms and Parking required; 1 parking space per bedroom
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ordinance effective January 22, 2019 (with subsequent amendments); registration program ongoing
jurisdictions.key_notes: Destin proper is in Okaloosa County; nearby Miramar Beach is in Walton County with separate rules ($300/yr Walton Vacation Rental Certificate). Pre-existing/grandfathered uses lapse if ceased for 6 months.
jurisdictions.penalties: Code Enforcement Special Magistrate imposes fines for failure to register; citations/impoundment for parking violations; noise fines
jurisdictions.additional_context: Registration applies to single-family/townhomes/duplexes/triplexes; condominiums do not register but still need a BTR and must comply with all other regulations.
jurisdictions.source: City of Destin Code of Ordinances Ch. 13 Art. VI (cityofdestin.com / Municode); City of Destin FAQs; Okaloosa County (myokaloosa.com); The Short Term Shop

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — City Vacation Rental Certificate required under Ordinance 1632, plus Florida DBPR license and a local Business Tax Receipt (BTR). Hosted room-only rentals where the host stays on-site are exempt from DBPR licensing.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — registration with the Florida Dept. of Revenue (state sales tax certificate) and Bay County Tourist Development Council/Tax required.
jurisdictions.fees: New registration $250; re-registration $150; $75 inspection/re-inspection fee; $100 lock-out/no-show fee. (Some sources cite a $50 annual STR license fee and $25 plan-submission fee.)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — investors may own and operate more than one vacation rental.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None — Florida state law prohibits local governments from regulating the frequency or duration of stays.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: One person per 150 sq ft of habitable space (balconies, porches, patios, garages excluded); a higher rate of one person per 200 sq ft may be granted after passing a life-safety inspection with no fire code violations.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 6% Florida state sales tax plus Bay County Tourist Development Tax; exact combined county rate Unknown.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Applies only within Panama City Beach city limits (Bay County has a separate ordinance, Ord. 23-18, for unincorporated areas). Specific permitted zoning districts Unknown.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None mandated by city; STR defined as rentals of less than 30 days more than three times per calendar year.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Liability insurance recommended (industry sources cite a suggested minimum of $1,000,000), but not confirmed as a city mandate.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb and Vrbo automatically collect/remit Florida state lodging taxes but do NOT collect Bay County tourist development taxes for hosts.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Requires annual life-safety inspection (fire), DBPR license proof, TDC registration proof, valid PCB BTR, pool inspection if applicable, exterior signage/decal with responsible-party 24/7 contact and certificate number, and interior emergency postings. A local responsible agent must be designated.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Ordinance 1632 largely codified pre-existing rules. Applies only to properties within PCB city limits; verify jurisdiction via city zoning map / Bay County Property Appraiser (District 13).
jurisdictions.penalties: First offense $500; second offense $1,000; third and subsequent violations within 12 months $1,000 plus revocation of the vacation rental certificate for one year. Warnings were eliminated, allowing fines on first offense.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Properties for fewer than 10 occupants follow single-family residence requirements; those over 10 must comply with Residential Group R-1 code. Pool inspection required where five or more properties share a pool.
jurisdictions.source: City of Panama City Beach (pcbfl.gov) Ordinance 1632; Avalara MyLodgeTax; vacationrentallicense.com; TheOfferSheet.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Florida DBPR vacation rental license required for entire-unit rentals; City of Kissimmee requires a Short-Term Rental Business Tax Receipt (BTR) and, for many properties, a Conditional Use Permit (CUP). Osceola County may require its own STR license for unincorporated areas.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register with the Florida Dept. of Revenue (state sales tax/surtax) and the Osceola County Tax Collector for Tourist Development Tax.
jurisdictions.fees: Reported by industry trackers: initial inspection ~$160, license ~$250, annual renewal ~$150. Official city figures should be confirmed.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — not identified as a requirement.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None — state law preempts frequency/duration caps.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Osceola County guidance: maximum 3 guests per bedroom plus 2 additional guests. City-specific cap Unknown.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 6% Florida state sales tax + 6% Osceola County Tourist Development Tax + county discretionary surtax; combined burden reported around 13.5%.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs are not permitted by right in all residential districts. Generally permitted only in certain zoning districts (e.g., RB-, RB-2, RC-1, RC-2) or designated overlays/planned districts (SRPUD/MUPUD within an STRO overlay). Many operators must obtain a CUP via Development Review Committee and a Planning Advisory Board public hearing. (Code Sections 14-2-64; 14-2-206; CUP provisions 14-2-44; 14-2-51.)
jurisdictions.min_stay: None mandated; STR defined locally as non-permanent use not exceeding 30 days within a twelve-month period for the same individual(s).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Unknown — governed primarily through zoning/overlay eligibility rather than an explicit density cap.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown — not confirmed as a city mandate; coverage allowing STR activity is advised.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb/Vrbo collect/remit Florida state taxes, but Osceola County is not contracted with these platforms for the Tourist Development Tax — owners must register and remit TDT directly. Monthly filings required (including zero filers).
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Building/housing inspection per Florida Building Code; fire inspection typically required before BTR issuance; government-issued ID required. City may deny applications inconsistent with zoning. HOA/condo rules may be more restrictive.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Unknown — governed by ongoing Kissimmee Code of Ordinances provisions.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Classified as Restricted because CUP and zoning eligibility limit where STRs may legally operate. Enforcement varies across Osceola County (St. Cloud strict with $500/day fines; Kissimmee generally more lenient). Verify jurisdiction (city vs. unincorporated county).
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown for City of Kissimmee specifically; St. Cloud cited at $500/day for violations.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Florida SB 280 (which would have centralized STR regulation) was vetoed in 2024, preserving local authority over zoning, occupancy caps, and permits.
jurisdictions.source: Kissimmee Code of Ordinances (via strprofitmap.com); bnbcalc.com; Osceola County Tax Collector guidance (via millanrealty.com / mikechenrealtor.com); funstayflorida.com.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — City Vacation Rental Registration / Certificate of Compliance required (Ordinance C-16-25, Chapter 15 Article X), plus Florida DBPR license and Broward County Business Tax Receipt. DBPR license exempt if owner-occupied homestead renting a room only.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Florida Dept. of Revenue sales tax registration and Broward County Tourist Development Tax registration required (or proof platform remits on host's behalf).
jurisdictions.fees: Initial registration fee $350 (covers first two inspections for up to 4 units on one folio); renewal $160 non-owner-occupied / $80 owner-occupied; $75–$100 per inspection/re-inspection/no-show. Combined initial registration + inspection reported around $460.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — both owner-occupied and non-owner-occupied rentals are permitted (with different renewal fees).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None — state law preempts frequency/duration caps; STR defined as rentals more than three times per year for periods of 30 days or less.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Maximum 2 persons per legal bedroom; transient occupants must not exceed the number on the certificate of compliance. (Note: 2024 Florida law allows alternative occupancy formulas based on square footage.)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: ~13% combined (6% Florida state sales tax + 6% Broward County Tourist Development Tax + ~1% surtax).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed in most zoning districts, but use of accessory dwelling units as vacation rentals is prohibited in RS-8 and RD-15 districts (per ULDR Sec. 47-19.2.A.7).
jurisdictions.min_stay: None mandated; rentals of 30 days or less are regulated as vacation rentals.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown — not confirmed as an explicit city mandate; tax/safety compliance is the documented focus.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb/Vrbo) may collect and remit state sales tax on the host's behalf; hosts can submit a tax statement/notarized letter naming the platform in lieu of separate proof.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: State and county licenses must be obtained before the city application via the LauderBuild portal. Mandatory annual safety inspection and Certificate of Compliance. Designated 24/7 responsible party must respond on-site within one hour. Noise-monitoring device required in all rentals retaining data for 180 days (Sec. 15-276(f)); off-street parking plan may be required. Applications process in ~5 business days once accepted; pending applications closed after 30 days.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Amendment approved 2023-09-19 (program governed by Ordinance C-16-25).
jurisdictions.key_notes: Three-tier compliance: State (DBPR), County (Broward BTR + TDT), City (registration + inspection). License approval can reportedly take months.
jurisdictions.penalties: Civil penalties increased under 2023 amendment; reported at ~$250 per uncontested violation and up to $15,000/day during suspension; license revocation possible. Maximum penalties set for operating while under suspension.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Sexual-offender residency restrictions (Sec. 16-127) apply to transient occupants; quiet hours typically 10 pm–7 am.
jurisdictions.source: City of Fort Lauderdale (fortlauderdale.gov) Vacation Rental Program / Ordinance C-16-25; loshaklaw.com; masterhost.ca; hometeamluxuryrentals.com; strrequirements.com.

jurisdictions.license_required: Florida DBPR license required where applicable. Properties within the City of Naples are exempt from Collier County's STR registration (Ord. 2021-45). City requires a Business Tax Receipt (BTR) and Certificate of Use (COU) with inspection; Collier County BTR also applies.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Florida Dept. of Revenue sales tax registration and a Collier County Tourist Development Tax (TDT) account required.
jurisdictions.fees: Business Tax Receipt fee varies by property size/location; Collier County registration (for unincorporated areas) is $50 per unit one-time. Specific City of Naples BTR/COU fee amounts Unknown.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Unknown — not clearly specified for the City of Naples.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Effective cap: single-family homes may be rented for less than 30 days no more than 3 times per calendar year; otherwise rental terms must be 30 days or longer.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Enforced based on property specifications and bedroom count; specific numeric cap Unknown.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 6% Florida state sales tax + 5% Collier County Tourist Development Tax.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: City of Naples effectively prohibits true short-term (<30 day) rentals in single-family homes except the three-times-per-year allowance; properties may not be advertised as available for rentals of less than 30 days. Sources conflict on whether recent state preemption has loosened residential-zone treatment. Guest houses may not be leased/rented (LDC 5.03.03 in county; Naples has its own restrictions).
jurisdictions.min_stay: 30 days or longer, except up to 3 short rentals (<30 days) per calendar year per property.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Recommended (property and liability), often required by platforms; not confirmed as a legal mandate.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Advertising a single-family property for rentals of less than 30 days is prohibited; advertising must include any required registration numbers (county). Platform tax-collection specifics for Naples Unknown.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: City of Naples is exempt from Collier County Ordinance 2021-45. Certificate of Use requires a safety/code inspection; smoke detectors and accessible fire extinguishers required. Quiet hours typically 10 pm–7 am. Sources disagree on how 'restrictive' Naples currently is — verify directly with the City of Naples Code Enforcement before operating.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Unknown — based on existing City of Naples Code of Ordinances (e.g., the 30-day rule); Collier County Ord. 2021-45 effective 2022-01-03 (does not apply within city).
jurisdictions.key_notes: The 30-day-minimum rule with a 3-time annual exception is the defining constraint, making genuine short-term rental operation in single-family homes within the city very limited. Note: Some industry trackers describe Naples as having become 'STR-friendly'; official city FAQ still states the 30-day rule — treat the official source as authoritative.
jurisdictions.penalties: Repeated noise/nuisance violations can result in fines or revocation of rental permits; specific monetary amounts Unknown.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Properties in unincorporated Collier County (not the city) follow a different, more permissive registration regime under Ord. 2021-45 ($50/unit, 24/7 responsible party, DBPR + TDT). Marco Island and Everglades City are also exempt from the county ordinance.
jurisdictions.source: City of Naples (naplesgov.com) Code Compliance FAQ; Collier County Ord. 2021-45 (colliercountyfl.qscend.com); lodgify.com; bnbcalc.com; naplesed.com. Note: sources conflict on current restrictiveness.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — City STR permit required (two-tier: Type 1 owner-occupied; Type 2 non-owner-occupied) under the STR ordinance (Article XXII), applicable only within San Antonio city limits. Applications via the BuildSA online portal / DSD.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register with the City of San Antonio Finance Dept. for a Hotel Occupancy Tax (HOT) account and with the Texas Comptroller for state HOT (state registration not required if a marketplace remits all state HOT).
jurisdictions.fees: After 2024 amendment: Type 1 permit $300; Type 2 permit $450 — both valid 3 years. Board of Adjustment special-exception application: $400 (homestead) / $600 (others). (Pre-2024 permit fee was $100.)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Not for all — Type 1 requires the property to be the owner/operator's primary residence (incl. accessory dwelling units); Type 2 is non-owner-occupied and subject to density caps.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Unknown — no explicit annual day cap identified.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Set per permit via a submitted floor plan identifying sleeping areas and maximum occupancy; no single citywide numeric figure identified. STRs may not be used for large events.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 9% City HOT + 1.75% Bexar County HOT, plus 6% Texas state HOT.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Permitted in various residential and commercial zoning districts (residential, O-1, O-1.5, O-2, NC, C-1, C-2, and D commercial zones). Type 1 has no density limit; Type 2 subject to density caps.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Type 1: no density limit. Type 2: allowed by right up to 12.5% of units on a blockface (one side of a street between intersections); multi-family buildings with fewer than 8 units limited to one Type 2; larger buildings capped at 12.5%. Exceeding caps requires a Board of Adjustment special exception.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of general liability insurance with minimum limits of $500,000 per occurrence and $1,000,000 aggregate.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Marketplaces (Airbnb/Vrbo) must remove San Antonio listings lacking a valid STR permit number and are now required to collect/remit city occupancy tax; they automatically collect the state HOT portion.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Permit application requires a floor plan (sleeping areas, max occupancy, evacuation routes, fire-extinguisher locations), designated agent contact for complaints, proof of HOT registration, statement of compliance, and proof of liability insurance. Monthly HOT reporting required even with no taxable receipts. Hosts may not provide food/beverages to guests. Properties receiving city housing incentives cannot operate as Type 2 STRs. Permit is non-transferable.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Applies only within San Antonio city limits; properties in unincorporated Bexar County need no city permit but must still register for HOT. City estimates 1,100+ unpermitted STRs operating.
jurisdictions.penalties: Civil enforcement via administrative hearing officers; mandatory meetings with the DSD director for repeat violators; permit revocation of up to 3 years for serious/repeat violations. Violation notice typically gives 14 days to correct.
jurisdictions.additional_context: 2024 amendment raised fees, added platform-enforcement requirements, and strengthened enforcement. Texas generally takes a lighter statewide approach, but San Antonio is comparatively strict.
jurisdictions.source: City of San Antonio (sa.gov / DSD) STR ordinance documents (docsonline.sanantonio.gov); Municode Article XXII; Avalara MyLodgeTax; bnbcalc.com; TheOfferSheet.

jurisdictions.license_required: Registration ordinance exists but enforcement is currently enjoined by court; existing minimum property standards still apply. The 2023 registration requirement is administered by Code Compliance Services when in force.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — operators must register and remit Hotel Occupancy Tax (HOT) to the City of Dallas (via dallas.munirevs.com) plus state HOT.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown (registration fee schedule not confirmed; registration ordinance enforcement enjoined)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Three people per bedroom with a total maximum occupancy of 12
jurisdictions.tax_rate: City HOT 7% of consideration paid for sleeping accommodations, plus 6% state HOT
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: 2023 zoning ordinance would create a 'Short-term rental lodging' use and effectively ban STRs in single-family residential zones, but enforcement is blocked by a temporary injunction (upheld by Texas Court of Appeals in 2025); STRs continue operating pending litigation.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as rentals fewer than 30 consecutive days (or one month, whichever is less)
jurisdictions.density_rules: Number of STRs in a single rentable unit may not exceed one; broader single-family-zone restrictions enjoined
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Listings must show the City of Dallas short-term rental registration number where the registration ordinance applies; platforms may delist non-compliant listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Two June 2023 ordinances (zoning + registration) are under a temporary injunction filed Dec 6, 2023; Fifth District Court of Appeals upheld the injunction multiple times in 2025, and the City has petitioned the Texas Supreme Court. City continues enforcing minimum property standards, noise, and nuisance ordinances.
jurisdictions.effective_date: June 14, 2023 (ordinances approved); enforcement enjoined since Dec 6, 2023
jurisdictions.key_notes: Highly volatile regulatory environment; single-family STR ban could be implemented quickly if the Texas Supreme Court rules for the City. 2026 FIFA World Cup is a political factor. Noise limits prohibit audible noise 10pm–7am.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to $500 per violation, escalating penalties on late taxes (cited as ~15% plus interest), citations, permit revocation, and platform delisting
jurisdictions.additional_context: Texas grants broad local authority over STRs with no statewide preemption. HOAs and deed restrictions may independently restrict or ban STRs. Analysts estimated the single-family ban would eliminate ~90–95% of existing STR supply.
jurisdictions.source: City of Dallas Code Ch. 42B (codelibrary.amlegal.com); dallascityhall.com Code Compliance & HOT pages; Rent Responsibly (Aug 2025); industry trackers (Vello, Truvi)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — all STR properties must register with the City of Galveston and receive a GVR (Galveston Vacation Rental) number; registration required since 2015 (Ord. 15-012).
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register and remit Hotel Occupancy Tax; authority transferred from Park Board to the City of Galveston effective October 1, 2025.
jurisdictions.fees: $250 registration fee for new listings (or when switching from management company to independent management); $25 fine for late/missing tax reports
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No (both owner-occupied and non-owner-occupied STRs allowed)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Not specified in the STR ordinance; best practice is to follow applicable building- and fire-safety code occupancy standards
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 9% local HOT on gross rental revenues (incl. cleaning, pet, damage fees), plus 6% Texas state HOT
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs permitted in all zoning districts on the island except the R-0 district where they are prohibited
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as rentals fewer than 30 consecutive days
jurisdictions.density_rules: No numerical cap; all qualifying properties outside R-0 may register
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: GVR number must be displayed on all advertisements including booking platforms, management company websites, and private/social listings; non-compliant listings will be removed.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: New comprehensive ordinance updates approved Nov 13, 2025, developed by an STR Ad Hoc Committee; includes a three-strike rule allowing license revocation if three violations occur at a property within 12 months. Current registrations expired Dec 31, 2025, with renewal/registration portal opening Dec 1, 2025.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ordinance 15-012 (2015) foundational; City regulatory authority effective Oct 1, 2025; updated ordinance approved Nov 13, 2025
jurisdictions.key_notes: Owners responsible for ensuring renters comply with rules on parking, trash, noise, firearms and fireworks. Violations reportable via STR hotline. A proposed mandatory 'parking plan' section was omitted from the final ordinance.
jurisdictions.penalties: Failure to register or remit HOT is a Class C misdemeanor, punishable by a fine up to $500 per offense/day, plus late penalties and interest; possible license revocation under three-strike rule
jurisdictions.additional_context: Registrations are non-transferable; new registration required when ownership changes. Quarterly filing allowed for properties under $500/month or $1,500/quarter in taxes. HOA/private community rules may supersede city allowances.
jurisdictions.source: galvestontx.gov official STR & ordinance pages; Houston Public Media (Nov 14, 2025); GetChalet and The Offer Sheet trackers

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a Short-Term Rental Permit is required for any dwelling rented for less than 30 days within City Limits (per Section 20.222 of the Zoning Ordinance); STRs in the ETJ do not require a city permit. Permits valid one year and renewed annually.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register with Texas Comptroller for state HOT; city HOT remitted via the My Government Online (MGO) portal quarterly.
jurisdictions.fees: Registration fee of $150 per unit plus $100 per bedroom at time of application (annual renewal)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No (ordinance defines categories including Accessory, B&B, Condominium, and Facility STRs)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Under 2024 ordinance: 2 occupants per bedroom plus 2, max of 12 guests (all ages). Grandfathered (pre-April 1, 2022) units follow 2018 fire-code-based limits (one occupant per 200 sq ft).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 7% city HOT (filed quarterly) plus 6% Texas state HOT = 13% total
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Commercial zones (C1, C1.5, C2, CBD) generally allow STR uses by right (STR-Condo needs a CUP). New entire-home STRs effectively prevented in single-family (R1/R2) zones; 'stranded property' adjacency and 200-ft radius rules apply.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as rentals less than 30 days
jurisdictions.density_rules: R1/R2 unoccupied 'stranded properties' must share two property lines with STR-unoccupied/C2/CBD/Public Facility zoning, and 50% of properties within a 200-ft radius must have STR-unoccupied permit or qualifying zoning
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Permit number/BECA sticker must be displayed; advertising compliance required. (Airbtics reports many listings operate without a displayed license.)
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Current STR Ordinance (2023-18) effective January 1, 2024. Annual inspections required, plus inspections on new permit issuance, ownership transfer, complaints, and modifications. Properties permitted before April 1, 2022 are 'non-conforming/grandfathered' but must comply with updated rules for life safety, signage, 24-hour contact, HOT, etc.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Current ordinance effective January 1, 2024 (prior major changes April 1, 2022)
jurisdictions.key_notes: Fredericksburg is a Dark Sky Community with an 11pm outdoor lighting curfew; quiet hours 10pm–7am must be posted. Parking: one off-street spot per bedroom (tandem allowed) under the 2024 ordinance.
jurisdictions.penalties: Permit revocation for violations of Section 5.401; lack of HOT remittance results in a 90-day suspension of permit
jurisdictions.additional_context: Operators must know which ordinance year their property was originally permitted under. ETJ properties exempt from city permitting but subject to state rules, county rules, and deed restrictions.
jurisdictions.source: City of Fredericksburg official STR pages (fbgtx.org); FBGTXSTRA ordinance breakdown; The Offer Sheet, Airbtics, STR Profit Map trackers

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — any property used as an STR (rented 30 days or less) must obtain a Short-Term Rental permit/registration from the city before advertising or operating (Chapter 12, Article VIII, §12-252). Annual renewal required by December 31.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Hotel/Motel Tax registration with the city for occupancy tax collection and remittance, plus Texas state HOT permits.
jurisdictions.fees: Updated STR and hotel registration fees noticed December 2025 (specific amounts: Unknown)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Based on number of legal sleeping rooms documented on the property information sheet (specific per-room figure: Unknown)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown (city Hotel/Motel Occupancy Tax rate not confirmed; 6% Texas state HOT applies)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Daily and weekly rentals permitted only in areas zoned R-2 and above (well over 85% of the city's residential areas); a small portion of the city (e.g., Channel Vista area) prohibits STRs to preserve permanent-resident neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as rental durations of 30 days or less
jurisdictions.density_rules: New development standards: minimum lot size of 5,000 sq ft; development density reduced from 21 units per acre to 8 units per acre
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Property must display a placard near the primary entrance showing the registration number and contact information (§12-252).
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Applicants must submit operator identity, property details, a designated 24/7 local contact, and sleeping room info. Requires trash toter allocation by sleeping rooms, fire extinguisher placement, building code compliance, parking plan, and tenant information sheet. Mandatory evacuation upon any non-resident evacuation order (§12-213). Updated STR/hotel registration fee notice issued Dec 2025.
jurisdictions.effective_date: STR Ordinance No. 2022-02 (Chapter 12); fee update December 2025
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of the most STR-dense markets in Texas, with over 3,000 registered short-term rentals; enforcement increasingly proactive via STR hotline (361-333-5659).
jurisdictions.penalties: Penalties or revocation for non-compliance (§12-214); denial of future permits and escalating fines for serious/repeat violations
jurisdictions.additional_context: Required operating documents include STR registration certificate, local contact designation, property/tenant information sheets, fire safety records, building code compliance, waste plan, and parking plan. HOA restrictions may also apply.
jurisdictions.source: City of Port Aransas official STR registration page (cityofportaransas.org); Code Ch. 12 Art. VIII via Municode; The Offer Sheet, Airbtics, Sand-N-Key trackers

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — requires both a general business license and a short-term rental license, plus a Lodger's Tax ID obtained before applying. Annual renewal required (with a 90-day renewal grace period).
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Lodger's Tax ID required; hosts responsible for city lodger's tax accounts and filings even if platforms collect.
jurisdictions.fees: Approximately $50 application fee and $100 annual STR license/renewal (one source cites ~$25/year for the STR license plus business license fees); confirm current amounts on the city portal
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — STRs are restricted to the host's primary residence; only one active STR license allowed per host. Host must live in the property (commonly cited as at least 50% of the year).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None for a primary residence (no night cap, but must be the host's actual home)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Limited by zoning to maintain residential character; commonly cited as up to two people per bedroom plus two additional occupants
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Denver Lodger's Tax of 10.75% on taxable lodging (Airbnb/Vrbo typically collect and remit)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs allowed only in residential zones and only as a primary residence; non-primary units require a lodging facility license, available only in certain zone districts and possibly requiring an occupancy classification change. ADUs eligible if part of/attached to the primary residence.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as lodging for fewer than 30 consecutive days (city language references fewer than 29 nights)
jurisdictions.density_rules: No density/separation cap, but one-primary-residence rule effectively limits each host to a single STR; overlapping rentals not allowed
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of liability insurance (minimum $1,000,000 aggregate coverage) or evidence each booking uses a platform providing equivalent guest coverage
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: License number must be displayed on every advertisement (Airbnb, Vrbo, etc.); platforms typically collect/remit the 10.75% Lodger's Tax. City actively monitors platforms.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: First enacted 2016, updated 2019. Initial applications generally reviewed within ~30 days (specialist reviews up to 90 days). Requires working smoke alarms, CO detectors, fire extinguisher, emergency exit instructions, and a floor plan; inspections may occur randomly, on complaint, or during specialist review. 2025 noise ordinance updates set residential limits ~55 dBA daytime / 50 dBA night. No commercial events or large parties.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ordinance first enacted 2016; updated 2019; 2025 noise ordinance updates
jurisdictions.key_notes: Considered one of the strictest STR markets in the U.S. due to the primary-residence rule, which disqualifies investment/second homes. Active enforcement via audits, complaints, and platform cooperation; hundreds of unlicensed STRs flagged/shut down recently.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to $999 per violation; license revocation for misrepresenting primary residence; providing false primary-residence information can result in felony charges (cited as 2–6 years prison and fines up to $500,000)
jurisdictions.additional_context: Tenants may operate STRs with written landlord permission affidavit. Colorado HB 25-1247 (2025) allows counties (with voter approval) to raise lodging taxes. State proposals to reclassify intensive STRs for property-tax purposes (possible Jan 1, 2026 effective dates) are pending. HOA covenants may independently restrict STRs.
jurisdictions.source: City and County of Denver STR licensing pages (denvergov.org); Minut, Hostaway, ElevateSTR, Proper Insurance, Awning, Corbitt Group, Rent Responsibly trackers

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — an Accommodation Unit License from the Town's Finance Department is required for any rental under 30 consecutive days.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — owners collecting payments directly must register/remit via MuniRevs (Town) and the Colorado Dept. of Revenue (state/county); platforms remit automatically for their bookings.
jurisdictions.fees: BOLT fee $75 (studio) to $175 (4+ bedrooms) per year, plus a regulatory fee reported at ~$756 per bedroom per year (e.g., a 4-bedroom owner pays ~$3,205/yr).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — but an exemption from fees applies when the unit is the owner's primary residence and rented no more than 21 days per year.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No town-wide day cap within Breckenridge town limits (caps are on license numbers, not days). Note: unincorporated Summit County applies different caps (e.g., 135-day limits).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (set by license terms/HOA; verify with Town).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Total combined lodging tax of 12.275%, including Town of Breckenridge 5.9% (2.5% sales + 3.4% accommodation), plus state/county taxes.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Zone-based licensing system. Resort Zone: 100% (no cap). Zone 1 (Tourism): 92%. Zone 2 (downtown core): 51% (waitlist). Zone 3 (single-family residential): 10% cap (~390 licenses; multi-year waitlist).
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as rental for less than 30 consecutive days; no stated minimum-night requirement.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Per-zone caps on the number of STR licenses; once a zone hits its cap, applicants are waitlisted until attrition frees a license.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown (not specified by Town in sources; HOA may require).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb/Vrbo and most major platforms collect and remit applicable taxes; the Town business/license number must be displayed in all advertisements.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Licenses are tied to the owner, NOT the property, and are non-transferable; a buyer does not inherit the prior owner's license. HOA rules may be more restrictive.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Original STR ordinance passed 2018, effective January 1, 2019; zone-based licensing system established in 2021.
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of Colorado's most strictly regulated STR markets. License availability changes via sales, relinquishment, or non-renewals; the Town updates availability roughly every three months.
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown (specific fine amounts not stated in sources; operating without a license violates Town code).
jurisdictions.additional_context: Created to balance tourism revenue against local workforce-housing pressures in Summit County. Verify zone via Town's interactive STR map.
jurisdictions.source: Town of Breckenridge (townofbreckenridge.com); thebrecklife.com; buybreck.com; golocalvr.com (2026 guides)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — an approved STR license/registration is required before advertising or operating a rental under 30 consecutive days (per STR Ordinance 20230207).
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — each STR address must have its own Town of Vail sales tax license and file periodic (monthly/quarterly) returns, including zero returns.
jurisdictions.fees: STR Registration Fee of $260 for Individual Property Owners; $50 for On-Site 24/7 Front Desk Managed STR License. Property managers must obtain a separate business license.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Per industry reporting, generally ~2 persons per bedroom plus 2 additional guests, not to exceed ~1 person per 200 sq ft of living space in R-1/R-2 zones (verify with Town).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Cumulative STR taxes in Vail reported at 10.8% (increased from 9.8% as of 1/1/23), including Colorado state sales tax (2.9%), Eagle County tax, and Vail's accommodation/sales tax.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Reported as no STR-specific zoning cap; properties divided into zones with residential (R-1/R-2) zones facing occupancy limits and commercial/mixed-use zones offering more flexibility.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as lease for less than 30 consecutive days (excludes B&Bs and accommodation units).
jurisdictions.density_rules: No license-number cap identified; ~2,506 STRs licensed as of May 2024.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — minimum $1,000,000 liability coverage covering STR activity; an affidavit using an approved marketplace facilitator's insurance (Airbnb, VRBO, Evolve) may be substituted.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb/VRBO collect and remit taxes for their bookings; owners must post the STR registration number in all listing advertisements.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Requires a designated local representative within 60 minutes, available 24/7, who must resolve complaints within 60 minutes (30 minutes between 11 PM–7 AM). Fire/building inspection required (every 3 years; exempt if 24/7 on-site management). Signed self-compliance affidavit and posted interior signage required.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Comprehensive ordinance in effect since January 1, 2023; licenses referenced as expiring February 28, 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Guest registry records (names, addresses, length of stay) must be maintained and available for inspection. Overnight street parking prohibited Nov 1–Apr 30.
jurisdictions.penalties: Failure to submit a sales tax return results in penalties and assessments (specific amounts not stated).
jurisdictions.additional_context: Tiered requirements by management structure; properties lacking 24/7 on-site management face stricter compliance and higher fees.
jurisdictions.source: Town of Vail (vail.gov); Airbnb Help Center; Rent Responsibly; getchalet.com; golocalvr.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — an STR/business license from the Town of Telluride is required before advertising; the license number must appear in all advertising.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — local sales tax number and excise tax number required before collecting/remitting; Town taxes paid monthly via Rentalscape and state/county via Colorado SUTS.
jurisdictions.fees: Town business license reported at ~$100/year; STR permit fees reported ranging ~$500–$1,200/year by category/size (per industry sources); context of a ~$857/bedroom effective regulatory fee reported.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — but permit categories distinguish Primary Residence permits (lower cost) from Investment Property permits.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: In Residential Zone Districts: STRs capped at up to 3 stays per year with a cumulative maximum of 29 nights per year. Non-residential zones generally not subject to this annual cap.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Per industry reporting, residential zones limit occupancy to ~2 guests per bedroom plus 2 additional, max ~10 people (verify with Town).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Reported total ~10.8% (Colorado state 2.9%, San Miguel County 3.4%, Town of Telluride 4.5%), plus a 2% Town Lodgers' Tax and affordable-housing/SMART components; state+county combined ~6.22%.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Residential Zone Districts (Residential, Historic Residential, Hillside Transitional, Hillside Developing One/Two, West Hillside, Medium Density Residential) carry the 3-stay/29-night annual caps; STR allowed by-right in zones where listed.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR period defined as 1–29 consecutive days (under 30 days). Long-term (30+ days) allowed town-wide but limited to 3 rentals/year.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Town-wide cap limiting STR licenses to the number issued as of election day (November 2, 2021). Ownership limit: no person/entity may hold financial interest in more than 2 units with an Accommodations Unit License or Rental House License.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Town uses automated systems to track activity via Airbnb/VRBO; license number must be displayed in all advertising. Platforms collect/remit some taxes but owner remains responsible.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Owner representative on call 24/7 during occupancy; contact info filed with Town Clerk and posted inside the property. Random inspections plus an annual self-inspection required. Records of rental dates/guests/taxes must be maintained.
jurisdictions.effective_date: License cap approved by voters November 2, 2021; new STR categories/fees implemented in 2024; increased SMART sales tax (1.07%) effective Jan 1, 2025.
jurisdictions.key_notes: 2026 renewals opened November 1, 2025, due January 1, 2026; late fees begin January 7, 2026. A new unified Bulk Notary Affidavit is required.
jurisdictions.penalties: Violations may be prosecuted as misdemeanors with fines up to $1,000 per offense and up to 90 days imprisonment; each day is a separate offense.
jurisdictions.additional_context: STR fee revenue is dedicated to the Town's Affordable Housing Fund. STR exchanges with out-of-town properties allowed only without payment/value transfer (except non-profit fundraisers).
jurisdictions.source: Town of Telluride (telluride-co.gov); Telluride Municipal Code/Land Use Code §§3-601, 6-1-35; Colorado Lawyer (cobar.org); strprofitmap.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — it is unlawful to advertise, offer, provide, or operate any STR, hosted STR, or temporary STR without first obtaining a City license (no grandfather clause for licensing).
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — operators must collect and remit applicable sales/lodging taxes plus the voter-approved 9% STR tax.
jurisdictions.fees: Application fees apply (specific amounts not stated in sources); proof of building/fire code conformance and a 24/7 local contact required. Annual renewals required.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — but a 'Temporary STR' license category applies specifically to a primary residence (max 2 occurrences and 30 cumulative days per year).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No general day cap for standard STRs; Temporary STR (primary residence) capped at 30 cumulative days per calendar year.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Maximum occupants = 1 per 150 sq ft of net floor area, but no fewer than 2 and no more than 16 occupants.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Voter-approved 9% STR tax (effective Jan 1, 2023) on every booking, layered on top of standard state/local sales and lodging taxes.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STR Overlay Zone with three zones: green (unlimited, includes downtown commercial off Lincoln Ave and properties near resort base), yellow (capped license numbers), and red (STRs prohibited).
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as lodging for fewer than 30 consecutive days; license types include STR, Hosted STR (one guestroom, max 400 sq ft), and Temporary STR.
jurisdictions.density_rules: License caps in yellow zones; red zones prohibit new licenses. Legal nonconforming status allows pre-existing lawful STRs to continue regardless of zone limits (must show rental within the prior 12 months).
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown (not specified in City sources; insurance endorsements for transient occupancy generally advised).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Operators responsible for collecting/remitting the 9% tax correctly (typically passed through to guests). License must be displayed inside the premises.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Each STR must have a designated local responsible party who responds to complaints within 1 hour; a visible interior notice with 24-hour contact info and STR rules is required. Functioning smoke/CO detectors and fire extinguishers required. Overnight vehicle parking cannot be in public right-of-way or emergency access easement.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ordinances 2856, 2857, 2858 adopted June 7, 2022; 9% tax approved Nov 8, 2022, effective Jan 1, 2023; licenses required by Jan 1, 2023.
jurisdictions.key_notes: STR licenses do NOT transfer with sale of the property. City Manager may exempt certain professionally managed STR groups in Overlay Zone A with 24/7 staffed desk.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a license punishable by citation to municipal court with a fine up to $2,650 per violation per day.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Regulations driven by workforce/affordable-housing concerns; 9% tax revenue directed largely toward affordable housing (e.g., Brown Ranch/Yampa Valley Housing Authority). Verify jurisdiction (City vs. unincorporated Routt County) by address.
jurisdictions.source: City of Steamboat Springs (steamboatsprings.net); Ordinances 2856–2858 & 2888; Steamboat Pilot; Airbnb Help Center; Rent Responsibly

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a City-issued short-term rental permit is required before advertising/operating; each advertised unit requires its own permit (as of Dec 2024, e.g., main house + casita = 2 permits).
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — owners must obtain a state Transaction Privilege Tax (TPT) License from the Arizona Dept. of Revenue before applying for a City permit; a valid TPT license is required for renewal.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual non-refundable, non-transferable permit fee of $210 per rental unit (increased $10 to $210 effective Jan 10, 2025). TPT license ~$12 per location. Late fees effective Jan 1, 2026: $50 (2–90 days late), $100 (90+ days), capped at $100.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No day cap (Arizona cities cannot cap the number of STRs). STR = stays under 30 consecutive days; 30+ day rentals need no permit.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (occupancy generally tied to property size/bedrooms per Arizona norms; not a specific figure in City sources).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Varies by county: Yavapai County 13.325% (6.325% + 3.5% bed tax + 3.5% City hotel tax); Coconino County 13.90% (6.9% + 3.5% + 3.5%).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: No STR-specific zoning restrictions (permissive); STR permits are for residential properties only, not commercial-zoned/commercial lodging.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Defined as rental for fewer than 30 consecutive days per A.R.S. §9-500.39; listings must show at least a 30-day minimum to avoid permit requirement.
jurisdictions.density_rules: No density/number cap permitted under Arizona state law (SB 1168). ~1,116 registered STRs (~16.39% of housing inventory).
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — maintain liability insurance of at least $500,000 in the aggregate per rental unit, unless the listing platform provides equivalent coverage.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb/VRBO) collect/remit some taxes but owner remains responsible; permit number must be included in every advertisement and listing. Marketplace may conduct required sex-offender background checks.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Must notify adjacent/across/diagonal single-family neighbors by certified mail or hand-delivery with permit number and 24-hour emergency contact. Sex-offender background check required ≥24 hrs before check-in (records kept 12 months). Posted interior safety notice required. Building safety approvals required.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Chapter 5.25 framework implemented following Arizona SB 1168 (signed July 6, 2022); fee increase effective Jan 10, 2025; late-fee rules effective Jan 1, 2026; City Code current through Ordinance 2025-13 (Dec 9, 2025).
jurisdictions.key_notes: Special events (weddings, receptions, retreats, conferences, workshops, etc.) are explicitly prohibited at STRs (Code 5.25.050). City operates a 24/7 STR complaint hotline (928-203-5110).
jurisdictions.penalties: Permits may be suspended up to 12 months; special-event violations can lead to citations, penalties, and permit revocation for one year (see Code §§5.25.110, 5.25.120).
jurisdictions.additional_context: Permits are non-transferable as to person or location; a property sale requires the new owner to apply for a new permit and the seller to remove all advertisements. Owners may place a voluntary 49-year deed restriction against STR use.
jurisdictions.source: City of Sedona (sedonaaz.gov); Sedona City Code Ch. 5.25; strprofitmap.com; theoffersheet.com; lodgify.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — City of Phoenix STR permit required (converted from registration to mandatory permitting). Owner must also hold a state TPT license.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Arizona Transaction Privilege Tax (TPT) license from the Arizona Department of Revenue (AZDOR) is mandatory for any transient lodging under 30 days.
jurisdictions.fees: Non-refundable $250 city permit fee (annual). TPT license fee separate via AZDOR.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — Phoenix allows both owner-occupied and non-owner-occupied STRs with no cap on the number of permits per individual. (Exception: ADUs with certificate of occupancy issued on/after Sept 14, 2024 require a notarized owner-residency attestation effective April 4, 2026.)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (state HB 2429, pending in early 2026, would set ~2 adults per sleeping area plus up to 2 additional persons; not yet law).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Combined transient lodging tax approximately 12.57% (state, county, and municipal components combined). Verify current rate with AZDOR.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Arizona state law (SB 1350) prevents outright bans; STRs are a permitted residential use. Non-residential uses prohibited. Reports differ on ADU eligibility — a 2024 state law bars cities over 75,000 from banning ADU STRs.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as rentals under 30 consecutive days; no specified minimum-night floor identified. Unknown.
jurisdictions.density_rules: None — no cap on number of permits per individual.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of at least $500,000 in liability insurance (or equivalent platform-provided primary coverage).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Permit/license number must be displayed on every STR advertisement/listing (Airbnb, VRBO, Booking.com, etc.). Online lodging marketplaces must register with AZDOR and collect/remit state and local taxes.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Must designate a 24-hour emergency contact (name, address, monitored phone, email). Must deliver notices of intent to operate via certified mail to neighbors/associations within 600 feet. Owner must register property with Maricopa County Assessor and submit notarized criminal-background attestation. Large events/parties prohibited.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Permitting ordinance (G-7156) effective November 6, 2023; ADU residency-attestation provision effective April 4, 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Authorized under Arizona SB 1168 (2022). Permits are property-specific and non-transferable; applications processed within ~7 days of complete documentation.
jurisdictions.penalties: Court-adjudicated violations: 1st — min $500 or one night's rental fee; 2nd — min $1,000 or two nights' fee; 3rd — min $3,500 or three nights' fee. Operating without a permit can carry fines up to $1,000/month. Three violations within 12 months can trigger suspension/revocation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Neighborhood Services Department is lead enforcement agency. State residential rental tax ended in 2025, but short-term stays remain taxable. HB 2429 (pending early 2026) would extend the violation-record window from 12 to 24 months.
jurisdictions.source: City of Phoenix Short-Term Rental Registry (phoenix.gov); Arizona Department of Revenue (azdor.gov); strprofitmap.com; bnbcalc.com; minut.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — annual City of Flagstaff Short-Term Rental License required (effective November 1, 2023). State TPT license also required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Arizona TPT license from AZDOR required for all STR properties; license number must appear in all advertisements.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual STR license fee $180 (initial $175, increasing $5 per year). Verify current year amount with the city.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Owners must ensure guests comply with occupancy limits; specific city figure not clearly sourced. Unknown.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: STR operators remit sales tax and transient occupancy (BBB) tax at the same rates as hotels; exact combined rate Unknown — verify with city/AZDOR.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Under Arizona SB 1350, Flagstaff cannot ban STRs in residential zones; conditional use permit requirement was eliminated by state preemption.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as any residence rented for less than 30 days; no specified minimum-night floor. Unknown.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Not mandated by city ordinance, but Arizona state law requires minimum $500,000 liability coverage. Safety equipment (smoke/CO detectors, fire extinguishers, deadbolts) strongly encouraged.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: License number must be included in all advertisements. Platforms (Airbnb, VRBO) required to collect and remit state and local taxes; operators remain responsible for ensuring payment.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Must designate a 24-hour emergency point of contact and notify each adjacent single-family residential property in writing with the STR license number and contact details. Building permits required for converting accessory structures. Licenses valid one year and renewed annually.
jurisdictions.effective_date: November 1, 2023 (license requirement effective); ordinance enacted November 2023.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Application returned if requirements (emergency contact, neighbor notification, TPT proof, fee) incomplete. City uses an authorized third-party provider for monitoring unlicensed operations.
jurisdictions.penalties: Hosts who fail to register after receiving notice from the city can be fined up to $1,000 per month.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Flagstaff had ~1,045 STRs at adoption (731 already registered). Note Coconino County has separate rules beyond city limits. HOA rules not enforced by the city — verify separately.
jurisdictions.source: City of Flagstaff Official Website (flagstaff.az.gov STR pages); Avalara/MyLodgeTax; theoffersheet.com; bnbcalc.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Short-Term Rental Unit (STRU) permit required for all vacation rentals; three permit categories including Owner-Occupied (one per person, owner need not be present). Permits valid one year, non-transferable.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Tennessee Sales Tax Certificate and Hotel Occupancy Tax registration required; city/county business licenses also required.
jurisdictions.fees: Application, annual licensing, and possible inspection fees apply; exact amounts vary — verify with the Pigeon Forge Community Development Office. Unknown specific figure.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No general mandate — owner-occupied is only one of three permit types; non-owner-occupied permits available subject to zoning.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Maximum of two guests per bed and two beds per room, capped at no more than 12 persons per property.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: City hotel occupancy (privilege) tax 2.50%; total sales tax 9.75%.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Significant — STRs limited to certain zoning districts. In the R-1 District (Ordinance No. 1065), residences may operate as STRs only if they were operating as such on or before August 13, 2018. New STR development restricted in many residential neighborhoods; overnight rentals generally allowed in R-2 and higher zones.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STRs defined as rentals of residential units/dwellings for less than 30 consecutive days. No specified minimum-night floor identified. Unknown.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Effective limits via zoning (R-1 grandfathering). No explicit numeric cap on permits per owner identified.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Insurance coverage to protect against liability and property damage is required per city guidance; exact limits Unknown. Property insurance documentation expected.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms like Airbnb/VRBO may remit certain taxes, but hosts remain responsible for ensuring all applicable taxes are paid.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Pre-issuance/renewal inspection required for compliance with building, fire safety, and health codes (smoke alarms, CO detectors, fire extinguishers). Must designate a responsible local agent. Structures over 5 sleeping rooms, sleeping accommodations for >12 occupants, >5,000 sq ft, or >3 stories must meet R-1 (hotel/motel) occupancy requirements (sprinklers, exits, guardrails).
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ordinance No. 1065 (R-1 provisions reference August 13, 2018 cutoff). Sevier County (outside city limits) annual permit + inspection program effective January 1, 2024.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Good Neighbor Policy (noise minimization, quiet hours) applies; adequate guest parking required. Permits are property-specific and non-transferable.
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown — enforcement via inspection/permit denial and code compliance; specific fine schedule not sourced.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Premier Smoky Mountains tourist market (Dollywood, The Island). STRs outside city limits in Sevier County require an annual permit and yearly safety inspection.
jurisdictions.source: City of Pigeon Forge (cityofpigeonforgetn.gov Building Department); Sevier County (seviercountytn.gov); gosummer.com; theoffersheet.com; airbtics.com; getchalet.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Short-Term Rental Property Permit required (Ordinance No. 5631, City Code Chapter 5-44) before operating or advertising; a separate permit required for each property. Permits non-transferable, expire 365 days after issuance.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register with the Shelby County Trustee's Office and the Tennessee Department of Revenue (TNTAP); collect/remit state sales and local occupancy taxes monthly (due by the 20th).
jurisdictions.fees: Non-refundable application fee $75 cited in ordinance; later reporting indicates $300 initial / $150 renewal under a 2023 amendment; Shelby County renewal fee $50/year. Verify current fee with the Memphis Permit Office. (Sources conflict.)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: No more than three sleeping rooms permitted per STR property. A widely applied (non-ordinance) working standard is two people per bedroom plus two additional guests.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Memphis room/hotel occupancy tax 5% (city hotel-motel tax raised to 4% in April 2025 per one source); total sales tax 9.75%. Verify current combined rate.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs must be in permitted zoning districts; check zoning maps before applying. No neighborhood-level outright ban, but parcels rezoned from residential to commercial face an 18-month waiting period before a permit can issue. Some downtown condo HOAs/CC&Rs prohibit STRs.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Defined as transient occupancy of less than 30 continuous days; no compensation permitted for occupancy of less than 24 hours (day-use/hourly rentals prohibited).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Unknown — no explicit per-block cap identified for Memphis.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of homeowner's fire, hazard, and liability insurance with liability limits of not less than $1 million per occurrence.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb/VRBO) may collect and remit taxes, but operators must verify compliance directly with tax authorities. Listing cannot advertise without a permit.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Must designate a Short Term Rental Agent (natural person, at least 21) who must appear on premises within 2 hours of notification of issues. Must comply with noise control (City Code §22-1), garbage rules, and building/fire safety codes (UL 217 smoke alarms; Shelby County requires a 5-lb ABC fire extinguisher per unit). Inspection by Memphis/Shelby County Code Enforcement required.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ordinance No. 5631 (Chapter 5-44) effective July 1, 2023; enforcement of updated rules began 2023.
jurisdictions.key_notes: STR defined as a residential dwelling unit, portion thereof, or detached ADU with no more than three sleeping rooms rented for under 30 continuous days. Principal renter must be at least 18.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a permit: fine of $50.00 per day per unit plus applicable court costs.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Applies inside Memphis and unincorporated Shelby County. Tennessee state law prohibits local governments from banning STRs outright. Fee/tax figures vary across sources — confirm with the issuing office.
jurisdictions.source: Memphis Code of Ordinances Chapter 5-44 (municode/granicus); Shelby County STR Permit Application (shelbycountytn.gov); steadily.com; getchalet.com; staywithlps.com; awning.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a nightly rental / business license is required to legally operate. Operating a nightly rental without a license is a Class A misdemeanor under Moab ordinance (cited as 5.67). Application process takes roughly 3–6 weeks.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register with the Utah State Tax Commission; may need local tax accounts. Operators must collect/remit sales tax, Transient Room Tax, and tourism tax regardless of license status.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown — verify business/nightly rental license fees with City of Moab.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Not applicable in most zones since new residential STRs are prohibited; Unknown for grandfathered/permitted properties.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown — Moab maintains strict safety standards; specific occupancy figure not clearly sourced.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Transient Room Tax 4.25% of rental fee (excluding cleaning) plus a flat per-night tourism tax; Utah base sales tax 4.85% (effective local rate often above 7%). Combined lodging taxes typically exceed 10%.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Among the strictest in Utah. Residential short-term rentals prohibited in numerous zones (A-2, C-1, C-3, C-5, FW, I-1, R&D-1, R-2, R-3, R-4, RA-1, and all zones where STRs are not listed as a permitted use), per MMC 17.09.700. Only resort zones or grandfathered/legally existing properties may operate; a moratorium blocks new construction for STR use.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as occupancy/lease for an effective term of 30 consecutive calendar days or less; pivot to 30-day-minimum (medium-term) leases is common where STRs are banned.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Effective via zoning and moratorium — new builds for STR use barred; legal STRs largely confined to existing licensed projects, purpose-built condo/townhome communities with recorded STR rights, or county parcels with Overnight Accommodations Overlays (OAO).
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of insurance required as part of the application (along with a site plan and local contact).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Advertising an unlicensed nightly rental can trigger code enforcement and fines. Utah HB 256 closed the prior loophole — cities can now use online listings as direct enforcement evidence and demand platforms remove listings lacking a valid local license/zoning permit.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Application requires proof of insurance, a site plan, and a designated local contact. Strict fire-safety rules apply (detectors, clear exits). Rules differ inside Moab city limits versus unincorporated Grand County (county generally more permissive but still requires business licenses).
jurisdictions.effective_date: MMC 17.09.700 amended by Ordinance No. 2024-03 (passed June 11, 2024); construction moratorium dates to 2019 and has been extended.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Most standard in-town residential neighborhoods cannot be newly converted to nightly rentals. Existing licensed projects and special overlays are where most legal nightly rentals exist. HOA CC&Rs may also prohibit STRs.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating a nightly rental without a license is a Class A misdemeanor; advertising unlicensed rentals can trigger code enforcement and fines.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Utah has no statewide STR license or definition — authority is delegated to localities. Moab faces tourism (~3 million visitors) and housing-shortage pressures driving restrictions. Always confirm zoning/licensing in writing with City of Moab or Grand County Planning & Zoning before buying or advertising.
jurisdictions.source: Moab Municipal Code 17.09.700 (moab.municipal.codes); City of Moab Ordinance No. 2024-03 (moabcity.gov); hometeamluxuryrentals.com; searchsaltlakeareahomes.com; theoffersheet.com; checkmaterentals.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes for permitted zones (business license), but the City does not currently issue business licenses for STRs in residential zones
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — a Utah sales tax collection/accounting number is required for STR operations
jurisdictions.fees: Equivalent to general business license fee (Unknown exact amount for SLC; county uses general business license fee schedule)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Unknown for SLC specifically; under-30-day STRs not allowed in residential zones regardless of occupancy
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (county code limits STRs to no more than four bedrooms)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown (state and local transient/sales taxes apply)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs only allowed in zones listing 'short term rental' as a permitted use; generally not allowed in Residential zones but allowed in Mixed-Use, Downtown, and Gateway zones
jurisdictions.min_stay: Rentals of less than 30 days are classified as STRs; 30+ day rentals treated as residential tenancy
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: State law prohibits local governments from punishing individuals solely for listing/offering an STR on an STR website
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: City has strengthened enforcement, including adopting data-gathering software to identify STR properties; state law limits using online listings alone as evidence of violations
jurisdictions.effective_date: Unknown (ordinance to allow STRs in residential districts not yet adopted)
jurisdictions.key_notes: Under-30-day STRs in standard dwelling units or ADUs in residential areas are not allowed; treated as hotel/motel/bed-and-breakfast uses. Note many 'SLC' addresses fall in neighboring cities (South Salt Lake, North Salt Lake) with separate, more permissive ordinances
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown for SLC specifically
jurisdictions.additional_context: Until an ordinance establishing regulations is adopted, STRs are not eligible for a business license in residential districts; viable alternatives are lodging uses in appropriately zoned areas or 30+ day rentals
jurisdictions.source: slc.gov/planning Planning Division FAQ; slcdocs.com STR document; strprofitmap.com SLC guide; Salt Lake County Municode Ch. 5.19 & 19.04.547

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a Transient Vacation Rental (TVR) Certificate (in VDA) or Non-Conforming Use (NCU) Certificate (outside VDA) required
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Hawaii General Excise Tax (GET) and Transient Accommodations Tax (TAT) licenses/registration required
jurisdictions.fees: Approximately $750 (plus tax) per TVR license, renewed annually
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No for TVRs (homestays in VDAs require owner present on-site)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (homestays limited to up to three guest rooms)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: TAT 10.25%; GET approximately 4–4.712%
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: TVRs allowed only in designated Visitor Destination Areas (VDAs) — Princeville, parts of Kapaa/Waipouli, parts of Poipu, small section of Waimea; outside VDAs only with grandfathered NCU permit
jurisdictions.min_stay: Rentals of less than 180 consecutive days classified as TVRs
jurisdictions.density_rules: No new NCU certificates issued for non-VDA properties since March 30, 2009 (caps non-VDA supply; ~438 TVNC dwellings grandfathered)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: County publishes a list of approved homestays & non-conforming TVRs by Tax Map Key (TMK); buyers should confirm status with County of Kauai Planning Department. TVR permits must be renewed annually with no grace period (strict policy)
jurisdictions.key_notes: Renewal applications must be submitted at least 60 days before expiration; missing renewal deadline forfeits licensing. May 2024 Senate Bill 2919 granted counties greater authority to phase out STRs in residential/agricultural zones
jurisdictions.penalties: Civil infraction fines; serious/serial offenders may face court action and criminal charges
jurisdictions.additional_context: TVR permits/NCUs transfer with property ownership; new owners must register updated info with the county. Over 3,000 active TVR properties islandwide
jurisdictions.source: kauai.gov Planning Dept TVR page; hawaiilife.com TVR guide; aekkauai.com; dinkabojanova.com; checkmaterentals.com Hawaii STR guide

jurisdictions.license_required: Pending — Reno requires an STR permit per its regulation, but the city is still finalizing its ordinance/licensing process; a Nevada state business license is required
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register to collect/remit Nevada sales tax, Washoe County room tax, and Reno room tax (via Reno-Sparks Convention & Visitors Authority)
jurisdictions.fees: New fee schedule began October 1, 2023 (Unknown exact amounts)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Occupancy limits apply per local ordinance (Unknown specific figure)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown (Nevada sales tax plus Washoe County and Reno room taxes)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Reno mandates STRs be 660 feet from other residential units and 2,500 feet from resort hotels
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR permit required for rentals shorter than 28 consecutive days
jurisdictions.density_rules: 660-foot separation from other residential units; 2,500-foot separation from resort hotels
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Likely — most Nevada jurisdictions require liability insurance with minimum $500,000 coverage per occurrence (confirmed for unincorporated Washoe County; Reno specifics pending)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: City of Reno and City of Sparks have separate jurisdiction; Washoe County Planning cannot issue permits for properties within Reno city limits. Enforcement noted as not strictly applied with low licensing compliance
jurisdictions.effective_date: AB363 (statewide framework) effective July 1, 2022; new Reno fee schedule October 1, 2023
jurisdictions.key_notes: Governed under Nevada Assembly Bill 363 (2021), requiring local governments (pop. 25,000+) to regulate STRs; Reno still developing specific ordinances
jurisdictions.penalties: Substantial fines for non-compliance; permit revocation for repeated violations
jurisdictions.additional_context: Events/large gatherings restricted at STR properties; STRs cannot host large parties without special permits. Washoe County limits owners to no more than five permits (county jurisdiction)
jurisdictions.source: steadily.com Reno STR laws; airbtics.com Reno; proper.insure Nevada; bnbcalc.com Reno guide; checkmaterentals.com Nevada

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Short-Term Rental Operator's License plus a Seattle business license tax certificate
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — business license tax certificate and lodging tax registration required
jurisdictions.fees: $75 per unit per year for operator's license; RRIO registration approximately $70 for secondary/dedicated units; business license tax certificate based on gross receipts (minimum approximately $55)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — if operating two units, one must be the operator's primary residence (lived in more than six months/year)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Unknown (no specific nightly occupancy figure; capped at 2 dwelling units per operator)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Combined sales/lodging tax approximately 10.35% (some sources cite ~15.7% combined state and local lodging taxes)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed in most residential and mixed-use zones; prohibited in non-dwelling spaces (RVs, tents, garages, boats), live-work units, caretaker's quarters, houseboats/waterfront residences where lodging is prohibited by Shoreline Code
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as rental for fewer than 30 consecutive nights
jurisdictions.density_rules: Most operators limited to two dwelling units total (primary residence + one secondary unit); legacy exceptions for STRs operated before September 2017
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Unknown (liability insurance recommended; not confirmed as mandated)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: License number and expiration date must be displayed on all listings; platforms require license info to list
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Dedicated (non-primary) units must register with and comply with the Rental Registration and Inspection Ordinance (RRIO) including periodic inspections; primary residences exempt from RRIO registration/inspection
jurisdictions.effective_date: City began issuing STR regulatory licenses January 2, 2019
jurisdictions.key_notes: Operator licenses valid one year and must be renewed annually. License issued to operator, not individual listing; rented rooms without their own kitchen/bathroom do not count toward the two-unit limit
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a license can result in fines (sources cite $500–$1,000 per violation); $513 citation for operating without a business license tax certificate; enforcement via audits and listing cross-checks
jurisdictions.additional_context: ADUs/DADUs on the same parcel as a primary residence are separate dwelling units requiring their own license; properties must meet basic habitability and safety standards
jurisdictions.source: seattle.gov Business Regulations STR page; airbnb.com/help/article/869; steadily.com Seattle 2026; beenstay.com unit cap; theoffersheet.com Seattle

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Accessory Short-Term Rental (ASTR) permit from Portland Permitting & Development (Type A or Type B); city business license registration also required
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — transient lodging tax (city/county and state) registration required
jurisdictions.fees: Type B permit costs $245 (Type A fee Unknown exact amount; application fees non-refundable)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — resident must occupy the dwelling unit at least 270 days (nine months) per calendar year
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No cap on nights bedrooms may be rented; maximum 95 days/year when the resident is not present (entire-home/away periods)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Type A: 1–2 bedrooms, max 5 guests; Type B: 3–5 bedrooms, up to 10 guests (rentals of 6+ bedrooms are not ASTRs)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Oregon state Transient Lodging Tax 1.5% plus local Portland/Multnomah County lodging taxes (Unknown exact combined rate)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs allowed in residential zones only as accessory to a primary residential use; whole-house rentals without a 270-day resident not allowed in residential zones; zoning code Section 33.207 governs ASTRs. As of late 2024 city no longer issues new Type B permits in commercial/mixed-use, employment and industrial zones
jurisdictions.min_stay: Rentals of fewer than 30 consecutive days
jurisdictions.density_rules: Unknown (no stated separation/saturation rule for Portland; effectively limited by primary-residence requirement)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Hosts must post permit number prominently in listings; platforms face regulations to report unpermitted rentals
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Residency verified via Oregon Driver's License/ID showing the site address; Type A requires neighborhood notice to neighbors and neighborhood associations; Type B requires conditional use review/land use hearing. City maintains a short-term rental registry on Portland Maps and scrapes listings to find unpermitted rentals
jurisdictions.effective_date: Original ASTR rules enacted 2014; new safety standards effective January 1, 2025
jurisdictions.key_notes: Two permit types: Type A (1–2 bedrooms, administrative) and Type B (3–5 bedrooms, conditional use). Owner-occupancy requirement effectively bans whole-house investment rentals in residential zones
