jurisdictions.penalties: Fines (often several hundred dollars per violation; each day can be a separate violation) and permit revocation; one operator was fined $115,823 for 18 violations — Ombudsman has recommended warnings before fines and lower first-time caps
jurisdictions.additional_context: Resident may use an operator to manage the ASTR but must still occupy the dwelling 270 days/year; on sites with an ADU, residents may occupy either the primary unit or the ADU. Bedrooms in legal detached accessory structures (guest houses) may also be rented
jurisdictions.source: portland.gov ASTR permit pages (Before You Apply, Type A, ASTR Permits); portland.gov Ombudsman report 2026; hostaway.com Oregon 2026; cascadiagetaways.com Oregon guide; hometeamluxuryrentals.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — both a land use permit (Type I or Type II) from the Planning Division and a Short-Term Rental Operating License are required. A STR is a dwelling rented for less than 30 consecutive days.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — operators must register with the City Tax Division for transient room tax collection (Type II rentals are subject to room tax; some Type I exempt).
jurisdictions.fees: Annual transportation fee for STR units increased from $100 to $200 effective July 1, 2025; 'whole house' supplemental fee applies to whole-house STRs. Phase 2 Transportation Fee rates adopted May 7, 2025.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Only for Type I owner-occupied permits (renting up to two bedrooms in a primary residence). Type II whole-house rentals do not require owner occupancy.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Type I whole-house rentals limited to a maximum of 30 days per year (up to four rental periods). Type II has no annual day cap.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Two guests per bedroom plus two additional guests (per industry tracker).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 10.4% City of Bend transient room tax plus 1.8% State of Oregon tax (per industry source; state base TLT is 1.5%).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Permitted in RL, RS, RM, RH zoning districts and MR district (outside Old Mill District); Type designations vary by zone. Mixed-use zones treated differently.
jurisdictions.density_rules: 500-foot separation required between Type II whole-house STRs (measured property line to property line) in residential zones; applies where cumulative STR days reach 30+ per year.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Minimum $500,000 liability insurance for Type II rentals (per industry tracker).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Permits are owner-specific and do not run with the land — they terminate on sale/transfer; new owners must reapply. Off-street parking required (one space per rented bedroom); quiet hours 10 PM–7 AM; screened trash/recycling. Long-term rental exemption available for up to three renewals.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Original STR provisions effective April 15, 2015; most recently amended by Ordinance NS-2541, codified December 2025.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Bend has one of the strictest STR frameworks in Oregon. Two permit types: Type I (administrative/owner-occupied) and Type II (whole-house, density-restricted).
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a permit may result in enforcement actions, fines, and denial of future permit applications. Code violations processed under BDC Chapter 1.3; repeated complaints can lead to permit revocation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: As of July 2025, roughly 1,325 Airbnb listings existed in Bend; enforcement is complaint-based with periodic audits. City ended an informal blanket-license agreement with Meredith Lodging in 2025.
jurisdictions.source: City of Bend (bendoregon.gov/services/business/short-term-rentals); Bend Development Code 3.6.500 (bend.municipal.codes); OPB (Jun 2025); Airbtics

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — annual Short-Term Rental registration with the Inspectional Services Department (ISD) plus a business certificate from the City Clerk's Office. STR defined as renting a residential unit for less than 28 consecutive days.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — operators must collect and remit occupancy taxes; registration with state for room occupancy excise applies.
jurisdictions.fees: $200 annual STR registration fee; $65 business certificate fee (valid four years).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — STRs only allowed in owner-occupied properties; owner must live in the unit at least nine months per year. Investment properties are ineligible.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No annual cap for Home Share and Owner-Adjacent units (up to 365 days/year). Limited Share units capped at 60 nights/year per some sources.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown (state room occupancy excise plus local/Boston add-ons apply; specific Boston rate not confirmed in sources).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Only owner-occupied condominiums, single-family, two-family, and three-family buildings eligible (owner must own all units in 2-3 family buildings). Banned in income-restricted/affordable units; ineligible in 'Problem Properties.'
jurisdictions.min_stay: N/A — rentals are for less than 28 consecutive days by definition.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Operator may only offer one whole unit listing at a time. In 2-3 family buildings, limits apply on number of units rented.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Hosts required to maintain liability insurance of at least $1,000,000 (per industry source).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (e.g., Airbnb) require the registration number and remove non-compliant listings; booking agents regulated under the ordinance.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Three STR categories: Limited Share (private bedroom, owner present), Home Share (entire primary residence), Owner-Adjacent (secondary unit in owner-occupied 2-3 family). Must display registration number in all ads; notify abutters (within 300 ft) within 30 days; designate a local contact who responds in person within two hours; post fire safety signage; retain records three years. Units with 3+ violations in six months are ineligible.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ordinance passed June 2018; registration opened January 1, 2019.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Among the most restrictive STR regimes in the U.S.; effectively eliminates investor-owned STRs.
jurisdictions.penalties: Penalties range from warning letters to fines and revocation of registration; registration suspended while violations exist.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Mandatory health/safety inspection required before approval; enforcement by ISD. Mayor Wu administration supports continued housing-preservation focus.
jurisdictions.source: City of Boston (boston.gov/.../short-term-rentals); Boston Code of Ordinances 9-14.5 (codelibrary.amlegal.com); Analyze Boston STR Eligibility dataset; BNBCalc; proper.insure

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — annual rental registration with the Town of Barnstable Health Division (Board of Health) under Chapter 170 Rental Properties. No comprehensive STR-specific licensing ordinance currently in force.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — STR operators must register with the Massachusetts Department of Revenue (DOR) for tax collection; DOR certificate required with town application.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual town registration fee: $90 for the first unit and $25 for each additional unit on the same parcel.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No (no primary-residence requirement under current town registration).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 14.45% total — 5.7% Massachusetts state excise, 6% Town of Barnstable local excise, and 2.75% Cape Cod & Islands Water Protection Fund excise (all remitted to DOR).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: No STR-specific zoning amendment in force — a proposed 2020 zoning amendment recognizing STR as a permitted residential use was withdrawn by the Town Council on November 5, 2020.
jurisdictions.min_stay: Unknown (a proposed ordinance had floated a three-night minimum, but it was withdrawn).
jurisdictions.density_rules: None currently in force (a withdrawn 2020 proposal would have limited owners to two STR properties and capped town-wide STRs at 1,500).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Annual registration must be renewed each year. Health/safety inspection may be required (typically at first registration and periodically thereafter). Operators must also register with MA DOR for room occupancy excise.
jurisdictions.effective_date: MA state room occupancy excise on STRs effective July 1, 2019; town annual registration under Chapter 170 ongoing.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Cape Cod / Barnstable takes a comparatively hands-off, Airbnb-friendly approach versus Boston; a comprehensive STR general ordinance and zoning amendment (Items 2020-192 and 2020-193) were withdrawn in November 2020.
jurisdictions.penalties: Failure to register can result in a non-criminal citation and a $100 fine; the Health Division conducts compliance checks and responds to complaint-hotline reports.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Note: 'Barnstable' here refers to the Town of Barnstable; other Cape Cod towns (e.g., Provincetown) have separate, often stricter rules. A June 2025 Nantucket Land Court decision on owner-occupancy could influence future Cape zoning.
jurisdictions.source: Town of Barnstable Planning & Development (barnstable.gov); BNBCalc Cape Cod guide; CapeCod.com (Sep 2020); acapecodhome.com (2025)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — all STR units must be individually registered under the Shared Housing Ordinance; hotel license numbers are non-transferable
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — operators register with the Illinois Department of Revenue and collect lodging taxes; as of July 1, 2025, all Illinois STRs are subject to the state Hotel Operators' Occupation Tax on stays under 30 consecutive days.
jurisdictions.fees: $125 non-refundable registration fee (single unit); $250 for the Shared Housing Unit Operator License (valid two years).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Often yes — for many single-family homes and 2-4 flats, the unit must be the host's primary residence; non-primary listings may require a Vacation Rental License instead.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: 2 guests per sleeping room (children under 18 not counted), OR 1 person per 125 sq ft, OR building-code occupancy — whichever is lowest. Units must have six or fewer sleeping rooms.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Chicago Hotel Accommodation Tax 4.5%; Vacation Rental/Shared Housing Surcharge 4%; Cook County tax 1%; Domestic Violence Surcharge 2% (shared housing units); Illinois Hotel Operators Occupation Tax ~5.98-6.17%; plus other state taxes ~5.73%.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Cannot operate in Restricted Residential Zones (long-term rentals over 32 days only); cannot be on the Prohibited Buildings List (2,300+ buildings), Scofflaw List, or Problem Landlord List; HOA/lease must permit STRs.
jurisdictions.min_stay: N/A — STRs are rentals of 31 days or fewer.
jurisdictions.density_rules: For buildings with five or more housing units, STRs capped at one-quarter of units or six units, whichever is fewer.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Hosts commonly carry $1,000,000 commercial general liability coverage (per industry trackers; verify current requirement with BACP).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb alleged to have processed bookings for unregistered/unlicensed units; city seeks injunctive relief to block future facilitation of unlawful rentals
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Lawsuit is the first time Chicago has sued Airbnb directly; signals escalation from citation-based enforcement to platform-level litigation.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Administered by the Department of Business Affairs and Consumer Protection (BACP). The old 'Vacation Rental' license type was largely replaced by the Shared Housing Registration framework.
jurisdictions.penalties: City seeking fines, disgorgement of profits tied to violations, and injunctive relief to block future illegal STR activity
jurisdictions.additional_context: As of July 2025, ~6,915 Airbnb listings in Chicago with ~90% holding registration; comprehensive approach reportedly reduced listings ~16.4%.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a Commercial Activity License (free) plus either a Limited Lodging Operator License (primary residence) or a Rental License with hotel designation (non-primary residence/Visitor Accommodation), plus a Zoning Permit. STR = stays of 30 consecutive days or less.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — operators must obtain a Philadelphia business tax account ID and register for a Business Income and Receipts Tax (BIRT) account; Hotel Tax collected/remitted monthly.
jurisdictions.fees: Limited Lodging Operator License $150/year ($20 non-refundable application fee applied toward it); Rental License with hotel designation $63 per dwelling unit annually; Zoning Permit $174 per permitted use.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: For Limited Lodging — yes, owner/tenant must live in the property as primary residence more than half the year. Visitor Accommodations (no primary resident) use the Rental License with hotel designation instead.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Limited Lodging: fewer than 180 days annually (per industry sources).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Limited Lodging cannot be occupied by more than three people (including owner and renters) who are not related by blood, marriage, life partnership, adoption, or foster status.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Hotel Tax 8.5% of gross rental receipts; plus BIRT (gross receipts and net income portions, ~5.99% net income) and Net Profits Tax where applicable.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Limited Lodging permitted as an accessory use in most residential zones (standards in 14-604(13)(c)); Visitor Accommodations need a visitor accommodation zoning permit and are generally in commercial zones. Tenth Council District: primary resident must also be the owner.
jurisdictions.min_stay: N/A — STRs are 30 consecutive days or less.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Minimum $500,000 liability coverage recommended (per industry tracker; not confirmed as a strict legal mandate).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Must use a licensed Limited Lodging and Hotels Booking Agent; license number must be displayed in all advertisements. Airbnb collects the 8.5% Hotel Tax.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Limited Lodging Operator License issued only to natural persons (not LLCs/business entities). Smoke alarms in each bedroom, hallways near bedrooms, and every floor including basements; CO alarms within 15 ft of bedroom entrances. Lead Safety Certification for pre-1978 properties. Guests allowed only 8 a.m.–midnight; retain records (primary residence, rental dates, number of renters) for at least one year. Certificate of Occupancy may be required (e.g., >5 bedrooms).
jurisdictions.effective_date: Current framework ongoing; new mandatory inspection requirement for Limited Lodging licenses began June 1, 2025, with virtual inspections at each application/renewal starting July 1, 2025.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Philadelphia distinguishes Limited Lodging (primary residence) from Visitor Accommodation (investment/non-primary). Applications via the eCLIPSE system.
jurisdictions.penalties: Late renewal incurs 1.5% of license fee per month after 60 days; license revoked if outstanding fees unpaid within 30 days; renting without a license risks fines and inability to evict/collect rent.
jurisdictions.additional_context: As of July 2025, ~5,128 Airbnb listings in Philadelphia, ~90% holding licenses. Issued by Department of Licenses & Inspections (L&I).
jurisdictions.source: City of Philadelphia (phila.gov short-term rental services & Limited Lodging FAQ Rev 9.25); Airbnb Help Center; philadelphiazoning.com; BNBCalc

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a Short-Term Rental License (STRL) is required for each unit; the STRL must be obtained and posted on all advertisements.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register with the Georgia Department of Revenue and the City of Atlanta; an Occupation Tax Certificate is also referenced for STR operators.
jurisdictions.fees: $150 annual license fee per unit (renewed annually).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — STRs are generally limited to a host's primary residence plus one additional dwelling unit; non-owner-occupied investment STRs are technically prohibited under the primary ordinance.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Unknown (no explicit nightly cap; STRs defined as stays of 30 consecutive days or less).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 8% Hotel-Motel Tax on stays under 30 days, plus 4% Georgia state sales tax and 3% local option sales tax.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs permitted across residential zones (R1–R5) with primary-residence verification; ADUs subject to same standards; some neighborhood/historic overlay restrictions reported by third parties.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None specified (STR = stays not exceeding 30 consecutive days).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Each host limited to two STR licenses citywide (primary residence + one additional dwelling). No general density cap confirmed in the city ordinance.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Partially — third-party sources indicate liability insurance is required as part of the STRL process; the city's own guidance does not always state a mandate. $1M+ commercial liability commonly advised.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Hosting platforms must provide information to aid enforcement and removal of illegal listings; platforms (e.g., Airbnb) require Atlanta hosts to display registration numbers and may delist non-compliant listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Ordinance 20-O-1656 adopted March 15, 2021; rules effective March 1, 2022; enforcement effective March 5, 2023. A 24/7 contact (STR agent) responsive within ~2 hours required; no large parties/events; noise and parking rules apply. Complaint-based enforcement with periodic sweeps.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Generous relative to many major cities — allows primary residence plus one additional dwelling. World Cup 2026 expected to drive increased registration enforcement.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines starting at $500 per violation, listing removal, and suspension/revocation of license; escalating fines for repeat violations and possible legal action.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Applies only within City of Atlanta limits; unincorporated Fulton/DeKalb and surrounding cities have separate, often less restrictive rules. HOA/condo covenants may impose additional limits.
jurisdictions.source: City of Atlanta (atl311.com KB0013809; atlantaga.gov STR page; Municode Pt.20); Steadily; Safely; Truvi; RentStellar

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — an annual STR (Short-Term Vacation Rental / STVR) certificate is required for any STR.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — an Occupational Tax Certificate (OTC) application or exemption form is required with the STR application; 7% local occupational room tax must be remitted.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown (specific certificate/OTC fee amounts not sourced).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — not a primary-residence model, but a phase-out framework: certificates are non-transferable on sale, death, or marriage.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Varies by home — occupancy limit set per the size of the home and number of designated off-street parking spaces.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 7% local occupational room tax, remitted to the City Finance Department by the 20th of the following month (monthly returns required even if no tax is due).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Proposed four-zone density model: east/south zones capped at 60% STRs, west/north zones capped at 30% STRs
jurisdictions.min_stay: None specified (STR = transient guests for less than 30 consecutive days).
jurisdictions.density_rules: 2024 ordinance moratorium on new permits in residential zones and prohibits license transfers; pending 2026 proposal introduces zone-based percentage caps and a waiting-list system.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown (not specified in city sources).
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Proposal must clear planning commission before city council vote; the current 2024 non-transferable-certificate ordinance remains in effect in the interim. Outcome uncertain.
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of Georgia's most regulated/controversial STR markets. ~1,575 of 2,994 housing units were registered vacation rentals as of 2024. A May 2026 amendment proposing four-zone caps was before the planning commission (not yet adopted).
jurisdictions.penalties: Citations/fines for noise or nuisance violations; possible eviction by owner/manager; non-renewal/forfeiture of certificate.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Status is in flux: phase-out rules from 2024 may be partly reversed by the pending 2026 four-zone proposal; outcome of the lawsuit could reshape the framework.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — all STRs need a Business Registration plus either a Residential Short-Term Rental Permit or a Non-Residential STR Registration, depending on zoning.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — a New Mexico CRS/GRT (gross receipts) number is required, and city lodgers' tax applies.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown (specific permit/registration fee amounts not sourced; late renewal fee applies after the standard deadline).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No primary-residence requirement, but residential permits are limited to one per natural person.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Seven-day rule: a unit may be rented only once in a seven-day period, except between Nov 15 and Jan 15 when the rule is waived.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Maximum guests equals twice the number of bedrooms.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: City lodgers' tax of 7% (5% occupancy tax + 2% convention center fee), plus New Mexico gross receipts tax (GRT).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Residential STRs require a permit; cannot be within 50 feet of another residential STR (proximity rule). In multi-unit developments of 4+ units, no more than 25% of units may be STRs and no more than 12 permits per multi-unit structure. Non-residential zones require a registration with no cap (except 12-per-building rule).
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (STR = any dwelling unit rented for less than 30 calendar days); subject to the seven-day frequency rule instead.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Citywide cap of 1,000 residential STR permits; permits non-transferable on sale (limited spousal/domestic-partner transfer on death); 50-ft proximity rule; 25%/12-permit multi-unit limits.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of insurance is among the required application documents.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Owner must post permit/registration number on all advertisements including Airbnb and Vrbo; if a permit/registration is invalid, the platform must remove the listing within 5 days.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Permits expire Dec 31; renewal season Jan 1–Apr 15 (deadline historically March 15 with April late option; 2026 renewal deadline April 15). Local operator must be available 24/7 and able to be on-site within 1 hour of a complaint. Neighbor notification (within 200 ft) by first-class mail within 10 days of issuance/renewal, plus affidavit. Parking: 1 space (1BR), 2 spaces (2+BR).
jurisdictions.effective_date: 2021 (city framework; current renewal cycle for CY2026)
jurisdictions.key_notes: Properties outside city limits fall under Santa Fe County's separate STR ordinance (2022-07, effective Nov 24, 2022), which uses owner-occupied vs. non-owner-occupied categories and CDP-level caps (e.g., 7% of housing stock in some CDPs).
jurisdictions.penalties: Listing removal by platforms for invalid permits; standard city enforcement for non-compliance (specific fine amounts not sourced).
jurisdictions.additional_context: Dual system — City of Santa Fe vs. Santa Fe County rules differ. HOA CC&Rs may be stricter than the city ordinance. New residential permits issued first-come, first-served subject to the 1,000 cap.
jurisdictions.source: City of Santa Fe (santafenm.gov STR pages); New Mexico STRA; Santa Fe County (santafecountynm.gov; Ordinance 2022-07 FAQ); STRProfitMap

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a City of Bozeman STR hosting permit is required (with annual renewal), plus a Montana state-issued Public Accommodation/Accommodations License via the Gallatin City-County Health Department and a Montana Dept. of Revenue lodging tax account.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register with the Montana Department of Revenue (Account ID ending in 'LFT' required in the STR portal) and set up a lodging tax account.
jurisdictions.fees: Unknown (city permit fee not sourced; separate fire inspection fee applies).
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes for most current categories — owner-occupied Types (1 and 2A) require the home to be the host's primary residence ≥70% of the calendar year; Type 2B requires the host's primary residence on the same lot/building. Only Legacy Type 3 (pre-Dec 14, 2023) does not require primary residence.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Maximum capacity determined by STR type and number of sleeping areas (per city regulations; specific figures vary by unit).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 8% Montana lodging tax (4% Lodging Facility Use Tax + 4% Lodging Sales Tax); Gallatin Valley TBID per-room-night fee also applies in Bozeman.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs restricted to certain zoning districts; new Type 3 (non-owner-occupied whole-home) STRs prohibited citywide as of Dec 14, 2023; Type 1 broadly allowed, Type 2 allowed in most districts but not RS/R1 (per third-party summary). Legacy Type 3 only allowed in non-residential zones.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Ordinance 2149 (effective Dec 14, 2023) bans new non-owner-occupied (Type 3) STRs; ~100 existing permitted Type 3 units grandfathered as 'Legacy Type 3.' Permits are non-transferable — sale/transfer terminates the registration and a new STR cannot be re-established for prohibited types.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Not mandated by the city; the city notes standard homeowner's insurance may not cover STR use and advises adequate coverage.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms (Airbnb, Vrbo) must require the city permit number on listings and remove listings lacking a valid number; platforms must share listing data with the city.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Ordinance 1974 (2017) created the three-type system; Ordinance 2131 (effective Aug 11, 2023) added compliance rules; Ordinance 2149 (effective Dec 14, 2023) banned new Type 3 and raised primary-residence threshold from 50% to 70%. Fire inspection required every three years. City does not issue accommodations licenses (state/health department does).
jurisdictions.key_notes: Among the strictest STR regimes in the U.S. Legacy Type 3 permits cannot be reinstated once expired, revoked, or forfeited by transfer of ownership — designed to gradually phase out non-owner-occupied STRs.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating an unregistered STR is a misdemeanor subject to fines, imprisonment, and civil penalties under Bozeman Municipal Code §38.34.160; forced delisting for non-compliant listings.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Properties in unincorporated Gallatin County follow county zoning (only Gallatin Canyon/Big Sky and Hebgen Lake districts specifically address STRs) plus state health licensing.
jurisdictions.source: City of Bozeman (bozeman.net / bozemanmt.gov STR pages, Ordinance 2149); Bozeman Daily Chronicle; Avalara MyLodgeTax; Love Big Sky; Bozeman Real Estate Group

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (functionally) — no incorporated-city STR permit (Big Sky is unincorporated), but a Montana state Public Accommodation License (via Gallatin City-County Health Department) is required, plus Montana Dept. of Revenue and Big Sky Resort Area District (resort tax) registration. County zoning may require a conditional use permit in some districts.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register for Montana lodging taxes (Dept. of Revenue) and Big Sky Resort Area District resort tax.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 8% Montana lodging tax (4% Lodging Facility Use Tax + 4% Lodging Sales Tax) plus a 4% Big Sky Resort Area District resort tax on reservations of 30 nights or shorter (Airbnb collects/remits the resort tax). One third-party source also references a Gallatin County accommodation tax.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Governed by county zoning — Big Sky is unincorporated, spanning Gallatin and Madison counties. Of Gallatin County's 22 zoning districts, only Gallatin Canyon/Big Sky and Hebgen Lake address STRs specifically; some zoned areas require a conditional use permit, and some areas prohibit STRs. Unzoned areas have no use restrictions.
jurisdictions.density_rules: No density caps identified; generally regarded as a relatively favorable, growth-friendly regulatory environment.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Not confirmed as mandated; $1M+ liability coverage commonly recommended by industry sources.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb collects and remits the 4% Big Sky resort tax on stays ≤30 nights; otherwise hosts remain responsible for taxes on off-platform bookings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Resort tax (4%) created in 1992 and stable since. Montana requires local health department review via the Public Accommodations License process; safety documentation (smoke/CO detectors), guest registry retained ≥1 year, emergency contact, parking, and bear-proof waste management are recommended/expected. Spans two counties, so applicable county rules depend on exact parcel location.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Unincorporated status means no municipal STR ordinance; regulation comes from Gallatin/Madison county zoning, state health licensing, and the resort tax district. Strong tourism economy; community discussion of housing impacts but no STR restrictions adopted as of mid-2026.
jurisdictions.penalties: Non-compliance can lead to fines, canceled permits, or forced closure (general Montana enforcement); resort tax non-compliance subject to penalties.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Verify the exact county (Gallatin vs. Madison) and zoning sub-district for any specific property, as requirements differ. HOA/condo rules may add restrictions.
jurisdictions.source: Big Sky Resort Tax (resorttax.org FAQ); Gallatin County (gallatinmt.gov STR FAQs); GoSummer; Hostaway; Steadily; Avalara; The Offer Sheet

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — both an annual Business License (Finance Dept.) and a Basic Use Permit (BUP) from the Planning Dept. are required to legally operate an STR anywhere in town.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — hosts must register with the Wyoming Department of Revenue for a sales tax license, collect taxes, and file returns.
jurisdictions.fees: Per current Town fee schedule for STR BUP plus annual business license; exact amounts Unknown (see Town fee schedule).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Outside Lodging Overlay (residential zones): max 60 total rental nights per unit per calendar year and a maximum of 3 separate stays per year. Inside Lodging Overlay/Planned Resort Zone: no such cap.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Wyoming statewide 4% sales tax plus 5% lodging tax on STRs; plus applicable local taxes.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Dual system: Town of Jackson permits STRs in residential zones (NL-1 to NL-5, NM-1, NM-2, NH-1, R, MHP, OR) with strict limits, plus the Lodging Overlay/Snow King Resort District. Teton County prohibits STRs in most residential areas unless in specific approved developments.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STRs defined as occupancy of fewer than 30 consecutive days; leases of 31+ days are exempt. Leases of 30+ days that limit occupancy to under 30 days are treated as short-term.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Advertisements must include the STR license number, and online ads must include a link to the Town of Jackson short-term rental law; operators responsible for collecting/remitting sales and lodging taxes.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Outside Lodging Overlay requires annual BUP renewal (expires Dec. 31) and annual neighbor noticing to owners within 200–300 feet, plus HOA notice. HOAs must approve STRs for Town rules to apply. STR units subject to building and fire codes.
jurisdictions.effective_date: January 1, 2024 (Ordinance 1402 governs Lodging Overlay size restrictions)
jurisdictions.key_notes: Property location (Town vs. County, inside vs. outside Lodging Overlay) dramatically changes whether STR is allowed.
jurisdictions.penalties: Properties that violate STR requirements will not be issued a BUP for a minimum of five years. In Teton County, fines up to $750 per offense, with each day a separate offense.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Ordinance enacted to address noise, parking, and neighborhood-character complaints and protect long-term residential housing; Town uses fees/fines to fund dedicated enforcement and tracking software.
jurisdictions.source: Town of Jackson official STR page (jacksonwy.gov/335); Teton County (tetoncountywy.gov/653); Buckrail; Avalara MyLodgeTax; ShelterJH

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — all STRs must be registered annually with the Town (managed by the Code Enforcement Officer) prior to rental; previously registered rentals must re-register by May 31 annually.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — owners must register with Maine Revenue Services and obtain a Retailer's Certificate; STRs must remit state sales/lodging tax.
jurisdictions.fees: $250 annual fee per registration (historically); minimum fine for operating without valid registration is $1,500.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Depends on category: VR-1 requires the dwelling be the owner's primary residence (owner resides more than 183 days/year); VR-2 is a non-primary-residence whole-unit rental.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Unknown (no nights cap specified; STR defined as rentals under 30 days)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Maine 9% lodging (vacation rental) tax (state-administered).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: VR-1s allowed in the 34 districts where currently permitted; new VR-2s limited to districts zoned for commercial and lodging.
jurisdictions.min_stay: VR-1: rentals under 30 days with a 2-night minimum. VR-2: under 30 days with a 4-night minimum.
jurisdictions.density_rules: VR-2s (non-primary-residence rentals) capped at 9% of the total number of dwelling units in town; transfer of any VR registration between owners is prohibited. Owners limited to two VR-1 permits per primary residence.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: STR registration number issued by the CEO must be included in all advertising, including online platforms.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Properties must pass a life-safety inspection (fire/code) before registration; emergency information sheet with registration number and local contact must be posted inside the main door; weekly trash removal required while rented; local contact required. Operating without registration requires a 12-month wait before reapplying. New Chapter 174 STR ordinance took effect December 18, 2025.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Original VR ordinance effective December 2, 2021 (Ch. 190 repealed 2021, replaced by Ch. 174); revised STR rules effective December 18, 2025 (VR-1 amendments adopted November 2025).
jurisdictions.key_notes: Town categorizes STRs as VR-1 (in owner's primary residence) and VR-2 (non-primary residence). VR-2 cap is the most controversial element.
jurisdictions.penalties: Minimum fine of $1,500 for operating without valid registration; each violation and each day of violation after notification is a separate offense; Town Council may revoke registrations.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Rules adopted amid a housing crunch to slow conversion of year-round homes to Airbnbs; about one in five dwelling units in Bar Harbor are used as vacation rentals.
jurisdictions.source: Town of Bar Harbor official STR page (barharbormaine.gov/516); eCode360 Ch. 174; Maine Legislature doc; Ellsworth American; Bar Harbor Story (Nov 2025)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a city Business License plus a Rental License are required for all STRs operating within city limits or the police jurisdiction; applications routed through Revenue, Planning & Zoning, and Fire Marshal.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — register with Alabama Department of Revenue, Baldwin County Revenue Commissioner, and the Gulf Shores revenue department for lodging tax collection.
jurisdictions.fees: Business license fee is revenue-based (based on gross receipts); reported application fee around $500 with ~$300 annual renewal (per industry tracker; verify with Revenue Dept. at 251-968-2426). Licenses expire Dec. 31, renew Jan. 1.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — Gulf Shores welcomes non-owner-occupied STRs.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Typically two persons per bedroom plus two additional guests (per industry source; verify with city).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Approximately 13% total in corporate limits / 9.5% in police jurisdiction, combining Alabama state lodging tax (4%), Baldwin County lodging tax, city lodging tax, and CVB tax (rate components vary by source).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs allowed in most tourist-oriented zoning districts; some inland/residential properties may not qualify. All applications undergo Planning & Zoning compliance review.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as rental of a dwelling unit for less than 180 consecutive days.
jurisdictions.density_rules: None — no caps or owner-occupancy requirements (business-friendly approach).
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Reported minimum $1 million liability policy (per industry source); verify with city.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Owner responsible for collecting/remitting lodging taxes (platforms do not automatically remit all local taxes); designated 24-hour local emergency contact required.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Safety inspection by Fire Marshal required (real inspection — deficiencies must be corrected before issuance/renewal); properties must conform to International Property Maintenance, Existing Building, and Fire Codes; quiet hours 10 PM–7 AM; one off-street parking space per bedroom. Single long-term-rental unit (180+ days) is exempt from licensing.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Vacation rental ordinance via Ordinance 1738 (amended); sign-permit rules updated under Ordinance 2201 (February 2026). Exact original effective date Unknown.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Ordinance 2201 (Feb 2026) now requires a planning-department permit for all future vacation rental signs to reduce sign clutter.
jurisdictions.penalties: Codes violations can trigger citation requiring cure within 21 days, suspension of rental license, and orders to vacate until remedied; late tax filing incurs penalties plus interest.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Gulf Shores is one of Alabama's most active STR markets; ~71% of listings owned by hosts with 5+ properties. Note: nearby Orange Beach (separate city) has a 180-day minimum-stay rule for most areas and requires a separate Vacation Rental Certificate.
jurisdictions.source: City of Gulf Shores official Rental License page (gulfshoresal.gov/780); Code of Ordinances Sec. 8-44 / Ord. 1738; The Short Term Shop 2026 guide; HometeamLuxuryRentals; Checkmate Rentals

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a Short-Term Rental business license (a type of city business license under Chapter 22) AND a separate STR Permit (issued by the Fire Department after fire-safety inspection) are required; a separate STR business license and permit is required for each business address.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must obtain/maintain a Missouri Retail Sales Tax license (Airbnb-only listings exempt from personal sales tax license but business name must still be registered); a Tourism Tax bond is collected during application.
jurisdictions.fees: $100 annual STR business license fee; $150 STR Permit fee (paid before fire-safety inspection). STR Permit (fire-safety inspection) valid 3 years.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — STR definition excludes rentals operating within a primary residence (those are not treated as STRs).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Occupancy and parking limits enforced (specific figures Unknown).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Missouri state sales tax 4.225% on stays under 30 days, plus Branson local tourism/lodging taxes (rates vary by location); no platform remits Branson's tourism tax on the host's behalf.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs prohibited in Low Density Residential Districts (amended Sec. 22-36); generally permitted only in higher-density/tourist zones, and planned developments must explicitly list STR as an allowed use.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as lodging for not less than one night and not more than 30 consecutive days.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unlawful to operate or advertise an STR without an STR permit; no platform (Airbnb, VRBO) remits Branson tourism tax — owner responsible.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Mandatory fire-safety inspection scheduled via CitizenServe portal; STR Emergency Safety Plan and evacuation map must be posted in each unit/bedroom; STR business license year runs May 1–April 30 and renews by April 30.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Ordinance No. 2024-0065 effective May 1, 2025 (city adopts 'Short-Term Rental' terminology exclusively); zoning amendments via Ordinance 6454 (2024).
jurisdictions.key_notes: 'Nightly Rental' and 'Short-Term Rental' are synonyms; some addresses listing Branson are outside city limits — owners must verify via the city mapping system.
jurisdictions.penalties: City can impose daily fines (industry sources cite $100–$1,000 per violation); permit revocation, platform delisting, and legal action possible; Branson performs audits and spot checks.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Branson hosts ~10 million visitors/year; STR Permit is assignable/transferable with new ownership.
jurisdictions.source: City of Branson official STR pages (bransonmo.gov/969); Branson Municipal Code Sec. 22-472 / 22-36; Branson Globe (Ord. 6454); HometeamLuxuryRentals

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — an STR Permit for each rental property plus an active Town Business License are required; permit must be obtained before registering for a business license.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — operators must comply with South Carolina Accommodations Tax (ATAX) laws and collect state and local accommodations taxes; registration via the Town's GovOS/MuniRevs system.
jurisdictions.fees: Transitioning from a flat $250 STR permit fee to a new annual fee of $150 per bedroom; business license application fee $10 plus a Business License Tax based on prior-year gross rental revenue.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Bedrooms must be primarily for sleeping and meet building/safety codes; maximum of six vehicles allowed to park in STR driveways (specific occupant count Unknown).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 10% combined state and local accommodations taxes on rentals to the same person for less than 90 days; plus Town Accommodations Tax and Beach Preservation Fee on rentals under 90 days.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Ordinance applies to privately owned residential property used as vacation homes/STRs for rental periods of less than 30 days; STRs within HOAs must provide an HOA letter authorizing STR use.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as a rental period of less than 30 days.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: STR permit number (account number) must be listed on any and all current advertisements; permit applications must be in a person's name, not an entity such as an LLC.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: New rules effective May 1, 2026: bedrooms must meet building/safety codes; max six driveway vehicles; 60-minute shutoff valve and propane detection alarms for external propane grills where applicable; properties 3,600+ sq ft require approved fire-safety systems and smoke detection. Program moving from complaint-based to performance-driven with proactive inspections, a 24/7 rapid-response hotline, and dedicated enforcement staff. STR permit period extended to April 30 annually (one-time extension to May 15, 2026); permit payment portal opens April 6, 2026.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Updated STR regulations adopted October 2025; new operational rules effective May 1, 2026; further FY2026 program/enforcement amendments at second reading March 31, 2026.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Per-bedroom fee model reflects a full cost-recovery structure so STR operations fund STR oversight.
jurisdictions.penalties: Escalating fines: $250 (first offense), $500 (second), $1,000 (subsequent); schedule resets after 12 months with no violations; $25 late fee on unpaid fines after 30 days; outstanding fines must be paid before renewal; three or more citations within 12 months can result in license revocation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Note: properties in POAs (e.g., Sea Pines) may have a separate free POA registration requirement (valid Jan 1–Dec 31) in addition to Town permit and business license.
jurisdictions.source: Town of Hilton Head Island official site (hiltonheadislandsc.gov/business/short_term_rentals); Avalara MyLodgeTax (Dec 2025); Destination Vacation HHI; The Agency Hilton Head 2026 guide

jurisdictions.license_required: No countywide STR permit/license for unincorporated Dare County; STRs are broadly allowed. (Individual OBX towns—e.g., Nags Head—require their own annual registration; Dare County itself does not restrict STRs in most areas.)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — operators must register with Dare County Tax Collection for occupancy tax and with the NC Dept. of Revenue for state sales tax (state registration not required if a platform like Airbnb/Vrbo collects/remits on the host's behalf).
jurisdictions.fees: No county STR permit fee for unincorporated Dare County. (Town-level example: Nags Head charges $25/year per STR registration.)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: No countywide numeric cap; in practice tied to septic capacity/bedroom count. Some OBX towns set limits (commonly ~2 persons per bedroom). Unknown for unincorporated areas beyond environmental-health/septic limits.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Dare County occupancy tax of 6% of gross receipts, plus NC state sales tax of 6.75%. Combined rates in OBX towns reach roughly 12.75%.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs are permitted broadly across unincorporated Dare County; the county does not restrict STRs in most areas. Note 2018–2019 zoning amendments require certain cluster-home/workforce-housing units to be long-term only (Workforce Housing Units may not be used for short-term rental Apr 1–Oct 31).
jurisdictions.min_stay: No minimum stay imposed for STRs (note tax thresholds: rentals of 90+ continuous days to the same person are treated as long-term and are exempt from occupancy/sales tax).
jurisdictions.density_rules: None at the county level (no caps or separation/concentration limits for unincorporated areas). Cluster-home developments are restricted to long-term occupancy.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Not required by Dare County ordinance. Industry guidance strongly recommends STR-specific liability coverage; check HOA rules. Unknown for any town-specific mandates.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Airbnb collects and remits Dare County occupancy tax (typically as a lump sum); Vrbo historically collects from renters and remits to owners who then pay the county. Hosts remain responsible for ensuring all taxes are correctly registered and remitted.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: North Carolina's Vacation Rental Act (NCGS Ch. 42A) sets minimum safety standards (smoke/CO detectors, etc.) for all vacation rentals. State law limits local governments' ability to ban STRs and protects STRs legally operating before new local ordinances. Septic capacity rules via Dare County Environmental Health can effectively limit occupancy.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Dare County occupancy tax framework long-standing (authorizations dating to 1991/2002/2014); zoning amendments affecting cluster/workforce housing adopted 2018–2019 (e.g., Ord. passed 9-16-2019).
jurisdictions.key_notes: The Outer Banks is one of the most established, STR-friendly vacation-rental markets in the U.S.; Dare County does not restrict STRs in most areas. Requirements vary by incorporated town (Duck, Southern Shores, Kitty Hawk, Kill Devil Hills, Nags Head, Manteo), so verify rules at the specific property address.
jurisdictions.penalties: No county STR-registration penalty for unincorporated areas. Tax non-remittance triggers back taxes, interest and penalties. Town example (Nags Head): $100 civil penalty for failing to register within 30 days, then $50/day.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Occupancy tax does not apply to a residence rented fewer than 15 days in a calendar year, or to stays of 90+ continuous days to the same person. An estimated 3,500–5,000 STRs operate across the OBX (2019 figure). A 2% portion of the 6% occupancy tax funds beach nourishment.
jurisdictions.source: Dare County Tax Department (darenc.gov occupancy-tax); Dare County Essential Housing FAQ & Code of Ordinances (amlegal §22-58.7); Nags Head, NC official STR registration page; NC Vacation Rental Act; industry trackers (Awning, Hostaway, The Offer Sheet, BNBCalc) — 2025–2026.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — a Short-Term Rental zoning permit is required for each STR address and must be renewed annually. New STRs in the Oceanfront Resort STR Overlay District also require a Conditional Use Permit (CUP) from City Council. Home sharing (owner-occupied, owner present, rooms only) needs no zoning permit but must register with the Commissioner of the Revenue.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — each STR must register with the Commissioner of the Revenue and maintain current registration; proof of registration is required for the zoning permit (Sandbridge STRs must register but need not submit proof).
jurisdictions.fees: $200 application fee; if approved it is deducted from the $500 annual zoning permit fee. (Some sources list the annual permit at $500.)
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No for STRs (entire-dwelling rentals). Home sharing does require the dwelling to be the owner's principal residence with the owner present during all rental periods.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No annual night cap, but no more than two (2) rental contracts within any consecutive 7-day period.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: No more than two persons per bedroom after 11 p.m. Events with more than 50 people require a special events permit.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: City transient occupancy tax (TOT) plus a $2-per-night flat room tax; also subject to Virginia state/local sales & use lodging taxes (apply to stays under 90 days). Exact combined percentage: Unknown.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs permitted by right in the Sandbridge Special Service District (with annual zoning permit) and in the Oceanfront Resort STR Overlay District (with CUP). Elsewhere, new STRs generally require a CUP and are otherwise prohibited unless grandfathered. Home sharing permitted by right in all districts.
jurisdictions.min_stay: STR defined as rental of an entire dwelling for less than 30 consecutive days; no minimum-night requirement specified.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Limited via overlay/zoning approach (STRs concentrated in Sandbridge and the Oceanfront Resort Overlay; new STRs elsewhere require CUP). No explicit numeric density cap cited.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of liability insurance of at least $1,000,000 applicable to the rental activity, underwritten by City-acceptable insurers and listing the STR address. Free policies offered by online platforms are generally not acceptable.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Under Virginia law (effective Oct. 1, 2022), platforms such as Airbnb collect and remit transient occupancy taxes on the host's behalf; owners must still register with the Commissioner of the Revenue.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Required: sworn statement form; life-safety inspection report with first application (good 5 years; attestation in off years); structural safety inspection report initially then every 3 years; parking plan; 4-sq-ft STR identification sign (must include property manager's name); approved off-street parking (min. one space per bedroom—note one task-force amendment cited one space per two bedrooms; no street parking; stacking allowed). CUPs expire 5 years and renew administratively.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Initial STR ordinance effective Nov. 1, 2019; major amendments adopted Sept. 7, 2021; further enforcement amendments (reverting violations to misdemeanors) adopted 2025.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Grandfathered properties: registered with the Commissioner of the Revenue with TOT paid before July 1, 2018, remain eligible under City Code §241.2; expanding the dwelling by more than 25% or 1,000 sq ft (whichever is less) revokes grandfathered status. Properties granted an STR CUP before Sept. 7, 2021 (outside the Overlay) are also grandfathered subject to CUP terms.
jurisdictions.penalties: STR zoning violations are misdemeanors (changed back from civil in 2025). Fines start at $1,000 for initial violations, rising to $1,500 for uncorrected issues and up to $2,000 for ongoing violations. City Council may revoke permits or grandfathered status after notice and hearing (15 days' notice).
jurisdictions.additional_context: STR defined as an entire dwelling rented for less than 30 consecutive days for compensation; home sharing is a separate, less-regulated category. The Oceanfront Resort District is currently the only STR Overlay District. Properties not in an eligible zone/overlay and not grandfathered are effectively prohibited unless the owner petitions City Council for an STR overlay.
jurisdictions.source: City of Virginia Beach Planning Dept. (planning.virginiabeach.gov/permits/short-term-rental & /rules); Virginia Beach Zoning Ordinance §241.2 (virginiabeach.elaws.us); Avalara MyLodgeTax (Oct 2025); APA/Municode code summary; industry trackers (STRProfitMap, Hostaway, BNBCalc, Steadily) — 2025–2026.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Ministry of Tourism License

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Ministry of Tourism License

jurisdictions.license_required: No — Business tax registration only

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines and interest for undeclared tax income

jurisdictions.license_required: No — Business tax registry only
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes

jurisdictions.key_notes: Tax registration required; no STR permit or municipal caps exist.
jurisdictions.penalties: Tax interest and penalties for undeclared rental income

jurisdictions.license_required: No — Voluntary certification only
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Subject to standard personal or corporate income taxes; no specific transient occupancy tax on Airbnb platform sales

jurisdictions.penalties: None specific to STR; general tax non-compliance penalties apply

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Business registration and hospitality/tourism classification
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Ministry of Tourism authorization & business registration

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines or administrative warnings for undeclared tourist lodging business

jurisdictions.license_required: No — Business tax registry only

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines or penalties under general tax code for unregistered income

jurisdictions.license_required: No — Business tax registry only
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Subject to national income tax (SAR) reporting; 15% Sales Tax (ISV) may apply to commercial lodging operations
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed in residential areas; check building association/condominium bylaws
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None

jurisdictions.source: Servicio de Administración de Rentas (SAR) and IHT Guidelines 2025

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — eTurista Categorization
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 20% rental income tax (with a 25% standardized cost deduction, resulting in an effective tax rate of 15%)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed in residential zones; must meet minimum technical and hygiene standards
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Hosts must categorize their property as a tourist accommodation facility with the local self-government unit and register all guests within 24 hours of arrival via the centralized electronic eTurista portal.

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines for failure to register guests and operating uncategorized accommodation

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — AJPES Registration & Business Entry
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Variable (usually 30 to 150 days per year under 2026 Hospitality Act)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Subject to national income tax (FURS) reported via eDavki; mandatory tourist tax (~€3.13 per person/night in Ljubljana)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed in residential buildings subject to 75% co-owner consent (and 100% of direct neighbors)

Hosts must enter the Business Register, register the property in the AJPES Register of Accommodation Establishments (RNO), obtain 75% co-owner consent (including 100% of adjacent neighbors), and adhere to municipal annual day caps.
jurisdictions.key_notes: AJPES registration, 75% co-owner consent, and municipal day caps (30-150 days) under the 2026 Hospitality Act.
jurisdictions.penalties: Significant fines for unregistered renting or lack of co-owner consent
jurisdictions.additional_context: Slovenia significantly tightened STR regulations in 2026 to address housing availability and tourist tax compliance in Ljubljana.
jurisdictions.source: AJPES and Financial Administration of the Republic of Slovenia (FURS) 2026

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Short-Term Rental Permit
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 60 nights per calendar year (unhosted rentals only)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 6.625% NJ State Sales Tax, 5% state occupancy fee, plus local occupancy taxes
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed only in owner-occupied primary residences (up to 3 units owned in the same building). Prohibited in rent-controlled units and buildings with >4 units.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Owners must obtain a Zoning Compliance Certificate first, then apply for an STR permit via the Division of Housing Preservation. Tenants/subletters are banned from operating STRs.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Owner-occupancy required; 60-night cap for unhosted rentals; banned for tenants.
jurisdictions.penalties: Municipal court fines for unauthorized rentals
jurisdictions.additional_context: Jersey City maintains some of the strictest STR regulations in the New York metropolitan area to preserve long-term housing stock.
jurisdictions.source: Jersey City Municipal Code Chapter 255 and Housing Preservation Guidelines 2025

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Annual STR Registration

jurisdictions.tax_rate: 9% VT Rooms Tax + 3% state surcharge + 9% Burlington Gross Receipts Tax (21% total)

jurisdictions.source: Burlington Code of Ordinances Chapter 18 and Housing Division 2025

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Short-Term Rental Registry

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines of $100 to $500 for violations or failure to register

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Annual STR Registration

jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Allowed in residential areas

jurisdictions.compliance_notes: No specific municipal short-term rental permits or caps exist. Hosts must register and declare all rental income to the Tax Administration of Kosovo (ATK) and pay the 9% flat tax quarterly.
jurisdictions.key_notes: 9% flat tax on rental income declared quarterly; no STR licensing or caps currently exist.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines or penalties under general tax code for unregistered income
jurisdictions.additional_context: ATK is actively working to reduce informality and increase registration in the digital lodging sector in Pristina.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory registration number + change of use with compensation)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (Taxe de séjour registration)

jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 120 days for primary residence; none for secondary residence (if compensated)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Standard safety limits based on habitable area
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Taxe de séjour (~€1 to €4 per person/night) + standard income tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Yes (Basque Country agglomeration tension zone)
jurisdictions.density_rules: Yes (compensation offset rule requires purchasing/converting a commercial space of equal size in the same municipality to permanent housing)

jurisdictions.compliance_notes: The strict Pays Basque compensation rule was implemented in March 2023 to combat housing shortages across 24 local municipalities (including Biarritz, Bayonne, and Anglet).
jurisdictions.key_notes: Mandatory registration + strict compensation rules for secondary residences.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €50,000 for unauthorized change of use.
jurisdictions.additional_context: One of the most restrictive STR markets in France.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory registration number + change of use authorization)

jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 120 days for primary residence; none for secondary residence (if compensated)

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €50,000 + daily enforcement penalties.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Requires purchasing commercial space to convert to residential in the central zone.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory registration number + temporary authorization)

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €50,000 for unregistered or over-quota rentals.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Strict limits implemented to protect long-term housing.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory registration number + temporary authorization)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory registration number + change-of-use authorization)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes

jurisdictions.density_rules: Yes (limits on change of use per building/owner)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory registration number + compatibility report)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory registry number + municipal authorization)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Strict occupancy caps based on square meters
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard rental income tax (IRPF) + regional tourist tax

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory registration + municipal compatibility certificate)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (IGIC registration - local Canary sales tax)

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €150,000 for severe infractions.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Strict environmental and sustainability criteria required.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory registration number + compatibility report)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €600,000 for severe unauthorized operations.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory registration with Xunta de Galicia + municipal compatibility)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes

jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Casco Histórico center has a complete ban on new licenses. Stays are capped at 60 days per year unless operating under a commercial hotel-equivalent permit.

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €90,000 for illegal tourist rentals.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Aims to protect housing stock along the Camino de Santiago route.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory Wohnraumschutznummer registration number)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes

jurisdictions.density_rules: Yes (strict protection of housing stock under Zweckentfremdung law)

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €500,000 for illegal Zweckentfremdung (misuse of residential space).

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory registration with Flanders Tourism + municipal permit)

jurisdictions.tax_rate: Municipal tourist tax (~€2 to €4 per person/night) + income tax

jurisdictions.density_rules: Yes (Strict caps outside center to prevent high concentrations in residential areas)

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to €20,000 and closure of the property.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Limits secondary properties to 1 per owner to control investor activity.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory registration number + tourist rental permit)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes (secondary residences banned in residential zones)
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 60 days per year for primary residence
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 6.5% tourist tax (toeristenbelasting) + standard income tax

jurisdictions.density_rules: Yes (moratoriums or strict zoning in popular districts)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory municipal permit + tourist activity registration)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory Scottish short-term let license)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.fees: Licensing fees range from £250 to £800 depending on occupancy
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Strictly capped based on bedroom dimensions and layout

jurisdictions.density_rules: Yes (Planning permission required for secondary let properties in control areas)

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to £2,500 and ban from operating.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory NSW Short-Term Rental Accommodation Register registration)

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to $1,100 AUD for individual breaches and $5,500 AUD for corporations.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory Planning Permit / Development Approval)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes

jurisdictions.penalties: Significant fines for operating without development approval under Tasmanian planning law.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Aims to prevent investor acquisitions of residential housing stock.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory Short Stay Letting Local Law License)

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to $7,000 AUD for operating without a license or breaching code.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Council notification required)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None (but renting >59 days triggers rate surcharge)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard income tax + 150% municipal council rate surcharge
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Yes (Requires development approval in certain zones)

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory Urban Hanok / Guest House Registration with the district office)

jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Must register and display registration certificate

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to 10 million KRW or imprisonment up to 1 year for unlicensed operations.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Active crackdowns by local district offices (Gu).

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory district registration / Hotel License)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.fees: Free for small host registration; hotel license fees vary by size
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None (if licensed or registered under the small host exemption)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Maximum 20 guests overall under the small host exemption
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard Thai personal income tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Yes (Prohibited in residential condominiums under standard building bylaws)
jurisdictions.min_stay: 30 days for unregistered apartments; daily rental legal only with license/exemption
jurisdictions.density_rules: Yes (exemption capped at maximum 4 rooms per property)

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to 10,000 THB + 10,000 THB per day of ongoing violation under the Hotel Act.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Condo buildings actively enforce bans with security.
jurisdictions.source: Thai Hotel Act B.E. 2547 / Chiang Mai District Office

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory business license + fire/safety certification)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 5% VAT + 5% personal income tax (10% flat rate for rental businesses)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Yes (Complete ban on short-term rentals in residential apartment blocks)

jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Article 6 of the Law on Housing bans short-term tourist lets in multi-family apartment blocks (chung cư). Standalone villas/townhouses are allowed with business registration and police guest reporting.

jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to 40 million VND for illegal operations in apartments.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Guest registration with police department is strictly required.
jurisdictions.source: Vietnam Law on Housing (Law No. 65/2014/QH13) / Hanoi Police Department

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (DBKK business license/permit)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard Malaysian income tax + regional tourism tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Yes (Strictly banned in residential-titled buildings; commercial titles only)
jurisdictions.density_rules: Yes (subject to co-owner/JMB management approval in each commercial building)

jurisdictions.key_notes: Banned in residential-titled blocks + JMB building bylaws approval required.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines and property sealing by DBKK municipal enforcement.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Covers high-growth tourist centers in Sabah state.
jurisdictions.source: Dewan Bandaraya Kota Kinabalu (DBKK) / Sabah State Government

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Department of Tourism accreditation + municipal mayor's permit)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (BIR tax registration)
jurisdictions.fees: Mayor's permit and DOT accreditation fees apply

jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard income tax + municipal business tax

jurisdictions.density_rules: Yes (condominium corporations enforce caps or bans)

jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Most upscale condominium towers (e.g. in BGC and Makati) enforce strict bans on daily rentals in their Master Deed of Restrictions. Commercial hosts require a mayor's permit.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Ministry of Tourism registration legally required but lightly enforced)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Standard safety limits based on habitable area
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard graduated rental income tax (practically unenforced)

jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Douglas County STR permit required, but unavailable in PD zones)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 0 days (effectively banned for rentals under 30 days)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Standard safety limits based on habitable area
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard Colorado state and county lodging taxes

jurisdictions.min_stay: 30 days (mandated by HOA covenants and county zoning defaults in PD zones)

jurisdictions.key_notes: Effectively banned under 30 days due to Planned Development (PD) zoning and HOA covenants.
jurisdictions.penalties: County fines for zoning code violations; HOA fines and liens for covenant breaches.
jurisdictions.additional_context: One of the most restrictive unincorporated suburban areas in Colorado.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Local security clearances and police/municipal registration required)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes

jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard Syrian income tax on rental yields

Hosts must obtain security clearance from local security branches for any tenant, and register lease agreements with the municipality.

jurisdictions.license_required: No (No dedicated short-term rental permits exist)

jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: None
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: The short-term rental market in Baghdad operates in a low-regulation, informal environment. There are no specialized short-term rental laws, day caps, or municipal permits. General tenancy rules under the Real Estate Lease Law No. 87 of 1979 apply. Non-Iraqi tenants may face additional residency/sponsorship checks.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Very low regulatory barrier. No specialized short-term rental permits or day caps; general real estate lease laws apply.
jurisdictions.penalties: Minimal enforcement for short-term stays; standard civil disputes default to tenancy courts under the 1979 Lease Law.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Evolving market with standard Airbnb usage, though cash-on-arrival and offline bookings through local brokers remain highly prevalent.
jurisdictions.source: Iraqi Ministry of Justice / Real Estate Lease Law No. 87 of 1979

jurisdictions.license_required: No (No specific municipal short-term rental license exists)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: No (unless operating as a formal hotel/guesthouse business)
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Standard safety limits based on habitable area
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard personal income tax on rental yields
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: None
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Low-regulation, informal market. No specific short-term rental registry or day caps exist. However, foreign nationals must comply with local visa regulations, and sub-leasing properties without landlord consent is strictly prohibited.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Very low regulation. No specific STR registry or caps; check landlord permissions and visa/residency laws.
jurisdictions.penalties: None for short-term letting specifically; standard landlord-tenant civil dispute penalties apply.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Offline bookings through local networks are highly prevalent, though Airbnb is increasingly popular in Vientiane.
jurisdictions.source: Laos Ministry of Information, Culture and Tourism

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (National registration under EU 2024/1028 regulations; trade license required for hotel-like hospitality services)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.fees: Nominal fees for business/trade registry registration
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Standard fire and safety limits based on room capacity
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard national rental income tax, plus a municipal tourist tax (~€3.00–€3.50 per person/night)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: None (subject to building co-owner rules in residential blocks)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Yes (Mandatory data-sharing with tax authorities under new EU directives)
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Regulated in accordance with the EU 2026 short-term rental data sharing framework. Hosts must register their properties to obtain a unique ID. Tourist tax collections apply, which are frequently handled by major platforms. Trade licenses are required if hosts offer additional services (like cleaning or breakfast) beyond basic rental.
jurisdictions.key_notes: EU registration and data sharing rules apply. Municipal tourist tax required.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines for failure to declare rental income or unregistered trade activity.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Active European capital city market with high summer tourist traffic.
jurisdictions.source: Bratislava City Council / Slovak Republic Financial Administration

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (National registry registration required under EU short-term rental regulations)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Standard capacity limits based on habitable space
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard Latvian personal income tax (10% flat rate if declared as rental income without costs), plus municipal tourism levy
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: None
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Yes (Mandatory transaction and host data sharing with local authorities)
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: In transition to full alignment with the EU short-term rental database and registration rules. Hosts must display a unique registration number on all online listings. Income must be declared to the State Revenue Service (VID). Hosts are responsible for collecting the municipal tourist tax.
jurisdictions.key_notes: EU-compliant registration required. Income must be declared to the State Revenue Service (VID).
jurisdictions.penalties: Administrative fines by VID for unregistered shadow economy activity.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Active Baltic market with municipal efforts to improve tourism statistics and tax compliance.
jurisdictions.source: Latvian Ministry of Economics / Riga City Council

jurisdictions.license_required: No (No specific municipal short-term rental license is enforced)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes (for formal businesses, or tax declaration for individuals)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Simplified tax of 4% on rental revenues, or standard income tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: None
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: No specialized short-term rental caps or registry numbers. General commercial and tax codes apply. Hosts must report rental income to tax authorities. Building/condo management rules regarding noise and safety are strictly enforced, especially during major global events.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Low-regulation market. Tax declaration is required; check building bylaws and zoning restrictions.
jurisdictions.penalties: Tax audit fines for failure to report commercial property income.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Very active market during international events (such as Formula 1), with high demand for central apartments.
jurisdictions.source: Ministry of Taxes of the Republic of Azerbaijan

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Registration under the national tourism framework is mandatory)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard Armenian rental income tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: None
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Armenia requires tourism service providers, including short-term rental hosts, to register under the national tourism framework managed by the Tourism Committee. While city permits are not required, condo association rules on guest conduct are common.
jurisdictions.key_notes: National tourism committee registry registration is required; low municipal restriction.
jurisdictions.penalties: Theoretical administrative fines for unregistered tourism operations.
jurisdictions.additional_context: A highly active regional travel destination experiencing rapid tourism growth.
jurisdictions.source: Tourism Committee of the Ministry of Economy of the Republic of Armenia
