jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Mandatory tourist registration via the emehmon.uz portal is required)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.fees: Nominal registration fee per guest night
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Flat-rate tourism fee, plus standard personal income tax on rental yields
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: None
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Although setting up listings is straightforward, hosts must register all foreign guests with local authorities within 3 days of arrival (via emehmon.uz). Failing to register guests can lead to severe fines or exit blocks at the border for travelers.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Mandatory tourist registration (emehmon.uz) required for all foreign guests; strict border compliance checks.
jurisdictions.penalties: Severe fines for hosts failing to register foreign nationals, and potential travel delays/exit denial for guests.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Very strict registration regime inherited from Soviet-era regulations, but digitized in recent years.
jurisdictions.source: Ministry of Tourism and Cultural Heritage of Uzbekistan

jurisdictions.license_required: No (No specific municipal short-term rental registry exists)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: No (unless operating as a formal commercial hospitality business)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard personal income tax on rental income
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: None
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: No municipal restrictions or day caps. Property-level restrictions (HOA bylaws and gated community regulations) are very common in premium towers. Hosts must report income on standard annual tax filings.
jurisdictions.key_notes: No municipal licensing or caps; check HOA rules in gated communities and high-rises.
jurisdictions.penalties: None under municipal codes; civil fines from HOAs if building bylaws are violated.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Experiencing a surge in vacation rentals due to increased tourism, surfing demand, and Bitcoin-related expat growth.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Furnished tourist apartment licenses required for commercial listings)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Subject to the BHD 3 per room per night tourism levy (where applicable)
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Yes (Prohibited in areas zoned exclusively for single-family residential housing)
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Zoning laws prohibit renting out furnished tourist apartments in areas designated exclusively for residential housing. Commercial licensing is required for dedicated tourist flats. High-rise condo complexes commonly enforce strict HOA rental caps or bans.
jurisdictions.key_notes: STRs prohibited in residential zones; tourism license and tourism levy apply to commercial operations.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines for zoning violations and operating unlicensed lodging establishments.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Very active luxury market catering to regional weekend travelers.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes (Declaration to the local commune (APC) under the homestay framework is required)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard personal income tax on rental yields
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: None
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Renting is governed by the national homestay (logement chez l'habitant) framework. Hosts are legally required to report the identity of all foreign guests to the local commune (APC) or police station (under Law 08-11).
jurisdictions.key_notes: Commune (APC) declaration required; mandatory identity reporting to police for foreign guests.
jurisdictions.penalties: Security audits and fines for failing to report foreign guests.
jurisdictions.additional_context: A smaller market compared to neighboring Morocco/Tunisia, heavily reliant on offline networks but slowly adopting digital booking.
jurisdictions.source: Algerian Ministry of Tourism and Handicrafts

jurisdictions.license_required: No (No specific municipal short-term rental license exists)
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: No (unless operating as a formal commercial business)
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Subject to standard rental income tax
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: None
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: The short-term rental market is largely unregulated with no night caps or licensing. General tax laws and business registration apply. Many professional hosts cater to expats and corporate clients, occasionally implementing 30-day minimum lease structures for consistency.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Unregulated market. No specific municipal permits or caps; standard rental taxes apply.
jurisdictions.penalties: None specific to STR; standard tax evasion penalties apply if rental yields are undeclared.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Rapidly growing market popular with business travelers and returning diaspora.
jurisdictions.source: Kampala Capital City Authority (KCCA) / Uganda Revenue Authority

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Accessory Guest Accommodation license required. Operation of standard commercial short-term rentals in residential dwellings is prohibited to preserve local housing supply.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for business taxes, collect and remit 2% Tourism Improvement Fee (TIF), and federal GST (5%) if revenue exceeds CAD $30,000.
jurisdictions.fees: Municipal business license application and annual renewal fees apply; rate depends on structural classification.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes. Host must occupy the property as their primary residence and reside on-site while hosting guest accommodations.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No explicit nightly cap if operating within primary residence limits; transient occupancy defined as stays under 28 consecutive days.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Regulated by dwelling size and layout; subject to strict safety inspections and building occupancy limits.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 2% Tourism Improvement Fee (TIF) plus federal GST (5%) where applicable.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Strict zoning boundaries. Stays under 28 days are banned in residential zones except for permitted Accessory Guest Accommodations. Non-owner occupied vacation rentals are restricted to commercial lodging districts.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (transient lodging covers stays under 28 consecutive days).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Limited to one accessory guest accommodation permit per primary residence; quota-based limits in specific neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — commercial general liability insurance covering short-term accommodations is required.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None formally active, but hosts must display valid business license number on all listing advertisements.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Operators must secure safety inspections, post emergency contact info, and meet fire safety standards including smoke detectors and fire extinguishers.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Accessory Guest Accommodation regulations in force.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Located in a National Park with extremely tight housing supply; short-term rentals are highly restricted. Operating an unpermitted STR triggers active municipal enforcement.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a license or in an unpermitted zone results in significant fines (up to CAD $2,500 for a first offense) and potential legal action.
jurisdictions.additional_context: National Park residency requirements apply; operators must also qualify as an 'eligible resident' to live and run a business in Banff.
jurisdictions.source: Town of Banff (banff.ca); Banff Lake Louise Tourism; Bow Valley Insider; bnbcalc.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Individual municipal business license required for each short-term rental unit.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for property tax reclassification, Tourism Levy, and federal GST (5%) if gross revenue exceeds CAD $30,000.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual business license fee applies (~CAD $250–$500+ depending on unit type). Fine for operating without a license starts at CAD $2,500.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No (for properties located in legally permitted tourist zones).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No nightly cap when operating in permitted zones.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Governed by building code and safety standards; typical limits are 2 guests per bedroom plus 2 additional guests.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Commercial property tax rate applies (properties designated as 'Tourist Homes' are taxed at the non-residential rate, roughly 3x the residential rate). 4% Alberta Tourism Levy + 5% GST.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Permitted only in properties specifically zoned as 'Tourist Home' or 'Hotel Condominium'. Traditional residential zones strictly ban stays under 30 days. The city is phasing out the 'Tourist Home' zoning category.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (short-term defined as stays under 30 consecutive days).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Limited to tourist-zoned developments; residential-zone bans prevent density expansion outside commercial hubs.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — commercial general liability insurance for short-term rentals is required.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None formally active; hosts are responsible for displaying a valid business license number on all listing platforms.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Unit must pass initial safety/code inspections. Non-compliant operations (failing to register or pay non-residential tax rates) lose grandfathered 'Tourist Home' status if inactive for 6 months.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Tourist Home tax and phase-out regulations in force; regular updates to municipal licensing fees apply.
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of Canada's most active and restricted mountain gateway markets. Applying the commercial tax rate significantly reduces net margins for casual operators.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a license or in an unpermitted residential zone results in steep fines (CAD $2,500 for a first offense, escalating up to CAD $10,000 for repeat offenses).
jurisdictions.additional_context: Owners can voluntarily convert 'Tourist Homes' to standard residential to lower taxes, but this conversion is permanent and cannot be reversed.
jurisdictions.source: Town of Canmore (canmore.ca); Alberta Tourism Levy; Kyan Properties; canmorealberta.com

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Resort Municipality of Whistler (RMOW) Tourist Accommodation business license required, plus B.C. Provincial Short-Term Rental Registration.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must collect and remit 8% PST, 3% Municipal and Regional District Tax (MRDT), and 5% federal GST if applicable.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual tourist accommodation business license fee is CAD $250. Initial application fee is CAD $25.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No (for properties zoned for Tourist Accommodation; Whistler is exempt from the B.C. provincial primary residence requirement).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No nightly cap in permitted zones.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Regulated by building code and strata bylaws; general guidelines are 2 guests per bedroom + 2 extra.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 8% PST + 3% MRDT + 5% GST where applicable.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Permitted only in Tourist Accommodation (TA) zones. Subject to covenants: Phase I allows unlimited owner use and rental management choice; Phase II limits owner use to 56 days/year and requires pooling/hotel management.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (short-term defined as stays under 90 consecutive days).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Strict zoning limitations prevent STR expansion into residential-only neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — commercial insurance covering short-term nightly rentals is required.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must share monthly listing data and remove listings that lack valid provincial registration numbers at the municipality's request.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Hosts must display their valid business license number on all listing advertisements. Strata corporation bylaws frequently impose additional rules or bans.
jurisdictions.effective_date: RMOW Tourist Accommodation licensing in force; B.C. Provincial Registry active.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A premier global ski resort. Title covenants (Phase I vs Phase II) are legally binding and dictate owner occupancy limits and rental management pools.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating an STR in a residential zone or without a valid business license results in significant fines (up to CAD $1,000 per day per infraction).
jurisdictions.additional_context: The B.C. Provincial Short-Term Rental Accommodations Act exempts Whistler from the province-wide principal residence requirement due to its status as a designated resort municipality.
jurisdictions.source: Resort Municipality of Whistler (whistler.ca); Province of British Columbia; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Transient Guest Facility registration with the City of Newport and registration with the Rhode Island Department of Business Regulation.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for state sales tax and local hotel tax collections.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual registration fee is $500 for Home-Occupation (owner-occupied) or $1,000 for Non-Home-Occupation (investor/non-owner occupied). $15 filing fee.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes (within residential zones; STRs are banned in residential zones unless the property is the owner's primary residence).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: For primary residence STRs in residential zones, limited to renting a maximum of 2 bedrooms to no more than 4 guests.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 14% total guest tax (7% state sales tax, 2% local hotel tax, and 5% state hotel tax or whole-home STR tax).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Banned in residential zones unless primary residence (host must reside on-site during rental). Permitted by right in General Business (GB), Waterfront Business (WB), and Commercial Industrial (CI) zones. Special Use Permit required in Limited Business (LB) zones.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (short-term defined as stays of 30 consecutive days or fewer).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Zoning bans prevent STR proliferation in low-density residential neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of adequate liability insurance is required upon registration.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None active, but hosts must list valid registration numbers on all advertisements.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Operators must complete mandatory human trafficking prevention training annually. Specific safety documentation (fire plan, contact info) must be posted inside the main entry door.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Rhode Island tax changes effective January 1, 2026; City zoning rules in force.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A historic coastal destination with highly restrictive residential zoning rules. The 2026 tax update introduced a 5% whole-home tax on residential dwellings.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating an unregistered STR or violating zoning laws results in daily fines of up to $1,000 per violation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: The city actively monitors listing platforms to identify and fine uncertified transient lodging facilities.
jurisdictions.source: City of Newport (cityofnewport.com); Rhode Island Department of Business Regulation; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Florida DBPR Vacation Rental License + City Business Tax Receipt (or County Certificate of Use for unincorporated areas).
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register with the Florida Dept of Revenue (for sales tax) and Pinellas County Tax Collector (for TDT).
jurisdictions.fees: For unincorporated areas, the County Certificate of Use is a $450 application fee plus a ~$150 safety inspection fee. City BTR fees vary by size.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No cap on total rental days, but frequency is strictly capped in permitted residential zones.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Typically limited to 2 guests per bedroom plus 2 additional guests, subject to building safety codes.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 13% total transient tax (6% Florida state sales tax, 1% Pinellas County discretionary surtax, and 6% local Tourist Development Tax).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Stays of less than 30 days are prohibited in municipal residential zones. In zones where short-term occupancy is allowed, rentals are strictly capped at a maximum frequency of 3 occupancies per 365 days. Short-term rentals are permitted by-right in DC (Downtown Center) and CG (Commercial General) zones.
jurisdictions.min_stay: 30-day minimum stay in most traditional residential neighborhoods (stays under 30 days count toward the 3-rentals-per-year frequency cap).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Strict zoning boundaries and frequency limits restrict density within the city.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Recommended (commercial liability covering transient occupancy is standard).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms collect state sales taxes automatically, but hosts are responsible for ensuring the 6% county TDT is collected and remitted.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Unincorporated properties must secure a Certificate of Use, undergo a mandatory safety inspection, and post emergency contact info. HOA bylaws may ban STRs regardless of zoning.
jurisdictions.effective_date: City zoning codes and Pinellas County licensing in force.
jurisdictions.key_notes: The 3-rentals-per-365-days rule in St. Petersburg city limits is one of the most restrictive frequency caps in Florida, designed to curb casual vacation rentals in residential areas.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating in violation of city zoning or running an unregistered transient rental results in fines up to $1,000+ per day and potential property liens.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Many properties with a St. Petersburg address are actually in unincorporated Pinellas County, which has much more lenient zoning but requires a Certificate of Use and inspection.
jurisdictions.source: City of St. Petersburg (stpete.org); Pinellas County Government (pinellas.gov); Pinellas County Tax Collector; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Florida DBPR Vacation Rental License + City Business Tax Receipt (BTR).
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register with the Florida Dept of Revenue and Pinellas County Tax Collector.
jurisdictions.fees: Variable municipal BTR fees. Pinellas County Tourist Development Tax registration is free but mandatory.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No cap on total rental days, but stays of less than 31 days are banned in residential zones.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 2 guests per bedroom plus 2 additional guests; subject to building safety codes.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 13% total transient tax (6% Florida state sales tax, 1% Pinellas County discretionary surtax, and 6% local Tourist Development Tax).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Short-term rentals (stays under 31 days) are strictly prohibited in all residential zoning districts. Nightly rentals are permitted only in designated Tourist (T) districts and specific commercial zones.
jurisdictions.min_stay: 31-day minimum stay (or one full calendar month) required in all residential zones.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Strict zoning boundaries limit STR operations to dedicated tourist sectors.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None formally active; hosts must collect and remit the 6% Pinellas County TDT if the platform does not automate it.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Must comply with noise, trash, and parking codes. City zoning regulations preempt HOA rules, but HOAs can still impose stricter bans.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Clearwater actively enforces the 31-day minimum stay rule in residential zones, utilizing code enforcement officers to audit listing platforms.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating an unpermitted STR in a residential zone triggers fines ($150 for a first offense, $300 for a second, and $500 for subsequent violations) and potential property liens.
jurisdictions.additional_context: The city's zoning ordinances are among the strictest on the Florida Gulf Coast, aiming to protect the residential integrity of local neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.source: City of Clearwater (myclearwater.com); Pinellas County Tax Collector; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Vacation Rental Registration with the City of Anna Maria, plus a Florida DBPR Transient Public Lodging license.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register with the Florida Dept of Revenue (for sales tax) and Manatee County Tax Collector (for TDT).
jurisdictions.fees: Variable municipal registration and safety inspection fees apply. Annual renewal is required.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No cap on total rental days.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Strictly limited to the lesser of: 2 guests per bedroom (for bedrooms >100 sq ft) plus 1 guest per bedroom (70-100 sq ft) plus 2 additional guests; or a maximum of 8 occupants per unit.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 13% total tax (7% Florida state sales and use tax + 6% Manatee County Tourist Development Tax).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Permitted in residential zones but subject to strict local occupancy, parking, noise, and trash ordinances. HOAs and condo associations often impose additional restrictions.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (transient rentals allowed by-right but highly regulated).
jurisdictions.density_rules: None, but occupancy and parking quotas limit unit capacity.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Recommended (standard commercial general liability covering guest accommodations).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None formally active; hosts are responsible for listing valid business license details on all advertisements.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Operators must pass an annual safety inspection, provide a 24/7 local contact agent, and post evacuation maps. Strict sea turtle nesting lighting regulations apply from May 1 to October 31.
jurisdictions.effective_date: City Vacation Rental Ordinance in force.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A highly active barrier island STR market. The city enforces strict quiet hours (10 p.m. to 8 a.m.) for outdoor pool and spa areas, with automated noise sensors becoming common.
jurisdictions.penalties: Violations of occupancy, parking, trash, or turtle-safe lighting codes result in heavy fines and potential suspension of the vacation rental permit.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Anna Maria Island is divided into three cities (Anna Maria, Holmes Beach, Bradenton Beach), each with its own separate vacation rental laws.
jurisdictions.source: City of Anna Maria (cityofannamaria.com); Manatee County Tax Collector; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Short-Term Rental business license required from the City of Folly Beach.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for state sales tax and local hospitality/lodging taxes.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual business license fees apply; rate depends on rental revenue.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No (but investor/non-owner occupied licenses are capped).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No nightly cap, but must be rented a minimum of 28 nights per year to maintain license validity.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Governed by local zoning and safety regulations.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Combined state and local transient lodging taxes apply (approx. 12-14%).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Strict cap of 800 investor short-term rental (ISTR) licenses. Once capped, new licenses are unavailable except through select hardship exceptions. Licenses are non-transferable and do not convey with property sale.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (short-term defined as stays under 28 consecutive days).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Cap limits total number of investor-owned STRs citywide.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of adequate liability insurance is required.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None formally active; operators must list their valid license details on all listing platforms.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Local contacts must be designated. City actively audits listing channels to identify and penalize unlicensed operations.
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of the most contentious STR caps in South Carolina. The 800-license limit on investor-owned properties has created an active waitlist.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a license or violating cap rules results in license revocation, fines, and misdemeanor charges.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Voter-approved referendum capped investor permits to preserve local community integrity and housing access.
jurisdictions.source: City of Folly Beach (cityoffollybeach.com); Folly Current; Smith Spencer Real Estate; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Certificate of registration required from the Nantucket Board of Health, plus state registry with MassTaxConnect.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for state room occupancy excise tax.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual Board of Health registration fees apply. Fines apply for late renewal.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Strictly limited to 2 persons per bedroom plus 2 additional guests.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Combined state sales tax and local room occupancy excise taxes apply (approx. 11.7% to 14.45% depending on local surcharges).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs codified into the municipal zoning code in late 2025. Corporate ownership (LLCs/corporations) is banned for new STR registrations to protect local housing stock; properties must be registered under individual names.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (short-term defined as stays of 31 consecutive days or fewer).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Strict spacing or quotas do not apply, but safety, parking, and wastewater regulations limit unit capacity.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — $1,000,000 in general liability insurance is mandatory.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None formally active; operators must list their valid registration number on all listing advertisements.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Must pass health and safety inspections. Mandatory designated local contact reachable 24/7. Waste disposal and parking rules must be posted.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Zoning codification and Board of Health registry active.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A high-profile island vacation market. The late 2025 zoning updates formally legalized STRs while restricting new corporate-owned investment listings.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating an unregistered STR or violating zoning/occupancy guidelines results in fines of up to $300 per day per violation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Annual renewals must be completed through the Board of Health access portal.
jurisdictions.source: Town & County of Nantucket (nantucket-ma.gov); Fisher Real Estate; Nantucket Current; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Placer County Short-Term Rental Permit required (applicable to unincorporated areas including North Lake Tahoe).
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must obtain a Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT) certificate.
jurisdictions.fees: Application and renewal fees apply. Code compliance and fire safety inspection fees are mandatory.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No (but owner-occupied properties are exempt from the caps).
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No nightly cap in permitted zones, but properties without a permit are subject to a 30-night minimum stay requirement.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 2 people per bedroom plus 2 additional guests, up to a county-wide cap based on septic/parking.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 10% Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT) plus local North Lake Tahoe tourism assessments.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Residential STR permits are capped at 3,900 county-wide. Hotels, commercial lodging, and tourist-zoned zones are exempt. Active waitlist exists for new permits. Permits do not transfer with property sales.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (short-term defined as stays of 30 consecutive days or fewer).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Cap limits total number of active residential permits in unincorporated county sectors.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — commercial liability covering short-term lodging is required.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None formally active; hosts must display their valid permit number and TOT certificate on all platforms.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Mandatory annual fire safety inspections. Quiet hours (10 p.m. to 7 a.m.) strictly enforced with county-funded hotlines. Dedicated local contact must respond within 60 minutes.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Placer County STR Ordinance in force.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Governs the unincorporated North Lake Tahoe region (Kings Beach, Tahoe City, Olympic Valley). The 3,900 permit cap is strictly enforced.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating an unpermitted STR results in fines starting at $1,000/day, escalating to permit revocation and misdemeanor charges.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Administered by the Placer County Community Development Resource Agency.
jurisdictions.source: Placer County Government (placer.ca.gov); Tahoe Lifestyle Team; Ascension Tahoe; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Town of Truckee Short-Term Rental Registration certificate required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must obtain a Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT) certificate.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual registration fee applies. Fines apply for late renewal.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No (but primary residences occupied at least 275 days/year are exempt from the permit cap).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Strict occupancy limits based on bedroom count (typical limit is 2 per bedroom + 2).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 10% Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT) plus 2% Truckee Tourism Business Improvement District (TTBID) assessment.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Total active STR registrations capped at 1,255. New owners must wait 365 days after a property sale before they are eligible to apply for a permit. Accessory Dwelling Units (ADUs), Junior ADUs, and multi-family housing are banned from new registrations.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (short-term defined as stays of 30 consecutive days or fewer).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Cap limits total active registrations Town-wide; active waitlist is managed.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None formally active; registration number and TOT certificate must be displayed on all listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Noise monitoring sensors may be required. Local contact must respond to complaints within 30 minutes. Quiet hours from 10 p.m. to 7 a.m. strictly enforced.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A premier mountain resort town adjacent to Lake Tahoe. The 365-day post-sale waiting period is designed to deter speculative real estate purchases for immediate STR use.
jurisdictions.penalties: Violations of the STR ordinance result in administrative fines starting at $500, leading up to certificate suspension or revocation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Waitlist applications are opened annually if slots under the 1,255 cap become available.
jurisdictions.source: Town of Truckee (townoftruckee.com); Mountain Luxury Living; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Short-Term Lodging Permit (STLP) and Newport Beach Business License required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT).
jurisdictions.fees: Variable permit and business license application and renewal fees apply.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 2 guests per bedroom plus 2 additional guests, subject to building codes.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 10% Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Permitted only in specific multi-family and commercial zoning districts (R-1.5, R-2, and RM). Prohibited in standard single-family residential (R-1) zones. Total active STLPs capped at 1,550 citywide; active waitlist managed when cap is reached.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Prohibited on properties with more than 3 residential units.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — commercial liability covering short-term lodging is mandatory.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None formally active; STLP permit number must be displayed on all listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Designated local contact must respond to complaints within 30 minutes. Strict noise, trash, and parking rules apply.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Newport Beach STLP Ordinance in force.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A highly popular Southern California coastal destination. Cap rules and zoning prohibitions make it extremely difficult to secure a new permit in single-family residential sectors.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a permit or violating zoning rules results in fines of up to $1,000 per violation and permit revocation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: STLPs must be renewed annually by the permit holder and do not transfer with property ownership.
jurisdictions.source: City of Newport Beach (newportbeachca.gov); Carter Kaufman Real Estate; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Transient Rental Permit required (but generally unavailable due to bans).
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must obtain TOT certificate if operating a legal nonconforming unit.
jurisdictions.fees: Municipal business license and permit renewal fees apply for grandfathered operations.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Regulated by building codes and permit rules for legal nonconforming units.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 10% Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Short-term rentals (stays under 30 consecutive days) are strictly prohibited in all single-family residential (R-1) zoning districts. Only grandfathered 'Legal Nonconforming' permits (issued prior to 2019) or specific housing incentive permits are allowed.
jurisdictions.min_stay: 30-day minimum stay required in all R-1 residential zones.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Bans prevent new STR development in residential neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None formally active; platforms are monitored for illegal listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: City code enforcement actively monitors listings to detect and fine illegal unpermitted transient rentals in residential areas.
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of the strictest short-term rental bans in California. The city strictly preserves its quiet, residential character, meaning new nightly rental permits are virtually unobtainable.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating an illegal STR in residential zones results in administrative citations, fines starting at $1,000, and legal prosecution.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Unincorporated areas of Monterey County surrounding the city are subject to a separate 4% cap on commercial STRs in coastal zones.
jurisdictions.source: City of Carmel-by-the-Sea (ci.carmel.ca.us); Carmel Coast Realty; Monterey County; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — San Juan County Vacation Rental Permit required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for Washington state sales tax and local lodging tax.
jurisdictions.fees: Permit application, public hearing, and annual renewal fees apply.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Strict occupancy caps specified on the individual permit (typically based on septic/bedroom limits).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Combined state/local tax rate is approx. 10.1% to 10.5% (includes local hotel/motel excise tax).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Island-specific permit caps apply to balance tourism: San Juan Island (337 permits), Orcas Island (211 permits), Lopez Island (135 permits), Outer Islands (10 permits). Shaw and Waldron islands prohibit STRs. Waitlists are managed. Permits are non-transferable.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (short-term defined as stays under 30 consecutive days).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Caps prevent new permit issuance once island limits are reached. Spacing or neighborhood quotas may apply.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — liability insurance coverage for commercial transient lodging is mandatory.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None formally active; permit number must be displayed on all listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Requires public notice to neighbors upon application, trash management plans, and dedicated parking spaces. 24/7 local contact must respond within 60 minutes.
jurisdictions.effective_date: San Juan County Vacation Rental Ordinance in force.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A highly restricted island market. The island-specific caps have resulted in active waitlists, and new permits are only issued when existing permits are cancelled or expire.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating a vacation rental without a permit or violating permit conditions results in fines of up to $500 per day and permit revocation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: The Town of Friday Harbor maintains a separate permitting process within its municipal boundaries.
jurisdictions.source: San Juan County Washington (sanjuanco.com); San Juan Islands Lifestyle; Friday Harbor; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Short-Term Vacation Rental (STVR) permit and City Business License required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register to collect and remit Clark County Transient Lodging Tax.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual STVR registration fee applies. Fines apply for late renewal.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Limited to 2 guests per bedroom plus 2 additional guests, subject to building code caps.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 13% Transient Lodging Tax (remitted to Clark County/City).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Strict spacing rules: a minimum of 1,000 radial feet of separation required between active STVR units. Multifamily buildings capped at a maximum of 25% of units operating as STVRs. Spacing rules prevent dense clustering.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Spacing requirement and multifamily caps restrict density within neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — commercial liability covering short-term vacation rentals is mandatory.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must verify and display valid city-issued permit numbers on all listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Noise monitoring sensors are mandatory. Designated local contact must respond to complaints within 30 minutes. Quiet hours from 10 p.m. to 7 a.m. strictly enforced.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A major suburb of Las Vegas. The 1,000-foot spacing rule is among the strictest in Nevada, preventing neighbors from operating competing rentals and limiting overall supply.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating an unregistered STVR or violating noise/spacing rules results in administrative fines starting at $500 per day, escalating to permit revocation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Owners are limited to a maximum of five registered STVR properties per state business license.
jurisdictions.source: City of Henderson (cityofhenderson.com); Airbnb Help Center; Jason Griggs Real Estate; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — City Rental License, Local Business Tax Receipt (BTR), and Certificate of Use (COU) required from both the City of West Palm Beach and Palm Beach County.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register with the Palm Beach County Tax Collector and Florida Department of Revenue.
jurisdictions.fees: Initial COU fee is $50, with a $20 annual renewal fee. Business Tax Receipt fees vary based on occupancy capacity.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: Prohibited for stays under 6 months in standard residential districts.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Governed by building, fire, and safety codes; typically limited to 2 guests per bedroom + 2 additional.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 12% total tax (6% Palm Beach County Tourist Development Tax + 6% Florida State Sales Tax).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Stays of less than six months and one day are strictly prohibited in standard residential zoning districts. Short-term rentals are allowed by-right only in designated transient lodging or commercial zones. Zoning review is mandatory.
jurisdictions.min_stay: 6 months and 1 day in standard residential zones (no minimum in commercial/transient zones).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Strict zoning limitations prevent STR proliferation in low-density residential neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Recommended (commercial general liability insurance covering transient rental operations).
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None formally active; hosts are responsible for ensuring all local and county lodging taxes are remitted.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Code enforcement actively monitors listing platforms. Properties with 3 or more units under one roof require mandatory Fire Department and Code Enforcement inspections.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Zoning regulations and minimum rental period rules in force.
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of the most restrictive coastal zoning environments in Florida. The 6-month-and-1-day rule effectively bans traditional weekend and weekly vacation rentals in residential neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating an unpermitted STR in violation of zoning codes results in daily code enforcement fines ranging from $150 to $500 per day.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Annual license renewals must be completed through the City's Development Services portal.
jurisdictions.source: City of West Palm Beach (wpb.org); Palm Beach County Tax Collector; Kelley Grant Law; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — City Business License and a Vacation Rental Certificate required from the City of Orange Beach.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for Alabama lodging tax and local city lodging taxes.
jurisdictions.fees: Annual Business License fees apply based on rental receipts, plus a Vacation Rental Certificate fee.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No nightly cap, but zoning regulations strictly apply.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 2 adults per bedroom plus children under 14 years of age.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Combined state, county, and local city lodging tax rate is approx. 11-13%.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Vacation rentals (stays under 30 days) are prohibited in standard single-family residential zoning districts. Permitted primarily in multi-family and tourist-oriented districts.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (short-term defined as stays under 30 days).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Strict zoning boundaries prevent STR proliferation in low-density residential sectors.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of liability insurance is required.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None active; hosts must list their business license number on all listing advertisements.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Emergency local contact must be designated and available 24/7 to respond to complaints within 30 minutes.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A major resort destination on the Gulf. The city strictly monitors zoning borders to keep vacation rentals out of quiet residential neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.penalties: Violations result in license suspension, warnings, and administrative fines starting at $250.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Condo buildings and private HOAs may have stricter rental restrictions than the city code.
jurisdictions.source: City of Orange Beach (orangebeachal.gov); Luxury Orange Beach; Awning; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Walton County Vacation Rental Certificate (VRC) required for all rentals of 30 days or less.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register to collect Walton County Tourist Development Tax.
jurisdictions.fees: VRC application and renewal fees depend on the size/occupancy of the property (typically $300-$500/year).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Strict occupancy limits based on bedroom count (typically 2 guests per bedroom + 4 additional guests).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 12% total tax (7% Florida state sales tax + 5% Walton County Tourist Development Tax).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Rentals must comply with county zoning maps. Requires passing a mandatory on-site life safety inspection (smoke alarms, fire extinguishers, emergency lighting).
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (short-term defined as stays under 30 days).
jurisdictions.density_rules: No spacing limits, but strict parking, trash, and noise regulations restrict capacity.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of adequate commercial general liability insurance covering short-term lodging.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: VRC certificate number must be displayed in the listing description on all platforms.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Emergency local contact must be posted on-site and reachable 24/7 to resolve issues within 30 minutes. Quiet hours strictly enforced (10 p.m. to 7 a.m.).
jurisdictions.effective_date: Walton County Short-Term Vacation Rental Ordinance in force.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Governs the highly popular 30A resort corridor (Miramar Beach, Seaside, Rosemary Beach). The VRC inspection standards are exceptionally detailed.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a VRC or failing safety inspections results in automatic code enforcement citations and fines up to $500 per day.
jurisdictions.additional_context: VRC certificates must be renewed annually before January 1.
jurisdictions.source: Walton County Florida (co.walton.fl.us); 360 Blue; Sea 30A; Municode; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Florida DBPR public lodging license and Escambia County Vacation Rental License required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for Escambia County Tourist Development Tax and Florida sales tax.
jurisdictions.fees: County and state licensing application and renewal fees apply.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Governed by fire safety standards and building capacity regulations.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 12.5% total tax (7.5% state sales tax + 5% Escambia County Tourist Development Tax).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs must comply with Escambia County zoning codes and Santa Rosa Island Authority (SRIA) leasehold terms. Grandfathered lease conditions apply.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None (short-term defined as stays under 30 days).
jurisdictions.density_rules: None, but condo rules and leasehold rules restrict multi-family density.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: State and county license numbers must be listed on all active advertisements.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Local emergency contact details must be registered and posted. Noise, trash, and parking rules are strictly monitored.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Escambia County and SRIA rules active.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Located on Santa Rosa Island. Since the island is leasehold property under the SRIA, operators must verify lease terms in addition to county zoning codes.
jurisdictions.penalties: Violating permit terms or operating without a license results in fines, lease violations, and permit revocation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Private condominium association bylaws frequently ban short-term rentals or impose strict minimum stay rules.
jurisdictions.source: Escambia County (myescambia.com); Santa Rosa Island Authority (sria-fla.com); Sold by Top Tier; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Short-Term Vacation Rental (STVR) permit required from the City of Carlsbad.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT).
jurisdictions.fees: Application and annual renewal fees apply.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No nightly cap, but zoning limits apply.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 2 people per bedroom plus 2 additional people.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 10% Transient Occupancy Tax.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Permitted only within the Coastal Zone. Prohibited in standard residential zones outside the coastal boundaries.
jurisdictions.density_rules: None, but limited by zoning district borders.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Permit number must be posted on all active listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: A local contact must be available 24/7 to respond to complaints within 45 minutes. Noise and parking rules are strictly enforced.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A popular coastal destination near San Diego. The city enforces strict zoning boundaries to protect inland residential neighborhoods.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a permit or violating conditions results in citations, fines, and permit revocation.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Private HOAs may completely prohibit short-term rentals.

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Short-Term Rental Permit required from the City of Napa.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT).
jurisdictions.fees: Annual permit fee and inspection fee apply.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 2 guests per bedroom, up to a maximum of 10 guests.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 12% Transient Occupancy Tax.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Prohibited in unincorporated Napa County. Within city limits, permitted only in specific commercial or mixed-use zones. A strict city-wide cap is in place; new permit applications and waitlists are currently suspended.
jurisdictions.density_rules: None, but the hard permit cap restricts new operators.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — proof of commercial general liability insurance required.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Valid permit number must be displayed in all online advertisements.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Local manager must be available 24/7 to resolve issues. Annual health and safety inspection required.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Napa has exceptionally strict laws to protect local housing and vineyards, making it one of the hardest markets in California for new operators.
jurisdictions.penalties: Unpermitted rentals face fines up to $1,000 per day and permanent ineligibility for future permits.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Properties outside the city boundaries are subject to the county's total ban on STRs.
jurisdictions.source: City of Napa (cityofnapa.org); Napa County Code; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — City of Sarasota Vacation Rental Certificate required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for Florida Sales Tax and Sarasota County Tourist Development Tax.
jurisdictions.fees: Application fee and inspection fee apply.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Maximum 2 people per bedroom plus 2 additional people, capped at 10 guests total.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 12% total tax (7% Florida state sales tax + 5% Sarasota County Tourist Development Tax).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Rentals must pass a mandatory safety inspection. Banned in many single-family residential zones, which enforce a 7-night minimum stay.
jurisdictions.min_stay: 7-night minimum stay applies in standard residential zones; no minimum stay in multi-family/commercial districts.
jurisdictions.density_rules: None, but zoning minimum-stay rules restrict transient operations.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Certificate number must be included in listing titles/descriptions.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: A local representative must be designated and available 24/7 to respond to issues within 1 hour.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Sarasota City Vacation Rental Ordinance active.
jurisdictions.key_notes: The city and county enforce zoning and safety standards very strictly, particularly on the barrier islands and waterfront districts.
jurisdictions.penalties: Violations result in warnings, administrative fines up to $500, and certificate suspension for repeat offenses.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Condo boards and HOAs frequently ban rentals shorter than 30 days.
jurisdictions.source: City of Sarasota (sarasotafl.gov); Sarasota County; Avalara MyLodgeTax; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Massachusetts Department of Revenue registration and local town registration required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register to collect state and local occupancy taxes.
jurisdictions.fees: Varies by town (Edgartown, Oak Bluffs, Vineyard Haven, etc.).
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Set by local town bylaws and board of health capacity certificates.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Combined state and local lodging taxes range between 11.7% and 14.45% depending on the specific town.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STR regulations are managed individually by each of the six island towns. Some towns restrict operations to a single property per owner or require owner occupancy for a portion of the year.
jurisdictions.density_rules: None, but wastewater/septic rules restrict capacity in rural areas.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — liability insurance required under Massachusetts state law.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: State certificate number must be listed on all booking platforms.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Trash disposal, parking rules, and noise regulations are strictly monitored, particularly during peak summer months.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Massachusetts short-term rental law and local town bylaws in force.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A highly seasonal resort market with massive summer demand. Regulations focus on preserving local year-round housing stock.
jurisdictions.penalties: Unregistered rentals face state tax penalties and local municipal fines.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Rentals for 14 days or less per year are exempt from Massachusetts state tax, but still require registration.
jurisdictions.source: Massachusetts Department of Revenue (mass.gov); Vineyard Gazette; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Vacation Home Rental (VHR) or Tourist Home license required from the City of Traverse City.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for Michigan sales/use tax and county excise tax.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No nightly cap, but licensing quotas apply.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 2 people per bedroom plus 2 additional people.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 11% total tax (6% Michigan Use Tax + 5% Grand Traverse County Excise Tax).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: VHRs are restricted to specific commercial and mixed-use districts. Residential zones generally ban VHRs, permitting only owner-occupied 'Tourist Homes' with strict licensing caps.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Strict limits on the number of licenses allowed per residential block to prevent cluster concentration.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: License number must be posted on all active listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Local emergency contact must be posted on-site. Noise, parking, and trash ordinances are strictly enforced.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A premier Michigan tourist destination. The city limits the total number of VHR licenses in residential blocks to maintain community character.
jurisdictions.penalties: Operating without a license is a municipal civil infraction resulting in fines up to $500 per day.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Grand Traverse County has separate tax collection rules.
jurisdictions.source: City of Traverse City (traversecitymi.gov); Grand Traverse County; Roam Management; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Malaga local license and Spanish National registration number (NRUA) required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for Spanish VAT (10%) and report rental income.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No nightly cap, but zoning freezes apply.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 4 guests in 1-bedroom units; capped by municipal capacity certificates.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 19% flat tax on rental income for EU/EEA residents; 24% for non-EU residents. VAT is 10% if hotel-like services are provided.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Moratorium on new tourist licenses active in 43 central neighborhoods until at least January 2028. New registrations are prohibited unless the building's homeowners association (HOA) explicitly votes to permit STRs.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Zoning freezes prevent new listings in high-density areas.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must verify and display the NRUA number and remove unverified listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: First-floor rules and independent access rules may apply depending on local zoning. Fines for non-compliance can reach up to €600,000.
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of Spain's most popular resort cities. The municipal government has frozen new licenses in the historical center and given HOAs veto power over new units.
jurisdictions.penalties: Severe administrative fines up to €600,000 for operating without a license or using a false registration number.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Verify zoning district via Malaga planning portal before purchase.
jurisdictions.source: City of Malaga (malaga.eu); Paton & Mayr; Waypoint Sur; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Basque Country registry license and Spanish National registration number (NRUA) required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must report rental income to Basque tax authorities.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Standard Basque Country income tax rates apply to rental returns.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Operates under a strict zone-based quota system. New tourist apartments are banned in designated 'saturated zones' (like the Old Town). In standard residential zones, STRs are restricted to the first floor only.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Strict caps on the percentage of tourist units permitted per block.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Registration number must be displayed in all online advertisements.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Subject to Basque Country tourist accommodation safety and noise regulations.
jurisdictions.effective_date: San Sebastian local STR zoning active.
jurisdictions.key_notes: San Sebastian has some of the oldest and most restrictive STR zoning laws in Spain, designed to prevent the conversion of upper-floor apartments in residential buildings.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines, listing removal, and license revocation for operating in saturated zones or without first-floor compliance.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Banned in the historic Old Town (Parte Vieja).
jurisdictions.source: Basque Country Government; City of Donostia-San Sebastian; Paton & Mayr; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — planning permission required for class change to holiday lets in designated areas.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for UK Income Tax on holiday letting income.
jurisdictions.fees: Planning application and council tax fees apply.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Governed by local fire safety and building capacity regulations.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Subject to standard UK income tax. Second homes and holiday lets are charged a double council tax rate (200% premium).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: To address severe housing shortages, the local council requires planning permission for property class changes to short-term holiday letting.
jurisdictions.density_rules: None, but second-home tax premiums discourage concentration.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — specialist holiday home insurance required.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: None active, but hosts must comply with UK safety standards.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Strict fire risk assessment and safety certifications required for all commercial holiday lets.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Cornwall double council tax premium active.
jurisdictions.key_notes: A massive coastal holiday destination in the UK. The local council has implemented aggressive tax policies and planning requirements to protect local housing stock.
jurisdictions.penalties: Unpermitted class changes face code enforcement actions, and tax evasion results in major financial penalties.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Check local parish council planning overlays as rules differ slightly by town.
jurisdictions.source: Cornwall Council (cornwall.gov.uk); UK Government; Visit Britain; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Unknown
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Unknown
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unknown
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: No immediate operational changes; hosts operating under existing voter-approved ordinance continue under current rules until any ballot measure is approved by voters in November 2026.
jurisdictions.effective_date: 2026-11-03 (conditional on ballot placement and voter approval)
jurisdictions.key_notes: Unique governance posture: existing STR rules were voter-enacted and require voter approval to amend, making this a ballot initiative process rather than standard council action.
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown
jurisdictions.additional_context: Unknown

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes; new special event license category proposed in addition to existing categories
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Unknown
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes for renters (primary residence, lease must not prohibit, unit must not be rent-stabilized); second-property license proposed for owners
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: 90 nights/year for unhosted stays (unchanged); no cap for hosted stays; special event license allows unhosted stays during designated events without counting toward 90-night cap
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unknown
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Renters in rent-stabilized units remain ineligible; bill is pending council action and provisions may change; operators should monitor DC Council committee hearings.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Pending DC Council vote (introduced March 13 2026; still under council review as of June 28 2026)
jurisdictions.key_notes: Unknown
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown
jurisdictions.additional_context: Unknown

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — City of Victoria short-term rental business licence and BC Provincial registry number required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — subject to federal GST and provincial PST/MRDT registration if earning thresholds met.
jurisdictions.fees: Licence fees start at $1,500/year for non-owner occupied units (where grandfathered) or $150/year for principal residence rooms.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — STRs are limited to the operator's principal dwelling unit only. Non-principal residence rentals are prohibited.
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: No nightly cap, but must be the operator's primary home.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Capped by bedroom count (typically max 2 adults per bedroom).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 16% total tax on stays (including 5% GST, 8% PST, and 3% Municipal and Regional District Tax (MRDT)).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Banned in entire self-contained accessory dwelling units, garden suites, or secondary properties. Restricted to the operator's principal residence. Existing non-conforming use protections were removed by provincial legislation in May 2024.
jurisdictions.density_rules: None, as principal residence requirement effectively caps non-owner occupied unit counts.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must verify and display BC provincial registration numbers and remove unregistered listings within 15 days of a provincial request.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Provincial legislation removes local grandfathering of investment units, requiring active compliance audits across all platforms.
jurisdictions.key_notes: One of the most highly restricted markets in Canada. The British Columbia government has overridden local zoning to enforce primary residence rules province-wide.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to $3,000 per day for operators and up to $10,000 per day for platforms for non-compliant listings.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Condo buildings (strata) can ban STRs entirely via their bylaws.
jurisdictions.source: City of Victoria (victoria.ca); Government of British Columbia (gov.bc.ca); AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Nova Scotia Tourist Accommodation Registry number and Halifax Regional Municipality (HRM) registration required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — subject to federal GST and provincial tax regulations.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — STRs are limited to the operator's primary residence in residential zones. Secondary properties are prohibited.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Capped by fire safety and building occupancy certificates.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 15% Harmonized Sales Tax (HST) applies to stays, plus standard personal/corporate income taxes.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: In all standard residential zones, STRs are restricted to the host's primary residence. Secondary or investment properties are prohibited from operating as STRs unless they are physically located in commercial or mixed-use zones that explicitly permit tourist accommodations.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must require hosts to input their provincial registration numbers.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: The province maintains a mandatory registry of tourist accommodations to monitor STR supply.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Halifax Regional Municipality STR bylaws active.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Implemented strict primary residence rules to combat local housing shortages in residential suburbs, shifting investment units exclusively to commercial zones.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines and administrative penalties for operating without a provincial registration number or violating zoning usage codes.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Primary residence verification may require tax returns, utility bills, or driver's licenses.
jurisdictions.source: Halifax Regional Municipality (halifax.ca); Province of Nova Scotia (novascotia.ca); AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Vivienda Vacacional (VV) regional licence and Spanish National registration number (NRUA) required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must register for Canary Islands IGIC tax (similar to VAT) and report rental income.
jurisdictions.fees: Licence application and administrative fees apply.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Max 2 people per bedroom plus 2 in common areas, capped by the property's certificate of occupancy (cédula de habitabilidad).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Canary Islands IGIC is 7% (reduced rate for accommodation). Income tax is 19% for EU residents or 24% for non-EU residents.
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: The Ley de Ordenación Sostenible (December 2025) imposes a five-year freeze on new VV licences. Restricts short-term let density to a maximum of 10% of total residential space in any municipality. New STRs are prohibited in areas designated as saturated.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Capped at 10% of the total residential built area in any municipality, with local councils having the authority to set even lower caps.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Platforms must verify and display registration numbers and share booking/occupancy data with tax authorities.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Stringent safety requirements (fire extinguishers, first aid kits, emergency signage) must be maintained inside the property.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Canary Islands Sustainable Tourism Law active.
jurisdictions.key_notes: The regional government passed a sweeping tourist accommodation freeze to curb rising rents and protect local communities across Tenerife, Gran Canaria, Lanzarote, and Fuerteventura.
jurisdictions.penalties: Severe administrative fines up to €150,000 for operating without a licence, listing on platforms without a verified number, or exceeding local density caps.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Building communities (Comunidades de Propietarios) can ban tourist rentals via a three-fifths majority vote.
jurisdictions.source: Government of the Canary Islands (gobiernodecanarias.org); Idealista; Gran Canaria Group; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — Highland Council short-term let (STL) licence required under the Scottish national licensing framework.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — must report income to HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC).
jurisdictions.fees: Licence application fees depend on property capacity, ranging from £300 to over £1,000 for a three-year licence.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No for secondary letting, but different licensing standards apply to home-sharing vs. entire-home secondary letting.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Strictly limited by the licence capacity, based on floor area and fire safety risk assessments.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Subject to standard UK income tax rates. Second homes/unoccupied holiday lets may be subject to a double council tax rate (200% premium).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: The Highland Council requires planning permission for property class changes from residential to short-term holiday letting in designated Short-Term Let Control Areas. Skye has extremely high STR density (approx. 17% of local housing stock consists of STLs).
jurisdictions.density_rules: Controlled via planning permission requirements in local control zones.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — public liability insurance (minimum £2 million or £5 million depending on council) required.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Hosts must display their licence number and energy performance certificate (EPC) rating on all online listings.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Requires mandatory annual safety certificates: Gas Safety (if applicable), Electrical Installation Condition Report (EICR), Portable Appliance Testing (PAT), Legionella risk assessment, and fire safety systems.
jurisdictions.effective_date: Scottish National STL licensing scheme fully active.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Scotland's holiday letting market is heavily regulated. The Highland Council rigorously enforces safety certificates and planning permission requirements on Skye to protect local housing.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines up to £10,000 for operating without a licence, and court orders preventing further letting.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Existing operators who failed to apply by the October 2023 transitional deadline must cease letting until a licence is granted.
jurisdictions.source: The Highland Council (highland.gov.uk); Scottish Government (gov.scot); VisitScotland; AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — The Sea Ranch Association (TSRA) Rule 6.7 registration AND Sonoma County Vacation Rental permit (Coastal Zone) required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Sonoma County Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT) certificate required.
jurisdictions.fees: Sonoma County permit application fees and TSRA administrative registration fees apply.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Capped by septic system capacity (standard maximum 2 people per bedroom plus 2, up to 10 or 12).
jurisdictions.tax_rate: 12% Sonoma County Transient Occupancy Tax (TOT).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Short-term rentals are prohibited in Accessory Dwelling Units (ADUs). Sonoma County vacation rental permits are non-transferable upon sale of the property.
jurisdictions.min_stay: None, but individual property rules vary.
jurisdictions.density_rules: Regulated under TSRA Rule 6.7 community density guidelines.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Sonoma County mandates that host listings display valid permit numbers.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Strict quiet hours (10:00 PM to 7:00 AM), dark sky lighting compliance, outdoor parking restrictions, and emergency contact manager availability.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Subject to double regulations: Sonoma County rules for the Coastal Zone and strict Sea Ranch Association (TSRA) HOA bylaws.
jurisdictions.penalties: Subject to standard Sonoma County noise and code violation fines, plus TSRA HOA enforcement fines and suspension of rental privileges.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Permits do not transfer to new owners upon sale. New owners must submit a fresh application and meet current zoning limits.
jurisdictions.source: The Sea Ranch Association (tsra.org); Permit Sonoma (permitsonoma.org); AirDNA

jurisdictions.license_required: Yes — City of Littleton short-term rental business licence required.
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Yes — Littleton sales tax license and Colorado state tax registration required.
jurisdictions.fees: Business license and sales tax application fees apply.
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: Yes — STRs are limited exclusively to the operator's primary residence. Non-primary residence licenses are prohibited.
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Maximum 2 renters per bedroom, capped at 8 renters per property.
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Approx. 9.9% combined tax (including 3.75% Littleton city sales tax, 3.25% county/district tax, and 2.9% state tax).
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs are prohibited in detached Accessory Dwelling Units (ADUs). Allowed in primary residences. Requires at least 2 off-street parking spaces.
jurisdictions.insurance_required: Yes — minimum $500,000 general liability insurance required.
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: License numbers must be displayed on all advertisements/platforms.
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Strict quiet hours, trash container requirements, parking limits, and operators must distribute a "Good Neighbor" handout to surrounding neighbors.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Strict primary residence rules to protect local residential character. Detached ADUs are banned from STR use.
jurisdictions.penalties: Fines and license suspension for violations of the zoning code, noise limits, or operating without a license.
jurisdictions.additional_context: Existing operators must prove ownership and residency, plus complete a self-inspection checklist.

jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Unknown
jurisdictions.occupancy_limit: Capped by International Property Maintenance Code and septic/sanitation limits
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: 500-foot separation between licensed STR properties; max 100 STR licenses per multifamily property; legacy properties may qualify for exemption
jurisdictions.density_rules: New STRs must be at least 500 feet from any existing licensed STR
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Only property owners or long-term lessees residing at the property as primary residence may apply; proof of residency required. Licenses are not transferable.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Unknown
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown
jurisdictions.additional_context: Unknown

jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Unknown
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unknown
jurisdictions.density_rules: 60-permit cap per each of 10 council districts; over-cap districts (e.g., District 8: 161 permits, District 10: 202 permits) closed to new applicants
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Dedicated STR compliance officer actively citing unlicensed properties as of June 18, 2026; unlicensed operators avoid city fees, inspections, and accommodation taxes. Properties in over-cap districts must wait for permit counts to drop before applying.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Unknown
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown
jurisdictions.additional_context: Unknown

jurisdictions.license_required: No — local STR-specific permit/license/registration/fee requirements prohibited from July 1, 2026
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Unknown
jurisdictions.primary_residence_required: No — owner-occupancy mandates prohibited statewide from July 1, 2026
jurisdictions.rental_day_cap: None — local rental day caps prohibited statewide from July 1, 2026
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: STRs reclassified as 'nontransient residential use'; cannot be singled out for additional restrictions where residential use is permitted
jurisdictions.density_rules: Local density/proximity caps prohibited statewide from July 1, 2026
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: Local governments may still require uniform health/safety measures (smoke alarms, CO detectors, IBC occupancy limits) and collect lodging taxes, provided requirements apply equally to all residential properties.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Unknown
jurisdictions.penalties: STR-specific penalties/fines/permit revocations tied to rules conflicting with HB 583 no longer permissible after July 1, 2026
jurisdictions.additional_context: Unknown

jurisdictions.license_required: Cities may still impose safety standards and non-cap regulations; outright license caps are banned
jurisdictions.tax_registration_required: Unknown
jurisdictions.tax_rate: Unknown
jurisdictions.zoning_restrictions: Unknown
jurisdictions.density_rules: Local STR density/license caps prohibited statewide from July 1, 2026; municipalities with existing caps have until January 2028 to comply
jurisdictions.platform_obligations: Unknown
jurisdictions.compliance_notes: HOAs retain ability to restrict rentals, but under HEA 1210 only homestead (owner-occupied) residents may vote on HOA rental prohibition matters.
jurisdictions.key_notes: Unknown
jurisdictions.penalties: Unknown
jurisdictions.additional_context: Unknown
jurisdictions.notes: HEA 1210 signed by Governor Braun on March 12, 2026. Preempts local STR license caps statewide; existing caps must phase out by Jan 1, 2028. HOA homestead-only voting rule effective July 1, 2026.

timeline.summary: On June 30, 2026 (tomorrow), the City of Nevada City is hosting a public workshop to review a draft STR ordinance intended for the November 2026 ballot; the proposed framework would repeal and replace the existing voter-approved STR rules (which cannot be changed directly by the City Council) with a new regulatory system developed over years of community input since 2024. Key provisions and final ballot language are still under public review.
timeline.old_value: Existing voter-approved STR ordinance in force; City Council cannot amend directly
timeline.new_value: Proposed new STR ordinance being reviewed at June 30, 2026 public workshop; if approved by Council, would go to voters on November 3, 2026 ballot to repeal and replace current rules
timeline.effective_date: 2026-11-03 (ballot measure, if placed; voter approval required)

timeline.summary: Indiana HEA 1210—signed March 12, 2026, passed 91-3 in the House and 48-0 in the Senate—takes effect July 1, 2026, explicitly prohibiting local governments from capping the number of privately owned residential properties used as STRs. Municipalities like Carmel and Fishers that previously capped STR density (e.g., no more than 10% of homes per subdivision) have a delayed compliance window until January 2028.
timeline.old_value: Local governments could cap STR density/numbers per subdivision or neighborhood (e.g., Carmel and Fishers enforced 10% subdivision caps)
